In-class TAs do not grade and cannot provide clear feedback or help, and the grading criteria is unclear. Students submit identical work and receive different scores. Assignments due @ the end of class. A waste of time.

analysis.name: Ernesto Quinonez
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ernesto was the most laid back professor I've ever had and this was probably the most chill class I've ever taken. Also the second half of class you watch movies once a week and discuss the other day, final project is also a 5 min video on any topic you want. Goated class.
Friendly, but provides no useful feedback and no help improving work. Doesn't pay much attention. Easy grader but you learn nothing.

The fact that this is my first time giving a review here is testament to Ernesto's teaching ability. He's sharp, open-minded, funny, experimental, inspirational, and doesn't BS. He won't sugar-coat, but he will make you a better, more aware writer. And if he pulls you aside to encourage you to send your work out... you know you've made it :)

Ernesto was my professor for True Stories (FWS). He is a great and will really work with students. He assigns few papers and gives good feedback. The class is about radical political movements in the US, and we sometimes watched movies and did light readings (less than 5 pgs). Would 10/10 recommend this class to anyone!!

Super laid-back. Classes with him are really relaxed. He points out great writing advice and tips I never knew about. He's just not too into fantasy writing and head's up: not good with remembering names. I've taken two classes with him.

analysis.name: Cheryl Qian
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Qian worked with me on my doctoral thesis and is a lifesaver in every definition of the word. She was the main advisor for our research team and saved us so much time and energy on our projects. She helped us get our findings published, present at conferences, and advocated for us at every step of the way. Can't say enough about her.

Dr. Qian was definitely challenging, but she's incredibly supportive and always willing to talk through ideas or give thoughtful feedback.

By the end of the semester, I felt like I had leveled up as a designer and as a thinker. If you're ready to put in the work, she will absolutely meet you there and help you get the most out of the process.

Dr. Qian is one of the most intellectually stimulating professors I've worked with at Cornell. She pushes us to question assumptions and find design solutions that are both elegant and ethical. She also genuinely cares about her students' growth. 

She gives tough feedback, but it's always constructive and deeply insightful.
She's the best professor I've ever had.
Probably the best professor at Cornell. She's a great mentor, accessible, and willing to work with you on every level. Taking her class redefined my entire life and career.

analysis.name: Quincy Loney
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Quincy has to be the worst professor I've ever had. He would often get confused by his own questions, struggle to explain basic concepts, and nitpick every HW calculation to his exact standards. Be prepared to teach yourself. He is a horrible professor, and a rude person who tries to make you feel dumb instead of helping you during class.

Loney is honest & funny, but MATH-1100's flipped format relies on a TERRIBLE textbook that sets students up for failure IMO. 

The real problem is grading--which is shared with Prof. Kaminski whos unbelievably harsh, inconsistent, & nitpicky. So be aware, if you take Prof. Loney in the summer, you're stuck with her grading too.

Professor Loney (wishes to be called Quincy) was an extremely animated professor. His course was super difficult for me, but most students seem to do just fine. A majority of the course was all about the textbook so the textbook is a MUST! He's super excusable outside of class and only wants to help the students succeed.

analysis.name: Qiuyun Teng
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: She's very helpful and reasonable, but it is a tough class with many pop quizzes. I had to work hard, but I am now able to spend hours with my Chinese friends without speaking any English, which ultimately is the only true measure of a language teacher and course.

Took both Chin 2201 and 2202 with her, she can be really funny sometimes and make the class interesting with some fun dialogue. However, she sometimes carries the crazy high maintenance mother persona which scares me. Besides all that, shes a really great Chinese professor who will teach Chinese in a very painless way and make you laugh.

Very personally invested in teaching and improving every student's Chinese skills, though that sometimes means being harsh with her students and assigning large amounts of memorization-heavy homework. She takes attendance and diligent completion of homework seriously. Her grading practices are predictable; you directly get out what you put in.

I took IMPAC over the summer with Teng Laoshi and liked her. She has her moments of snappiness and strictness but usually she's really nice and patient. She also teaches you a lot of useful vocabulary that isn't in the textbook, which is somewhat obscure. Her tests are manageable and her expectations are reasonable. Teng helps move students along.

Pretty strict but wants you to improve. Skilled at teaching the material and getting conversations going. In class exercises are repetitive but improved pronunciation a ton. Sizable daily HW + daily writing quizzes. When you make a mistake, she is strict with you, making you repeat until you get it correct, which can be stressful (Whiplash vibes).

analysis.name: Daniel Quan
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment:
Don't know why Quan has so many haters. You can literality fup the Prelim (miss almost half of his 25 MCQ exam) and still get an A. He is THE BEST professor in hotel school. Years of experience in  Real Estate. 

YOU need to forget about what your $10000 career coach tells you. Just go to class and learn!!!

One of the worst classes I've taken at Cornell, 100% wouldn't recommend. Posts no grades, exam averages are god awful, and curve isn't published until grades are finalized on transcript. He reads off of word documents during lectures and makes them unbearable. Called guest lecturer by the wrong name and fell asleep during another guest. No good.

tests are extremely tricky... lectures and slides do not prepare you well enough for them. all MCQs, no partial credit. answer choices include a lot of none of the above, not enough information, or all of the above. easy for him to grade but hard for us to take as test-takers.

His grading can be a bit obscure with one M/C question being worth 15% of an exam and his case studies are a headache but overall good professor. Not impossible getting a good grade. He'll give you an A if you do better than everyone else as its subjective at the end of the semester. (grad students tend to be bricks and bring grade average down)

Professor Quan tricks you the first day. He makes fun of his grades on rate my professor which makes you think that they are fake however they are real and he is one of the worst professors at Cornell. Do everything you can to avoid him.

analysis.name: Shu-Bing Qian
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Avoid if possible. He is not easy to understand and his lectures are so lackluster.  Even though I found the material interesting, I only understood the slides by studying them on my own... basically, Khan Academy was my best friend taking this class.  He is terrible at answering questions.  His exams are manageable, but nonetheless difficult.

Took NS 3200 in Fall 14.  Did not like this class and would not recommend. Professor Qian taught 34 of the class and he was not very clear due to his accent.  Also, by cramming it all into one semester, you do not go very much in depth. Fell more like a Cell bio than biochemistry. Easier than 3310 but I would recommend  3310-3320 to learn the most

Obviously biochem is going to be hard, no matter who teaches it. I thought Prof. Qian did a good job of presenting the material in as accessible of a way as possible. He was also really kind and somehow kind of funny. I appreciated him even more compared to next professor who switched in halfway through the semester.

He is a demanding professor and covers a lot of very important content. That being said he does move fast and it is hard to keep up at certain times in the semester. I spent about 10-15 hours a week fully digesting the material and it was a course I will remember because it reminded me why I love biology. Would take it again in an instant.

Professor Qian is one of the worst professors I've had. Hard to understand lectures. Prelims are ridiculous true/false questions that don't test actual understanding of material. Very rude! Makes fun of questions in lecture. Doesn't accommodate students needs. But! It's easier than the other biochem classes and is more pre-med/vet focused.

analysis.name: Robbert Van Renesse
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Genuinely the kindest professor I've ever had, you won't regret taking a class with him.

RVR is one of my favorite professors I have had at Cornell. Knowing that you're being taught OS by someone who worked at Bell Labs with the creators of Unix and C is very compelling and inspirational. He is hilarious and his office hours are immensely helpful, more so than TAs. He is also very active on Ed. His demos in class are also insightful.

RVR's weekly assignments were sized appropriately and reinforced the lecture materials, reasonable workload. He was helpful after lectures in answering questions, and walked through difficult assignment questions. I wasn't a fan of his exams and this was my downfall - it required a deeper understanding of concepts than what is provided in lecture.
Although he is kind, its clear he did not spend much time preparing for lecture. This resulted in a very disorganized course.
the whole course is a mess and won't teach you much stuff. The professors and TAs are unhelpful. You'd rather self-study instead of taking this class

analysis.name: Reyer Sjamaar
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 43 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: mediocre lecturer. just regurgitates the textbook, moves really quickly. grading only depends on the tests, which really depends on which semester u take it. homework takes a while and our prelims were ridiculously hard.

Professor Sjamaar is a great professor; his analogies are funny and useful, and he has a pleasant Dutch accent.

His favorite word is orthogonal, and his office hours are always popular. I would recommend him, he's a memorable lecturer for sure!

I suppose 1920 can be difficult, but he made it enjoyable; it's always help to Read as well :)
Great math class if you want to truly understand linear algebra and multivariable calculus. Expect a LOT of work, but no busywork. For comparison, it is significantly harder than CS 2112.

Excellent lecture notes (probably the best lecture organizer), accessible outside of classroom. He writes very hard exam problems but curve is very generous (Median A- in 2240). Course itself is worth taking.

This class was pretty tough for me personally. I found lectures difficult and unclear and the textbook was not great at explaining certain topics. Quizzes during discussions were alright since they closely followed the homework, however, tests extremely difficult and unfair. Also ed-discussion responses were a bit mean sometimes.

analysis.name: Robert Kleinberg
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor R. Kleinberg was excellent for Algorithms, Spring 2014. He is very open to student questions and attempts to make a sometimes-challenging subject clear. Not an easy professor by any means, but assessments fair. Relies on the textbook a good amount, but also references outside research.
His lecturing style as well as his mastery of the subject matter are both top-notch. He also seems like a great person. It's an absolute privilege to have him as a lecturer.

Really good class but super challenging, def got a lot out of the class. In class quizzes were fairly easy as long as you paid attention in class, and the final was fair. Would recommend starting HWs later as he often makes edits because he realized problems are way too hard.
Super engaging lectures, explains complex topics well. Makes proofs a conversation and motivates each step

Bobby is, no lie, a genius and is well-respected. Lectures can be slow, but are very intuitive. #3 on problem sets can be very difficult sometimes. Exams are pretty easy. Advice: start psets the day they get released and rEAD the TEXTBOOK!

analysis.name: Matt Ryan
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Ryan is a great instructor, genuinely cares about his students and is always open to questions. Labs were the highlight of the class.

One of the easiest classes at Cornell if you just show up. Minimal studying is needed if you go to lectures, and pay close attention during labs. Field Trips are engaging and tie back into exams. Lots of small projects, reading, and quizzes throughout the semester so just keep up with all the work. Watch the syllabus and you'll be fine.

The class is pretty easy, lots of different assignment styles, which break up the rigidness unlike other Cornell Science classes. Each lecture is pretty boring, and Prof Ryan is not the most well spoken. There are farm field trips instead of traditional labs that are fun and inspiring. Midterm and Final were both straight forward.

Professor Ryan is a great guy and is very accessible. The TA's were very nice and helpful as well. The class is easy as long as you turn things in on time. The only downside was the 3 hour labs outside on farms during freezing rain or snow.

analysis.name: Peter Wolczanski
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 143 reviews, this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: One thought comes to mind when I think of PJ: What is life? This man simultaneously does not care about his class while still enjoying his class more than anyone else in the lecture. Although his lectures are truly incomprehensible, I think we should give him a break -- he is only super ten year. I can only hope to be as cool as him one day.
I see him at parties, what a guy. Not a guy during lectures :
He takes pleasure in his students failing. He relishes in the feeling of superiority and enjoys picking on his students' struggle in the class.
8th grade math yo, actually though just CASE out
he's impossible to understand and lectures about everything we don't need to know but leaves out everything we do. Sometimes it's actually funny how confusing he can be

analysis.name: Ryan Tapping
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 41 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Super sweet and enthusiastic guy. You can genuinely tell teaching is a passion, and he does it well! Always receptive to feedback and willing to help everyone. Class isn't as easy as everyones making it out to be though, its still pretty difficulty, ESPECIALLY when magnetism is introduced. So participate in ur COOPs and stay on top of it :-)

Ryan Tapping is by far my favorite professor at Cornell. He cares so much about all of his students, and is truly enthusiastic about what he teaches. It is easy to do well in his class, because 45% of your grade is participation/homework. The exams are very doable. I hope Cornell recognises professor Tapping for his exceptional teaching.

Dr. Tapping is an amazing professor. He is truly one of a kind. I have so much respect for him. He made phys2213 enjoyable even though it was a hard class. His office hours were very helpful, and he is very patient with students. Thank you for everything Dr. Tapping and to all students taking his class in the future, please be nice to him.

He is a really nice guy and puts in the effort to make a nice learning environment. He seems to really care about his students and goes out of his way to try to make his lectures more enjoyable. The work is not light though its a physics course so you do get a bunch of homework and everything else that comes with a physics course.

Tapping is the kindest and fairest professor you can get at Cornell. Lectures are easy to follow along, and he makes every class enjoyable. You will LOVE this class. It's not too hard to get a B in the class, but DO NOT slack off.

analysis.name: Sharon Sassler
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 55 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Oh my god this was the most painful lecture I've sat through in my three years of college. The exercises are graded unreasonably harshly and the class is graded just by exercises and the two exams. Sassler makes awkward jokes and laughs at herself all the time and it's just irritating. Unless you have to take this class for PAM, avoid it.
You can spend entire weeks on an assignment with extensive office hours help, and think you completely perfected it, only to receive a poor grade with little/confusing feedback.

Widely held as the worst professor in the PAM department. Class is dreadful. She totally picks favorites, which affects your grade. Her name tells part of the story because she is quite "sassy," as in rude. The grading of her assignments makes no sense, yet the assignments will be graded harshly. Good luck figuring out how not to lose points.

If 20+ people show up to a lecture every day just to click in and then leave immediately, something is wrong. Sassler is the worst prof I've had in the PAM department, and if this class had been my first exposure to the major I probably would have switched out of it. Painful, useless class that was torture to sit through twice a week.
It seems like rate my professor is being flooded because Sassler gave us her horribly graded assignments back. This class made me drop from PAM.

analysis.name: Tracy Mitrano
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: This might be the worst class I've taken at Cornell. The lectures are nonsensical, unrelated, and redundant. She rarely engages with the course material and instead veers off on tangents throughout most lectures. She has little to no knowledge about AI, so anything she teaches about it are more speculative than grounded in reality

The class is very easy, but you won't learn anything.  Professor typically rambles on about random political topics, and she doesn't really understand technology.  I don't know how she got this teaching position.  Attendance is graded but it's not worth paying attention.

She means well, but her lectures are disorganized and poorly planned, with scribbles on the whiteboard and no clear connection between topics. She often goes on tangents about her personal beliefs that don't add much value to the course. Additionally, the assignment instructions are unclear and inconsistent, making it hard to know what to expect.

I really loved Professor Mitrano. Now I will acknowledge she is not everyones cup of tea. If you prefer very structured classes with strict schedules/ assignments, don't take this class. However, I thought that this class was helpful in addressing the societal impact of AI. Mitrano is so passionate and willing to meet students where they are.

Gives out grades without justifications. Let us debate all the time so she can do nothing and just be lazy. Talks randomly and writes down random things on board with no organization. Biased toward immigrants and minorities. Super mean and subjective. Save your time please, don't take her class.

analysis.name: Toby Ault
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I took Dr. Ault's EAS 1330 and 1310 courses. Dr. Ault explains the material in a way that's easy to understand for both majors and non-majors. He offers plenty of extra credit and is overall a great professor. Easy A, definitely take him for distributions. He was also recently nominated for the CALS Chancellor's Award for Excellence in Teaching!

This is a guy you would WANT to find outside lecture. He cares about his students' experiences just as much as the lecture material. Although he cracks jokes and likes to make students laugh a lot, his anecdotes and rambles tie into class material, creating cohesive stories that teach the material at a competent and memorable level.

This review is actually for EAS 1300. He's one of the greats at Cornell. He wants all of his students to succeed, only ones that wouldn't are who to refuse to show to class. Take a class of his not only for a requirement, but if you need to reach 120 college credits. Lectures were awesome. Not much homework either. He is the GOAT

AWESOME PROF. Prof Ault understands most people are taking this class for distribution and doesn't try to make the class too hard. Genuinely, he just wants to make people understand the threat of climate change and need for action. Rewards people who come to class with extra credit opportunities. Tries to make lectures engaging and class not hard.

For an easy A class that fufilled two of the annoying Arts & Science distributions at once, Professor Ault understood the assignment. He knew most people didn't care about it and was taking the class because they had to, so he made the lectures interesting within that context. He was fun and gave tech breaks, absolute G!

analysis.name: Tom Gilovich
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 76 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: My favorite class at Cornell, definitely take it. So interesting and applicable to daily life. Gilovich is a rock star in the psych world and he knows all the material by heart + wrote the textbook. Both prelims and the final are horribly specific. Read the textbook word for word. Attendance not required but will definitely help you on the test

PSYCH 2800 was a really interesting course. Lecture is definitely super important - he would include niche comments from lecture on tests. Top 3 class I've taken at Cornell. If you are interested in the class content, the grades will follow. Would not recommend taking it as a non-psych major for fun/distribution, unless S/U. His outfits also eat!

One of the best psych classes I've taken at Cornell. Attendance not mandatory but his lectures are always super interesting and I agree with others that say he has the best fits. Tests did include a lot of specific facts from readings/lecture, but nothing too unmanageable.

Would not take this class unless it's required - big mistake as someone who's not a psych major and just thought the subject matter would be interesting. He's a good lecturer but the grade is 80% based on tests, so makes it very hard to do well if you're not a strong test taker.

Not an easy class, and I wouldn't recommend taking it just for fun. The material is interesting, especially if you have an interest in the social sciences, but the class structure is a bit strict and most of your grade is from exams that are quite difficult. I've taken several Psych/HD courses, and this was definitely one of the hardest ones.

analysis.name: Ulrich Wiesner
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Funny dude - I really enjoyed his lectures. The course does not cover much content so discussions were constant review, didn't have to spend much time outside of class (please still study though). Exams were straightforward. You are set up to succeed in the class - wanted to put this review so students are deterred from taking his class, he's good.

He is one of the worst professors I have ever had at Cornell.  The guy does not care about his students, never answers emails, barely gives any homework (which makes it impossible to study) and is so boring in lecture.  I think he really doesn't care if you do well or not and makes his tests really hard.  He also has awful handwriting.

Great lecturer excited about the material that tries to make sure that all students learn in his class. It is an interesting and enjoyable class if you stay engaged, as he also definitely cares about participation. Class doesn't have too much homework but requires paper reading. I and other friends all had positive experiences

Very crappy professor. No website, not enough homeworks given, poor handwriting, and no assistance before examinations. I honestly think that he is one of the most lazy and unmotivated professors who simply does not care whether or not you learn anything. I learnt nothing and he sucks.
The worst professor ever in my life.

analysis.name: Madeleine Udell
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Rarely am I compelled to post a review on Rate My Professor but this class was so horrible and utter garbage that I must insist. The class is hard for no reason at the expensive of learning, I can probably find more help from Scooby Doo than the Professor and TA team. There is no transparency in grading. Don't take any of her classes stay far away.

Udell's explanations were so unclear to the point that I had to google everything she said - the Internet did a much better job than her in teaching. Grading was not transparent - hw instructions and grading rubric were very vague. She made us do peer grading - this coupled with non-transparent grading incentivized students to sabotage their peers.

I find it difficult to believe that Cornell University would include this professor in its team of excellent professors. You can just skip class because the lectures are worthless. Support from TAs and Professors are also non-existent. The group projects are graded with unsupervised peer review, incentivizing people to sabotage others. Don't take.

The project has no rubric no matter how much you ask. Then you get a random grade a few peers come up with. Your project can be better than others but you will get a worse grade. learn very little in this class. Avoid this class please. Its just a shame there are not other good ML classes available. it forces people to take this class.

Probably one of the worst classes I've taken at Cornell. Udell is a confusing lecturer, and the TAs for the class are very focused on not giving you the answers to problem sets, even if it comes at the expense of a student's understanding. Very unclear and somewhat arbitrary grading rubrics. Avoid at all costs.

analysis.name: Christopher "Kit" Umbach
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: exactly what others talk about. He expects too much prior knowledge about chem and physics even if its engri class and the class is really boring and long.

In MSE, you will probably have to take a decent amount of classes with Umbach. He is pretty not well-received as he doesn't teach well and tends to give grades arbitrarily based on personal feeling. You probably won't learn much in his classes and will def feel frustrated, but if you BS effort and try to seem nice, you'll get a better grade...

Umbach, although tough, is an entertaining lecturer and a kind professor. Arguably, Nanotechnology is one of the hardest ENGRI classes, and as such, many people are disappointed with workload. However, the class was inspirational, and it completely changed my personal career path; as such, I highly recommend the class for motivated individuals

Decent professor. His lecture notes are extremely dense, but he posts all the power points which are very convenient for reviewing for prelims. For the most part the tests are fair. The homeworks didn't seem particularly useful but they weren't very hard. The material is often interesting, other times not so much. Overall a decent experience
horrible teacher. expects you to know too much. ridiculous tests and unfair grading policies. do not take nanotech!

analysis.name: Audrey Ukhov
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: GREAT Professor. had him for advanced corporate finance. new guy, but dont be scared, he knows what he is talking about and has a genuine interest in the students. Very helpful at office hours, and makes advanced corporate finance interesting. Oh, his random facts about the finance world are the great. All hail Buffet!
Legit professor of finance. hotel school sucks less with real finance professors like ukhov
Great professor who cares about his students, especially  if you go to class and get to know him. Loves athletes. His lectures tend to be long and a bit boring but great professor overall.

My favorite professor at Cornell thus far. Extremely smart and knowledgable, and also willing to help his students in office hours. Go to office hours and get to know him-- he's a great guy with funny stories, and it will help you in the class. If you show up and pay attention, no reason you can't do well.

This guy is hilarious. Has a heavy Russian accent, which makes his stories even funnier. Cares that you learn the material. Exams are much harder than the practice problems and homework he gives. But all in all, one of the best professors I've had simply for the pure entertainment value of his stories.

analysis.name: Stephanie Thomas
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 171 reviews, this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Thomas is an amazing teacher. For those who may have not understood econ prior, she is able to break it down in a way that actually makes sense. She assigns just the right workload -- not too overwhelming, but not a walk in the park. She is also able to make the topic interesting, which is a huge plus because Labor Econ can easily be boring.

Disorganized + careless. Professor mishandled the final: some students seemed to start 30–40 min early, discussed questions, took photos, and bragged about ChatGPT with no enforcement. Exam was nothing like practice/MindTap. Lectures felt rushed, questions dismissed, expectations unclear all semester. Grading felt arbitrary too. Avoid at all costs.

Her wait time for grades is absolutely ridiculous sometimes. Plus, her tests are also very irrelevant to what we're actually taught in the course so it's very easy to wait a long time for a frustratingly bad grade. Overall though, she's not a bad teacher.

God awful professor who does not care about her students. She purposely sets her students up to do terrible. Doesn't give enough time on exams and her exams are "open note" but you have no time to check the notes adequately.

Her lecture quality and teaching is fine, but she needs a lot of work on the grading aspect. People learn econ pretty well, yet only 3 exams are given and they're mostly irrelevant so most people do bad, thus their grades inaccurately represents their knowledge. Plus, she has the longest wait time for grades I've ever experienced for a professor.

analysis.name: Uri Possen
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I had the privilege of taking three classes with the late Professor Uri Possen, including an independent study. He was one of my best and favorite professors at Cornell. He taught financial economics, a subject I loved. He helped me sign up for an internship, because of which I got the visa papers. Always there for his students. I miss you. RIP!

He doesn't explain the models in detail during the class, rather you should study on the textbook. However, he and his TA like to help students preparing the exams. He provides good insights on the current macroeconomic situation especially on fiscal policy and financial economics . Generous grader.

Good Professor. Very interesting course. Go to all lectures and take good notes; cross-reference with the book and go to office hours for whatever you don't get, he'll appreciate you all the more. Start the paper assignment EARLY!!

I had literally already taken finance when I took this class, so it was kind of a joke. But he's not a bad guy. Strangely though, I got a higher grade in the Johnson school finance class I took previously than I did this one. Odd.
His lecture is a bit tedious, but he is personally nice guy and always willing to help students. When you rely on textbook, which is the best i have ever seen, you can ace this course

analysis.name: Virginia Doellgast
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I'm not completely sure how this professor is at Cornell. I've ever met a less engaging and more annoying person in my life. I mean truly the worst experience of my academic career. I look at the clock and it doesn't move in this class. I've learned nothing. Made a course that should be interesting so incredibly boring.

ICL is an easy class, no idea why the grade is this low. Her section is about Europe (particularly France/Germany), which is probably the section you'll go into with the most understanding. questions 1-5 on the final are worth 10% each. DO THE LAST QUESTION FIRST its worth 50% and takes a lotta time. Lectures were interesting readings were helpful
The least engaging course I've ever sat through its a complete an utter waste of time and money. They cannot lecture whatsoever. My friend is sleeping as I write this

Writing this before I get my grade back on the first exam. She assigns ~50 pgs of reading/week and isn't the best lecturer. IMO she could improve on linking examples in class to key concepts needed for exams. (this is coming from someone with a >4.0 GPA). This class would also REALLY benefit from having a discussion section (idk how it doesn't)

The worst lecturer of the four professors in this class. Painfully boring, disengaging lectures, and a droning voice. I'm unsure how she became a professor because she has zero public speaking skills. The content is all over the place and doesn't follow a logical sequence. I managed to get an "A" by cramming the study guide.

analysis.name: David Usher
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 34 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: His exams sometimes tested things that were specific examples during lecture. There are 3 prelims, 1 final, and no homework. This saves you time, but also gives you less of a grade buffer. I hate orgo, and even though he was entertaining with singing and stories, I couldn't wait for the semester to end. His digressions got annoying after a while.

He's a great professor in one sense - he's very kind and funny and overall a really nice person.  Open to questions etc.  He sings in class and shows us amusing videos  But I don't feel like I learn chemistry from his lectures.  he just demonstrates calculations.  As with Zax, I feel like I learn the most from the supplemental prof Dr. Johnson.

105 was kinda hard.  Only because his freestyle lectures were not supplemented with a textbook, so exams were pretty tricky.  He doesn't TEACH you how to do the problems in class, just shows you what you should know, but with no textbook this is hard to deal with, though I gotta love his singing, family slide shows and random episodes of Buffy.

Love, love, love! Usher is a great professor and an outstanding opera singer. As long as you understand the lectures, textbook chapters, and how lab demonstrates what you're learning in class, you should be fine! Do plenty of practice problems, read the textbook, and go through lecture notes! Not a bad class if you know your stuff...

Amazing Chem teacher who makes use of class time to make sure everyone understands the concepts by going over examples and demonstrations in detail.  He makes class fun and enjoyable with his personal anecdotes and songs.  Overall, great teacher and is a class everyone should take before graduating Cornell.

analysis.name: Lyrae Van Clief-Stefanon
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Lyrae is a walking inspiration. I took Advanced Poetry with her, and I can honestly say that she completely changed my perspective on writing, and transformed me from a dabbler to a poet. She has given me confidence. She is also sassy and hilarious. Take advantage of this opportunity to take a class with such an amazing human being.

Lyrae is art incarnate. She's inspirational and brings out the artist in everyone. If you don't have semi-thick skin expect some exfoliation, especially if you have little exposure to creative writing. But if you stick through it I promise she'll make you a better writer and a better human. I love her  and you'll love her. Go for it.

The grade for this class is primarily based on attendance. Each class, you have to submit printed copies of either three poems or three pages of prose. There is no feedback on these submissions unless you bring them to workshop classes. Prof. was often very serious during class, which made her feel unapproachable and discouraged participation.

Lyrae is one of the most incredible teachers, and inspiring people I have ever met. I came into her class hating poetry and left after every class understanding something knew about poetry, literature, and life. She cares about her students, engages the class and facilitates great discussions. Take her class. She's amazing.

S:106. Avoid if possible. More than half of the initial class dropped w/in the first week. Harsh and biased grader. Quite mean when things don't go her way. Has stated that she does not like the KIfW. Assigns "group essays". Course books are perverse &/or boring. Deceptively changed the course title. Does not use internet. Great poet, bad teacher.

analysis.name: Vivian Zayas
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 60 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: There is a ton of reading, the exams are very hard. I find this class harder than organic chemistry, which is the worst class I've ever taken. The means are low, and there is no curve. Professor Zayas is a very good lecturer, though. Most of the information from lectures is not on the slides, so it is important to go to lecture.

Zayas is a really great lecturer. I found class very engaging and interesting. However, there is a LOT of content to cover in this class. Lecture can move very fast and cover a wide breadth of content. Prelims also cover a ton of content and can be hard if not prepared. Extra credit is nice though.

The slides genuinely do not help you when you are reviewing and you have to come to every class to understand. She doesn't teach you all the content you need to know. The multiple-choice questions are sometimes confusing. The content is pretty interesting though.

Professor Zayas is a great lecturer and made class engaging! The key concepts she provides for each unit were super helpful for studying for exams. The grading system is clear and unbiased, and I found attending lecture to be incredibly useful.

This professor knows her stuff!  And though she may not be the easiest or clearest lecturer, she is able to clear up any confusion if you go to her after class or during office hours.  She is a new prof, so she is still getting the hang of teaching.  Interesting material, difficult tests.  But she practically hands you points (i-Clicker, essays).

analysis.name: Kora Battig Von Wittelsbach
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 99 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professoressa is the greatest teacher ever. She is the type of teacher who is there to grow your mind and help you think, and is not just to show off how brilliant she is (even though she is ridiculously brilliant). Definitely take her classes. Her lectures are interesting, inspiring, and engaging. She is also incredibly sweet and an great dresser.

Brilliant professor. The way she incorporated her knowledge of Mediterranean literature, history, culture and her personal experiences of the Mediterranean made every seminar extremely interesting! She is very sweet and approachable, cares a lot about her students and is very passionate about what she is teaching. I truly learned so much from her.

Writing assignments were primarily completed in class, which means the only real homework was reading! This was my favorite class ever. We read engaging books, watched an awe-inspiring documentary, and had our emotions wrenched by two spectacular films. I loved this class because I feel more knowledgeable and more intellectual for having taken it.

Professoressa KVW was my favorite professor I have had at Cornell so far. She is so elegant, intelligent, caring, and has an incredible sense of humor. There was a lot of daily homework that was went over each class (she cold calls), but it is not too hard to complete and is helpful. She genuinely cares a lot about her students and the class a lot.

Professoressa is such a delight to learn from. Having had her for 2 semesters now, I can confidently say that she is one of the most understanding professors here at Cornell! If you are going to learn Italian I HIGHLY, HIGHLY, HIGHLY recommend it be with her. You will thank yourself for picking one of the best Cornell professors around!

analysis.name: Kim Weeden
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 35 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Class is pretty heavily memorization-based, so get ready to make flashcards and mnemonic devices. But overall probably the most interesting course I've taken at Cornell with extremely engaging lectures and funny professor. Exams can be a bit tough but honestly if you study hard for the 3 total exams in the semester you'll be perfectly fine, no HW.

Professor Weeden is best described as "respectable." She knows her stuff, and she's also good with dry humor. However, don't expect this class to be easy. It's an information dump of a bunch of studies (making big concepts a little harder to grasp), the results of which you need to memorize for tests. Great sociologist, but not the best professor.

Professor Weeden is a knowledgeable and engaging lecturer, but this class is far from easy. The material is dense and heavily grounded in specific authors and studies, so you need to know not only the concepts but also who developed them. This is not an easy A and takes lots of studying!!

Professor Weeden is incredible. She is engaging, thorough, funny, as nice as can be. I HIGHLY recommend taking this class, especially if it fulfills a requirement. The material taught is tremendously important and relevant, and this course will make you a more informed citizen. See previous ratings for the grading policy; it's more than fair.
My queeeeen Kim! She cared about the material and made sure to organize the content in an accessible way. Easy to do well in the class and interesting.

analysis.name: Cora Whitmore
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Cora is an excellent professor, and I surprisingly found her Developmental bio the more interesting half of the course. Lectures are similarly engaging yet even more fast-paced, so make sure you're a fast writer. Grading is extremely fair - TAs are wonderful and genuinely care about students. BIOMG1350 is my favorite class I've taken this semester.

Cora was very kind and helpful, especially during office hours. She explains difficult concepts clearly and is patient with questions. Exams were very fair, everything came straight from the lectures, with a lot of memorization. If you attend class and review the slides, you'll do well. Don't be afraid to ask her questions.

Cora is very nice and accessible. 1350 is a grind but the prelims were very easy (all MCQ + during class time) and you are able to drop up to two of them. Homework is easy and course is run well– plenty of resources to do well, including practice prelims and other practice questions. Just memorize the slides and you're guaranteed an A

Prof. Whitmore is super nice and always willing to chat during OH! Lectures are clear and the slides mostly contain everything she covers, so nice to not have to sit through the recording to catch up. Always engages with students and exams are very straightforward MCQs during class time. 1350 is a great and easy class!

Cora is such a sweet professor who seems genuinely passionate about biology. Her lectures are clear and more engaging than the molecular biology section. Yes, there's a lot of memorization in this class, so be prepared to spend time processing the content and memorizing it. But it's definitely doable to get an A.

analysis.name: William Miller
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The goat. Cares so much about students and is a great teacher with fun stories. Super flexible with deadlines, and assignments are very reasonable. Interesting guest lectures and labs that cover a good variety of plant science related fields.

Miller is a kind professor. While his class required effort it was not difficult. Would recommend studying for tests, and also try to talk to him outside of class. He has a lot of interesting experience, will be sad to see him go.
This guy is the man. He is hip with a capital H

I loved PLSCI1101 so much. Dr. Miller made it fun and is a hilarious guy. Reading is somewhat heavy in the beginning of the semester, late semester is mostly guest lectures. He is very flexible with due dates and lets you submit late assignments. Gets back to email fast. By far my favorite class this semester!

Professor Miller was kind and helpful. He took the time to meet with each student individually and gauge how we were feeling about the class. Not a lot of work and lectures + lab were very interesting especially if you don't know exactly what you want to do with a plant science degree yet.

analysis.name: Kristina Workman
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: If you show her that you're motivated to learn the material, she will be very caring and helpful in learning the material and receiving a good grade, she does not like slackers though. Tests were lecture-heavy and projects had clear rubrics. Very fair grader. Put in the work and you will get a good grade. Participate in lectures for extra credit.

Workman's lectures can sometimes be dreadful because feel very long, content is dry, and honestly her voice is a little annoying (sorry queen). BUT, she is fair and pretty nice. Class is soooo memorization and lots common sense (name of the concept often tells what it is). This class taught me to use flashcards.. great for this type of class.

Never taught during the entire semester, only read off slides. Very uncommunicative about assignments, readings, exams, etc. Expects you to know everything and to do everything yourself. Always had an attitude when I asked questions or had concerns. Wouldn't take this class again if my life depended on it. Teaching style was rude and dismissive.

This is my least favorite class I've taken at Cornell. The content is not engaging, and the tests are overly complicated. The professor isn't communicative and is not a great lecturer. I dreaded this class. No matter how hard I studied, I would do mediocre.

I did not enjoy her class. Participation matters a lot, and she talks a lot during lectures, so there is a lot to remember for the prelim 😒. I also disliked the group project. Working with six people made communication hard. Some group members ghost you, then act like kind, smart people in class. It felt really two-faced.

analysis.name: Xi Yang
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Yang is by far one of the best business professors at Cornell. She was always accessible by email or office hours, constantly checked in with students to ensure they understood the material, and was always willing to meet outside of class for extra help if needed. Though the material in Finance was challenging, she was always there to help

I love her from the moon and back. She's amazing. You can tell she cares about her students. She tries to remember everyone's name on the first day of class‼️Her OH are 3 to 4 pm, but I have class so I often go around 4:15, and she's always eager to help. The only reason I got a B+ was cuz I messed up prelim 2 and 2 hw assignments 😔

Genuinely the best professor I've ever had. Her lectures can be a bit boring at times, but she's an amazing teacher. She's clear, her slides are neat, her canvas is well organized. If you go to office hours, she basically do the HW for you. 3 exams, all online and open everything except AI. Two easy group projects. Great TAs, TAKE THIS CLASS!

My favorite professor at Cornell by far. You can tell she loves her job and she is very good with explanations. She cares to remember students' names and is always ready for questions. Everything you need to know for exams are taught in class and you can learn them through the slides. If I got to take another class with her, I would.

It is clear that Professor Yang genuinely cares about her students. She is always open for questions, and her office hours are super helpful and personalized. The course workload is very manageable, with only a few homework assignments spread throughout the semester alongside exams and 2 simple group projects.

analysis.name: Xiaolong Yang
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: AVOID. AVOID. AVOID. Work load unreasonably heavy. Lecture is no more than reading off the slides and is BORING. Inflexible about schedule conflict, etc. Just avoid him if you can.

Strongly recommend everyone not to take his course. Unnecessary heavy workload and you will not learn anything. If you have taken a introductory CS course in high school you are overqualified for this course. and he uses outdated softwares beware of that !

He reads from the slides, you can pretty much learn it all by watching YouTube videos. Mostly HW, which is nice, but the midterm exam is terrible, no Final exam but Final Project. Pop quizzes are common, and so are pop attendance checks, these are extra-credit. The software he uses are so outdated, 90's-early 2000's.

For the sake of your mental health, please don't take this man's class. I wouldn't even call him a professor. I honestly have no idea how is he even teaching this class and many other classes and being part of statistics faculty at Cornell. He reads off a slides that were made in 90s and doesn't know anything. He's the worlds worst prof. periodt

Avoid him! His lectures are dry and confusing, office hours unhelpful, assignments poorly structured, and exams/quizzes overly detailed with outdated materials, no TA, and little genuine learning support despite being head of the program. I strongly urge the school to review his teaching and encourage improvements that actually help students learn.

analysis.name: Xiadong Cao
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Cao is a decent lecturer. At times, he struggled to re-frame explanations when asked questions. Make sure to read the textbook BEFORE lecture, or you may find yourself struggling to follow, especially towards the end. His tests are difficult, and he only provides one practice exam per prelim, not including final. Use hw to prepare for exams.
Professor Cao was nice, but the tests were harshly graded and the material was proof heavy. Tests were curved to a B.

Professor Xiadong is a very caring and compassionate professor. He inspired me to learn about Ricci flow and alleviated some of my insecurities about applying for graduate school. Only suggestion I would have is to encourage more collaboration among students. If multiple students worked together on a single paper, it would more likely be published.

analysis.name: Xin Zhou
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I find the lectures to lack structure and they are very unclear and disorganized. I mostly learned from the book instead of him. His prelims are outrageously difficult with the average being around a D. The course is mismanaged, with practically no office hours, no homework/exam solutions, no practice exams

MATH 4130 was my first higher-level math class, and I do have to say that I quite enjoyed my experience in it with Prof. Zhou. His lectures cleared up many confusions I had with the textbook and I found the problems he assigned to be challenging without being impossible. Would definitely recommend if you're looking to learn real analysis :)

Prof Zhou is easily one of the most consistent and efficient lecturers I've ever had. He rarely makes even minor mistakes and his delivery of material is extremely smooth. Real analysis is not always the most riveting subject - there are a lot of tedious details to keep track of, but Prof Zhou manages to make it interesting.

This class doesn't deserve the difficulty of an "Honors" class. We are entirely following the textbook, which is very easy, and nothing covered in the class went beyond the a regular mathematical analysis class. The professor is nice and teaches well, though. I just worry that this class is too easy.

analysis.name: Xingen Lei
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: While I appreciate Xingen's efforts in delivering course content, my learning experience was significantly marred by TA Keith Ou's unprofessional conduct that warrants urgent attention. As a student who values academic integrity, I feel compelled to document multiple instances of differential treatment that created a hostile learning environment.
Very manageable class and it covers two requirements for CALS so no reason not to take it.

Dr. Lei in general is a good professor. This rating is more a reflection of the course in general. ANSC 4880 is a very disorganized class that in its current state, I wouldn't recommend. The TA has only graded 2 assignments and his expectations are unclear and unfair. You can learn as much or as little as you want, but you have to go. Study hall?

Dr. Lei is one of my fav profs!!!!!! This class was the most applicable class I've taken in AnSci. The assignments are very reasonable but time consuming. Exams are graded fairly easily. He can be hard to understand at times, but you will get used to it quickly. Genuinely interesting class.

analysis.name: Xiaodong Cao
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Doesn't provide practice materials, doesn't respond to email or ed discussion, & offers absolutely no transparency regarding final grades, score distribution, or curves. Lectures are so disorganized that students skip his 10 am to go to the 8 am in order to learn from a different lecturer. Would avoid unless you've taken linear in HS or take eng.

the professor does not make the material accessible to people who haven't been exposed to linear algebra before. i saw people in the class enjoying it, but i'm not sure why. don't take this class unless you are super into math---it's super theoretical, and if you're not into that, take a different linear algebra.
Class wasn't organized. They gave no practice material for the finals. Prof. Cao wasn't very engaging and lectures got a bit boring.

I took him for 3210 and I left his lectures not knowing what I learned if anything. He only focused on explaining the conceptual concepts without providing examples which left us struggling on the tests. His lectures were extremely disorganized and often did not even cover the topic that we were supposed to be learning according to the handout.

I took him for 1920 and I left his lectures not knowing what I learned if anything. He only focused on explaining the conceptual concepts without providing examples which left us struggling on the tests. His lectures were extremely disorganized and often did not even cover the topic that we were supposed to be learning according to the handout.

analysis.name: Ding Xiang Warner
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I took Chinese Literary Tradition with her few years ago.  As other students point out, she is extremely dogmatic and stubborn. No powerpoint slide/textbook.  She expects you to take very good notes.  Tests were hard,  and she is a tough grader.  Avoid this class if you can.  It is not worthy to take it.

Worst professor I have ever had. The negative reviews below are all warranted and the positive ones are obviously written by her – a red flag on so many levels. ASIAN 2271 is an amalgam of ego, faux intellectualism, and a miserable attempt at over-compensation. Please avoid this class and this professor at all costs.

I typically suggest toughing out a course with interesting material even if the professor is less than ideal. Unfortunately, Professor "Warner" has somehow managed to create an environment that has greatly inhibited my learning abilities. Don't even bother adding any of her classes, you'll thank yourself later.
Absolutely no text book useage and she even refuse to provide any external reference book. All readings are fragmental and not connected with class material,which is merely her own creation.

Every thing she said was a bunch of foreign words jumbled together. Absolutely incoherent, at times I thought she was talkign to herself. When you ask help for an essay, she goes around in circles and does not help one single bit. Whatever she says goes in one ear and comes out the other. It's not your fault if you do bad, its her bad teaching skil

analysis.name: Grace Xing
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is very knowledgable but she assumes we are as knowledgable as her. Lectures have absolutely NOTHING related to the homework assignments. Get ready to read 600 pages of the textbook to have a chance at solving the HW.
Very unorganized class. Easily get lost in the lecture.
Lectures are so unorganized and useless

analysis.name: Munther Younes
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Munther is an amazing person and professor. He might seem intimidating, but is very kind, non-judgemental, and approachable. Stay on top of homework every night, and you will be just fine. It's a difficult language, but he does his best to make it digestible.

Fantastic. I took his intensive Arabic program - best decision ever! He wrote the textbook(s), and his methods of teaching dialect together with MSA are awesome. Take any/all of his classes. Attendance is a must, but if you do what he asks of you, you will learn Arabic quickly. I got an A+ in his 16 credit course and had no problems in Jordan after

This teacher is really dedicated to his students.  I was doing badly (I was too scared to get help) and he's worked with me and encouraged me to keep trying.  I'm now doing better.  He's great in class, a lot of fun, and easy to talk with outside of class.  I suggest: use office hours, do homework, and don't be afraid of him! He's great!

Munther is so brilliant and kind, highly recommend taking Arabic with him! Be prepared to learn and do the homework every night - if you miss more than a day or two, it will start building up. He's happy to help and has really interesting stories if you can get him to talk about his own life. Definitely not a class to slack off in but so worth it.

Amazing. He is wicked smart and so knowledgeable about all things language. He is truly passionate and makes you love the subject. Teaches Arabic and the Quran so well, even for non-heritage students. Pushes you to work and makes you a stronger student.

analysis.name: Yiran Zhang
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Zhang is a good professor. This class will be difficult regardless of who you take it with, but she has a genuine care for her students and does her best to relieve stress. She rounded my grade up more than I think I deserved. TA also plays a large role in ensuring that we studied well. Use the open note exams to your advantage!

Zhang is a extremely nice teacher, but a very tough grader. Quizzes are open book open note but will be challenging. Attendance is really only taken on your panel days. Content is interesting and her classes are very easy to follow and great powerpoints.TA will do most of the gradings. Watch out for the Memo tho, thats the hardest part of the class

Dr. Zhang is a really great professor with clear slides and plenty of resources for success. She's usually strict on assignments and extensions, and her grading can feel unpredictable unless you're 100% clear with the content. Visiting office hours is honestly the best way to clarify content and the best way to get a high grade.
Highly respect Prof Zhang. She was great. However, the grading can be harsh and unclear with little feedback. Would definitely take her again though.

Professor Zhang is a great professor and really cares about her students. Labor law is already a hard class, and her expectations about assignments and grading criteria were really unclear. The class introduces new ways of writing and thinking, and she fails to provide rubrics or teach students how to comprehensively answer questions.

analysis.name: Walker White
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 166 reviews, this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: CS 1110 is not beginner friendly in any way, shape, or form. Non-coders ABSOLUTELY DO NOT TAKE. Mr White expects his students to create a Alien Invaders game in one week, AND makes it due 4 days before the final. Be prepared to practically LIVE in consulting hours to get assignments done. Mr White himself is also egotistic.

Overall he's not bad but in my opinion he is overrated. If CS 1110 is your very first computer science class or you have no previous knowledge, you definitely want to do some studying beforehand. His lectures are very fast and he doesn't go very in depth. The exams are fair but the assignments were overwhelming.

Just graduated MechE and I took this first sem freshman year. I can confidently say that while dynamics and mechatronics were worlds harder in content, the class I struggled with most was undoubtedly CS1110 by FAR.
Walker's a great prof! The assignments require a lot of work, but are extremely interesting. It's hard to go to class and not listen to him - the lectures are interesting.

It was advanced projects in game design so not much teaching. Had to impress him with our projects instead. He would give good feedback and teach relevant game design ideas for your project. Im not sure that he fully appreciates good work though. If you ask him to recommend you, he may not present you favorably, regardless of your past work.

analysis.name: Yian Yin
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The more clear, organized, and through of the 2 instructors for this course. Material was not always explained well initially but he is very willing to help. The class was very disorganized and problem sets were often far removed from lectures. Be ready to wait until the day before a prelim to receive answer keys/grades from assignments

Course was a bit disorganized but professor Yin always posted his material on canvas and really tried to make sure people understood the concepts which weren't too difficult. The problem sets for this class are written by TAs so sometimes it was not synced with the lectures. Overall good professor but the course did lack organization.

Professor Yin is an alright professor. Very accomplished: he's on the 30 under 30. But the course was disorganized. Topics did not clearly relate to one another and were not explained intuitively. He was the better of the two professors for the course, but often the old lecture videos and textbook were more useful than the actual in class lectures.
homework are far away from the test. just study the test review

analysis.name: Cristobal Young
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: He gives the study guide beforehand, so it's easy to study in advance for the first two prelims. Final is a take-home with 3 short answers and 1 long essay question. Relatively easy, but grading varies by your TA. 3 short op-eds throughout the semester, but the rubric is odd, and TA feedback didn't help me much. Prof. Young is a great prof though!

There are 3 short essays throughout the semester and2 prelims and 1 final with a long-essay question. The 2 prelims come with study guide, so you can prepare before. Everything is basically graded by your Grad TA with the help of Professor Young. Does not take attendance frequently and is more spontaneous so you'll never really know when he will

The class is very easy. Lecture is late at night and they occasionally do random attendance checks at lecture, so try to go. Dr. Young is very approachable. Your grade is based on 3 opinion based memos, 2 prelims & a final (great study guide posted for prelims), and section/lecture attendance. He posts slides that correlate with assigned readings.

Young's super chill. Lectures could be understood by a middle schooler--if you really understand something, you can explain it to a 5-year-old. Young can do that. He delivers lectures in a casual and unpretentious manner. Study guides available w/ all possible questions for the prelims. Papers: ez opinion pieces. Actually riveting content!!!

I took 2 classes with Professor Young back in 2023 and 2024. Great guy, extremely accessible & down-to-earth. 
If you are struggling in his classes, you are clearly doing something wrong because the professor posts a list of possible questions. While some of the topics we discussed were a little outdated, the topics were generally very interesting.

analysis.name: Peiying Zhu
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Peggy grades very harshly, but gives good feedback and is super helpful during meetings. The class requires several essays, twice-weekly discussion posts on readings, daily quizzes, and an optional extra credit class-leading assignment. The extra credit is substantial, which makes it easy to get an A+, but be prepared to read and write a lot.

The difficulty was manageable but the workload is definitely on the heavier side for an FWS with weekly writing journals, quizzes, and discussion posts. On the flip side, Peggy is a nice person and puts in the effort to help you succeed. Overall, a solid class, but consider how interested you are in the course material before taking it.
Compared to other FWS I think this class is more difficult, but the content it covers is very interesting, so I think it was worth it. Essays are graded pretty harshly, but good feedback is given

Peggy is a tough grader, but is very knowledgable about her subject area. I enjoyed being in her class, and I would personally rate her a 5, but I know that she wasn't well liked by many others in the class. It was her first time teaching, and I think she did a great job. Her feedback was always articulate, and she was always very direct.

analysis.name: Zakhary Mallett
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is knowledgeable of the content, but doesn't seem to care much about the class itself. Many times, his lecture slides are incomplete in class. Course material is covered in lecture once, and never again. Prelims are incredibly in-depth and he provides no additional material for studying. Pop-quizzes are on very specific details from the readings

Very knowledgeable professor of urban planning. Lacks personable skills to relate to students and takes topics too seriously. Harsh grader and brutal critic. Limited grades throughout the semester, many readings required, with potential to do poorly on pop quizzes due to exact fact memorization required. Learning potential is high, work hard.

Prof. Mallett is very knowledgeable and I enjoyed the content, but I found the grading structure to be difficult to deal with, and ultimately dropped the class. Expect unreasonably hard pop quizzes occasionally. If you don't ace every test + the final paper, it's hard to get above a B+ as there is no curve and questions are weighted heavily.

analysis.name: Alan Zehnder
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Lectures weren't great but you can't blame him when not even a third of the class shows up. Quick to reply to emails and Ed and helpful in office hours. Exams are fair but HW is time consuming. The final project takes a while but isn't worth much.

His explanations are super clear and I actually found his lectures very insightful because he brought up many interesting real-life problems and how diffeq could be applied to derive models to solve those problems. Even when the class went online, he still managed the course logistics very well. It was my most favorite class for Spring 2020.

Dude's a legend. Takes time after class to explain anything you had problems with. Yeah the homework is a lot for sure, and he expects some basic fluency in excel/python for some assignments, but he's really good at introducing Differential Equations. Handled the online transition like a champ too (Spring 2020)

Assigns way too many problems for homework, get ready for over 20 pages of problems that really are just a waste of your paper. Doesn't teach well, gets most examples from Paul's Math Notes. Since moving to online (Spring 2020), the class has become a nuisance.

Good prof, explains things pretty clearly, but by no means goes in detail.  Teaches how to solve problems, but no more, so all context of problems is lost.  Not hugely interesting, either.  Nice guy though, and very understanding.

analysis.name: Samantha Zacher
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor Zacher is a wonderful professor who truly enjoys the subject she is teaching. Her enthusiasm makes engaging in class much easier. She is also not a harsh grader by any means so it is easy to do well in her classes. All that is required is that you engage in the class. Overall her class was very easy, informative, and fun.

Professor Zacher is incredibly knowledgeable, funny, and gets you thinking far beyond the classroom in "The Imaginary Jew." The course is a brilliant interweaving of films, manuscripts, books, and poems. There is lot of writing (which was a good thing) but the class was not only English majors. Fascinating and very well taught- TAKE THIS CLASS

As my first four-thousand level English course, especially one on the roots of anti-Semitism, I was nervous as to what this class would be like. Professor Zacher was amazing. Not only did she her happy energy lighten some of the heavy readings, every lecture was engaging. This class (and its professor) are wonderful. Would recommend highly!

Professor Samantha Zacher is an incredibly warm, funny, engaging individual. Her enthusiasm and her teaching abilities make class easy and really enjoyable. She is also invested in her students and their work. She takes the time to read and comment closely on your papers, improving your knowledge and your writing abilities at the same time.

I love Professor Zacher!  She made an obscure topic (Icelandic lit) fascinating by her enthusiasm and obvious expertise.  She wants her students to do well and learn and is willing to spend time helping them do so.  Her class was not easy, but it was not stressful either.  If for no other reason, take her class to learn how to be chic!

analysis.name: Brad Zukovic
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Brad will push you to deepen your analysis and ground it in textual evidence and close-reading. He's not always super clear with his feedback but gives plenty of room for improvement. He rambles a bit in class but is a good professor overall and has very flexible deadlines. He will definitely push you to improve your writing.

If you just wanna gain a S grade in FWS , this course is your best choice. Pro. Brad put the whole grading and workload pretty little and the estimated time spending per week is 5 hrs or less. But he is willing to listen to your advice and if you want to dive in deep topics, you can still talk with him in person.
Brad is hands down the GOAT. Class will improve how you write and think. Do anything you can to snag a spot in pre-enroll.

Brad is the professor I recommend to anyone needing to take an FWS. He is an amazing storyteller and seems to know so much about everything! He was pretty flexible with deadlines especially if you have a lot going on. He is always available for office hours and will work around your schedule, so take advantage of that. Your grade will thank you!!

One of the best prof at Cornell. Write whatever ideas that into your brain, Brad is always neutral and will be 100% respectful to them, and he likes us to come up with 'special ideas'. Great and deep lectures, great and deep feedback on the paper. You will have revision chances for each paper, and you can show him the draft for each paper.

analysis.name: Sasa Zivkovic
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt
