review_highlights.comment: Worst professor ever had. Completely unclear grading standards, random and nonsensical feedbacks. Seems to enjoy discouraging students rather than helping them improve. I've consistently received A's throughout my academic life, yet got a C in ARCH1101. If you're assigned to him taking a gap year would honestly be a better choice.

Hands down the worst professor imaginable. His grading system is a complete joke, no logic, no transparency, just random punishments disguised as “feedback.” He doesn't teach; he demoralizes. Every class feels like psychological warfare.

In my entire time taking this class I only saw him on lecture days with the exception of one pin-up where I received vague and confusing feedback.

I wish I had more time to create a rapport with him, and maybe get more project clarification from him, but he was gone for a significant part of the year.

Sasa often skips classes and gives vague feedback, but he is quite flexible with deadlines (don't take that for granted) as long as students stay healthy. He also doesn't tell us how to think about drawings and spaces, which makes us think of more radical and unconventional ideas and solutions. He really pushes us to challenge precedents and norms!

Put so much into this class to receive only a B- I did what I was told and made changes to assignments to the professors liking and still had trouble. The professors were barely around in the class compared to the TAs.

analysis.name: Aaron Adalja
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: He gives out almost weekly reading quizzes online to do. He told us they would be simple if you done the reading but they are very specific with a bunch of numbers and thus it's quite complicated even as someone who did all the reading. His exams are crazy as well. They are so heavy in material and nothing like past professors of Food Ops. BAD PROF

The class is not enjoyable at all. He does not care about his students. All he does is give incredibly hard quizzes that ask you very specific questions. His exams are also unnecessarily hard. He is also very uptight during exams and won't let you ask any questions at all even if they are just clarifying questions. I miss the old professor.
Good lectures and Good office hours. I just hate the labs. No finals and the prelims are pretty easy if you study (I got an average of high 80s just by studying 2 days prior with chatgpt)
Fantastic professor. Two prelims, gave review sessions for both. While lab grade primarily depends on chefs, Professor Adalja is very understanding and helps in giving proper feedback.

The content of this class isn't the most interesting; however, Professor Adalja does a good job of making it engaging. Grading criteria is very clear. Exams were easy and straightforward if you studied. There are lots of ways you can cusion your grade (reading quizzes, hw, projects, etc.) if you are worried about not doing well on the prelims.

analysis.name: Fengqi You
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 151 reviews, this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Very engaging class and interesting applications. Professor is extremely knowledge about course topics and always ready to help. Great class overall. One of the best in the Systems Engineering Masters track.
Professor You is the most professional, most helpful, and most well prepared lecturer in my experience as a distance learner at Cornell. Very GOOD class!
Excellent professor. I have taken all of the classes that he offers and enjoyed every single on of them.

A wonderful machine learning course! I did not take 5800 in the fall, but the professor made an exception for me to try it out this term as he may leave Cornell next year. Super DL-friendly course! The best one in Cornell System Engineering. I hope other DL students could benefit from Professor You's classes while still here

Great professor. Very responsive to questions on piazza and by emails. Homeworks are the right level of difficulty to help us comprehend the material without placing an unexpectedly difficult burden/challenge on the students.

analysis.name: Aaron Sachs
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Sachs is a good lecturer and he puts a lot of passion into his Environmental History course, but I get the vibe that he wishes he was teaching a literature class. We fixated on a few specific books at various points rather than covering various perspectives on each period. The class is well-structured, but I expect more variety in a history class.

Sachs was very passionate and kind. Lectures were a bit more vague than I expected and the essays were also very arbitrary, but the grading is not too harsh and you learn a lot of really interesting aspects of American history through the lens of environment that you wouldn't expect.

Lectures are not mandatory but they are essential (no slides, only talking). Handwritten notes only. He'd lecture for most of the class and then do a Q&A at the end. I found him to be really funny and engaging. 4 papers and they're graded pretty fairly. Really (really) long readings every week for discussion though. Def one of my fav professors tho

Took Enviro History with Prof. Sachs and would recommend for anyone who is interested in the topic. Readings are lengthy but usually interesting. Only 3 papers, and prompts allow you a lot of flexibility to be creative or explore a topic of your interest. He takes the writing process very seriously but if you put in the effort you'll do well.

I cannot stress enough to anyone considering taking the course: take it. As long as you do most of the readings (enough to gain a general understanding), the essays are not hard (and they aren't long either). He is such an amazing lecturer, and it feels like he is telling stories rather than information dumping.

analysis.name: Aaron Wagner
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Clear and engaging lectures along with helpfulness outside of class make one of the hardest classes I have ever taken an extremely worthwhile experience.

Wagner is one of the best professors at Cornell. He organizes lecture in a clear manner and always tries to keep it interactive so you don't fall asleep learning a ton of math. Projects in the second half of the course are extremely well designed and educational; however, they are very challenging. Exams and problem sets were also difficult.

Overall a good course. Attendance is not mandatory for portion of grade. While slides are not provided, the textbook is enough for keeping up with the material. Homework and exam mainly cover basic concepts in information theory, which may be a bit tough for students more focused on coding. Two projects emphasize novelty over programming skills.

I took Wagner for ECE 2720 and initially didn't like him.  I matured a bit since then in terms of school preparation and took ECE 4670 with him recently. Wagner will not hold your hand --- you need to go to lecture, office hours, and understand the material well. But if you do, he's a fair guy, great lecturer, and the coursework is very doable.

Excellent, if a bit quirky, lecturer. Really cares about students. Extremely helpful. Exams require not only mastery of material, but cleverness. Minimal amount of homework, with flexible policies. One of the best professors I've had.

analysis.name: Aaron Rock-Singer
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Rock-Singer was disorganized and messy. I took an intro class with him, yet I felt like I was super behind all of the students because I didn't have any previous knowledge of Islamic Civilization... the class brought down my GPA even though I was working super hard, and the readings weren't helpful!

I really loved Prof. Rock-Singer's lectures because they cleared up anything I didn't understand in the pre class readings. His class definitely packed a lot of information about pre-modern Islamic civilization, but coming from no previous knowledge on this topic, it was easy to succeed if you do the readings and attend lecture.
Prof. Rock-Singer is a passionate teacher who is invested in his students as people.

analysis.name: Ron Mize
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Not enough space here to describe how horribly unorganized this professor is. Its like they poured researched gathered online and put it on random powerpoint presentations that were full of errors and messy. No clarity or explanations on assignments, very dry course teaching, no interactive experience on such an importance study of a course. Awful

He really knows what he is talking about. Though he is very distractable and goes off on tangents sometimes, he is always entertaining, and will get the class laughing at/with him at least once per class. The reading is heavy, and necessar. You can choose to make this a hard or easy course depending on how much real thought you put into it.

He used statistics, history, & a few specific case studies to describe the communities, but never went deeper than that. No analysis, no insights, almost nothing about culture. Just when I thought it was going somewhere, he would move on to a different ethnic group. Bland, superficial, uninteresting. Not bad, just not good.

Covered a broad range of topics concerning an incredibly broad range of racial and ethnic groups. He assigns a lot of readings but it's all really interesting. He really challenges you to introduce new thoughts. Extremely fair grader. AMAZING professor. Take any one of his classes.

The best teacher I've had at CU. He made lectures interesting and engaging. I couldn't ask for a better lecturer. It's a bit reading intensive (~150pgs/wk) but worth it!!! Take his classes. He's even got a fanclub on facebook!

analysis.name: Aaron Hodges
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Aaron has devoted a number of years of his life to the subject matter he teaches. Having said this, he is very passionate and intuitive about his work. His sincere care about philosophy reflects a deep concern about human nature, and in a larger sense, the nature of nature. He is a wonderful instructor and I recommend taking his class.

Aaron's class really freaked me out at first. I think he hated my first couple papers. The trick is to really listen to what he says in class and take notes. He says lots of key stuff you can use in your paper. I think he likes that.
Aaron is a really professional instructor who really cares about philosophy... and it shows!  He may seem strict at first but he always gave really helpful comments on papers.

analysis.name: Abigail Crites
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment:
Doesn't teach well, loses chain of thought mid sentence and moves to something different
Didn't give good intuition behind concepts, could not answer questions properly
Spent half the classes giving unhelpful worksheets, making people discuss among themselves
Did not use chalkboard much to write derivations (ironically this being an honors class)

Professor Crites is a terrible lecturer. What is taught in lecture is not even remotely close to the level of difficulty of the problem sets which aren't even insanely difficult; the class is just taught so poorly that you are on your own for the homework. The prelim was confusing and almost impossible to prepare for even with the practice prelim.

Prof. Crites is one of the most caring and approachable professors I have ever had. Unfortunately, many of the other ratings are correct in that her lectures tend to be quite scattered and lack the foundational base I feel is needed in EM. Exams are also very odd and non-representative of psets. Report assignment was very fun and unique though!

Prof. Crites' lectures definitely needed some reorganization, but she was overall very pleasant and willing to help all of her students. Though I did not learn as much as I think was needed for future classes, much of this was due to students interrupting the lecture, not Prof. Crites

As someone who has consistently enjoyed Electricity and Magnetism before this point, much preferring it over mechanics, I have found this class to, finally, have the opposite effect. It drained significant interest that I'd previously had for the subject. If you have the choice, take 2213 instead. You'll learn something in that class.

analysis.name: Ron Applegate
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Very nice guy, lectures can be boring sometimes, especially if you have done the reading.  They tend to be long summaries. He does try very hard to give each student individual attention with their final papers and goes out of his way to be helpful

Hands down my favorite class at the ILR School. Applegate is an excellent professor if you like historical analysis! I can see how the subject matter can be sort of boring but between the readings, you're bound to find something to spark your interest. Professor Applegate gets an A+ for both his teaching and his charming character. HIGHLY RECMD.

His classes make a great choice for an ILR history elective. Grade was based on a short essay, prelim essay, and a final essay (all take home). His grading criteria is very clear and he explicitly lays out everything you need to do on the essays in order to do well on them. He also provides a lot of feedback on essays that I found helpful.

I hate to dis this guy because he's very caring and loves his students, but he's an incredibly boring speaker.  Also, a****LOAD of reading to do, and even then it's tough to put together a compelling essay you're proud of given the weird material you have to draw from.  If you like history/reading, though, it will not be that tough.

Lectures were extremely dry and hard to get through for this course. The reading wasn't unbearable, but definitely a little on the tougher side. A nice course in the sense that it is a lift from the traditional "labor history" of the LR department but I thought it was challenging. Expectations on assignments were pretty vague.

analysis.name: Dorit Abusch
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Blunt and generally unfriendly both inside and outside of class. Disengaged students from participation. Confusing presentation of course material and unclear about what she expected from students. Difficult to understand, not because of her accent but because of her convoluted manner of expressing herself. Not recommended.
Fairly hostile and consistently late for class.

There are 6 final essays in this FWS. Each essay has one draft. There is basically one piece of writing due each week (she assigns them Thursdays and they are due on Sunday). Essays are short (1-2 pages, double spaced).

She edits them, but if you write a bad draft, she will deduct points off the final essay. Attendance is 10% of the grade.

This is definitely a class worth taking, the writing is relatively easy just annoying that its due sunday. The prof is hilarious, many times unintentionally and it's overall a fun time. Lots of reading (DO IT) but not unbearable, work totals to about 2-4 hours a week. Feedback is not the best but you'll eventually learn what she expects.

analysis.name: Abe Davis
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Lectures are good but also kinda unrelated to the projects. Projects take up ~60% of your grade, there is only one midterm and no final exam. Abe is too busy. Grading criteria is very vague everywhere. Better sell your soul to the project otherwise you only get completion credit (~85%). It'll be real tough if you have bad teammates.

Assignments can be extremely long and tedious at times, lectures are not as helpful as needed, pretty much just verbatim from slides. Would not recommend if you are looking for an easy 3 credit CS distribution.

Professor Davis is still new, so I'm sure that he will continue to get better, but the class was rather disorganized. Assignments were being written and bugfixed while we did them, and lectures, while interesting, were unrelated to any graded content. The grading was rather lenient to adjust for these issues, though.
I love everything about this class, except that it's mostly group projects. If you don't have good group members, you're kind of stuck.

Abe is good at demonstrating concepts with engaging materials, such as code demos, animation, and high-quality explainer videos. He wrote his own package for making graphics web apps, which can make the assignments more fun if it's better documented. Abe is super approachable and he held lots of extra OH. His organization does need improvement...

analysis.name: Abdul Nanji
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great professor, would definitely recommend.

not a very good teacher, impatient, defensive when challenged, particularly nervous around grad students.  not sure prof nanji has the training and background to be teaching at this level at this University

analysis.name: Abraham Stroock
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Get there early. He starts writing notes before lecture that you need. He makes mistakes-just writes the wrong stuff, and has gotten corrected on concepts by students. He starts 3-4 sentences at once and finishes 1. He does everything he can to avoid answering questions. Hes very smart, but get ready to teach the class to yourself. Tas r chill

His lectures were always pretty clear. He was precise with how he worded things, and I was rarely ever confused by how he taught. Not an exciting lecturer, but he certainly gets the job done well. Pretty nice guy.

Jumps back and froth between topics during lecture. Legible handwriting. Long problem sets. Was missing for some crucial lectures that the grad TA ended up giving which resulted in an average of 50 on the final.

Prof Stroock is a great professor. Not only does he engage the students in class in via posing questions, but cares to get to know them on a personal level too. He's great 1-1 if you're having difficulty with the course, but just raising your hand and asking the question during lecture doesn't bother him in the least (unlike some other professors).
Pretty good professor - has clear lecturing style and engages class by posing questions frequently.

analysis.name: Jayadev Acharya
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The lectures for this class are very boring, and have no substance. What makes it even worse is the fact there is no real "book" that you can look at to self study. The discussion sections are even more boring as the TAs are very lost and disorganized. The whole class structure is disorganized and questions in ED remain unanswered.

Pretty light workload, but when exams come around be ready to spend a lot of time figuring out what you were supposed to be learning all semester. Extremely poor organization, 60% of lecture recapping last lecture. Review material almost nothing like what gets tested and is often incorrect. Check EdDiscussion constantly for corrections of mistakes

Such an unorganized class. The prelim is in a few days but nothing about the prelim has been answered on Ed. The only thing given was a past prelim as practice material given on Friday when the prelim is on Tuesday. TAs can't even tell us if a note sheet is allowed for the prelim. Overall we, including the TAs and professor are all confused.

Every lecture, repeats 70% of content from previous lecture, yet somehow still makes it incomprehensible. Disorganized course with zero feedback from grades, students are left on their own to figure everything out.
Don't bother. Take the class in the spring.

analysis.name: Gerard Aching
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He is a solid professor overall and likes to answer questions. He does not give rubrics for his few graded assignments. I found that he contradicted himself sometimes ideologically and seemed to respect his own ideas more than students' or just had a hard time relating. He says he is understanding but also puts you off a bit too, so tread lightly.

Professor Aching is awesome! He is extremely intelligent and knowledgable of the subject he is teaching while simultaneously open to hearing and understanding the opinions of others. He is also really understanding when it comes to extensions, missing class, etc. Grading is a bit tough but you get used to it and there's not that much work.

He has a reputation for not caring about classes since he's near retirement, and can be a bit ethereal and touchy as someone else mentioned. He's really brilliant, but during the pandemic kind of lectured about something off topic. Kind of moody, and maybe even rude at times

analysis.name: Chloe Ahmann
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: knowledgeable person and very fair grading, i enjoyed lectures and we covered a good variety of interesting content. my TA was also great, some readings were dense but she tried to make discussion sections engaging as well.

Dr. Ahmann is incredible. Not only is she a knowledgeable, and passionate anthropologist, but she also is relatable and approachable. She even took the time to speak to me about my interest in switching to anthropology as a major. Her lectures were Ted Talk quality and grading was more than fair. Couldn't recommend taking her classes highly enough!

The class is interesting but Prof. Ahmann often uses language that can be confusing for a person who has not taken an anthro class before. Her slides are helpful so I recommend taking notes. I also think the readings were a little tough but attending discussion helps (Attendance is mandatory for discussion). You can bring notes to exams which helps
Very intelligent professor who gives well put together lectures. Make sure to take notes in class because lecture slides aren't posted.

Had her for a FWS on time travel. The nature of the class meant it was really reading heavy but they were all interesting and nothing felt like busy work. She took the time to get to know her students and clearly cared about our wellbeing. I would reccomend

analysis.name: Ahmed El Alaoui
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Genuinely an aweosme professor, I can tell he knows a lot about what he's teaching. Lectures sometimes rehash from the textbook but I like the real-world examples + exercises he gives us. Lots of emphasis on tests which are pretty difficult but fair. I feel like you get what you put into this class, if you put a lot of effort it's really enriching

I think he's a nice guy, but I didn't really find his lectures to be that helpful. He writes on the chalkboard and it's hard to follow along, and his notes are hand-written and just not presented in a super clear manner. The tests were a bit too hard in my opinion but he did release practice exams with solutions.

Class started a little tense but he eased up as time went on and the course was relatively enjoyable because it didn't require too much work and tests had the right amount of challenge imo. I wouldn't say lectures were terribly interesting, more of dump from his notes. Overall ok class and chill professor while a good lead into to math stats.

Tests are worth a lot and are not very comprehensive. The best way to describe them is that they tested a few specific topics but tested them very in depth. Lectures are completely useless if you are brand new to the material. You will spend most of your time teaching yourself and then trying to figure out what he wants you to know.

Lectures were poor regurgitations of the textbook, so I just read the textbook instead. Each homework would take 12+ hours because they were insanely long and difficult. Exam medians were 30% and 50% and the professor still did not adjust his difficulty and it just made me feel dumb and stressed. Class felt like multivariable calc part 3 not stats.

analysis.name: Fred Ahl
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I took a few courses with Ahl almost ten years ago and his remain the best lectures I have ever heard in my life.  I sat through his classes riveted and anticipating whatever it was about antiquity he would say next.  It was amazing and I am grateful I happened into his classes.

An absolutely amazing professor and person in general. Show up to his class and just listen. Write a few notes but really just absorb the content. Most importantly DO THE READINGS!!!! If you go to class, participate and do the readings this will be the most fun and amazing class. A class with Professor Ahl is an experience no one should pass up!

The most fascinating and engaging lecturer I've had at Cornell, and in my top 2 or three all time (I have a BA elsewhere).  Like another reviewer said, sometimes you just put the pen down and listen. Assigned very creative and effective essay prompts, one of which I now use as a writing teacher. Opinionated for sure. Progressive white British.

Fred Ahl is an all-time great professor. I was his student 43 years ago and he was one of the most impressionable professors I have ever had in my life. Fred has no hubris. He is what he is, and he is excellent at what he teaches. I learned Attic Greek From Prof. Ahl, and he was the best Classics professor I ever had.

This professor is very opinionated and does not grade fairly. I wrote a paper about religious themes in a certain play and he objected strongly to everything I wrote. He has a pet theory for every play he assigns. And he shows very little respect for students. He would mockingly read little bits from students' papers.

analysis.name: Ahren Sadoff
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: The class itself is interesting, but the tests are incredibly unfair. I feel as though I learned a lot, but it is difficult to demonstrate that knowledge on tests, as it reaches farther than what we have learned in class. He is also difficult to follow and has a very old-school way of lecturing. May have been good in the 80's, but now should retire

I loved being in Prof. Sadoff's class, but his tests were extremely difficult. The subject matter was interesting, and his lectures and book explained it very well, but the exams went so far beyond anything we'd learned in class. And he requires answers word-for-word. Also, your TA really matters-- and frequently they aren't very good.

I'm the same person who wrote below comment. The prelim today was hard, because I didn't study hard. I think if you study hard this subject, you will get much out of it before taking more harder Quantum mechanics courses. However, you should know that it requires much effort despite of 1 credit. Besides, dinner with the professor everyweek is nice.

Warm hearted man with a whack sense of humor. Very passionate about his material & wanted us to know HIS book, word for word. Lectures were more often than the not, WORD FOR WORD of his txtbook. Test questions can be worded VERY poor. Interesting subject until he battered it to a pulp. I say go for it if you're ready to memorize a novel :)

A great man and teacher who tries to instill his passion for physics onto his students. Most of the class is based on his book with some stuff from his lectures as well. There is homework and a few exams that require time and effort, but the class isn't anything crazy. That doesn't mean this class is an easy A, though, because it's not.

analysis.name: Sharon Ahlers
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: There is a LOT of work for this course, and she is a HARD grader. If you are taking a tough engineering courseload, it's probably not a good idea to take this class with her.I decided to switch to the ENGRC  335 class taught by Rick Evans and he was a substantially easier/better professor. I would recommend taking the class with Rick Evans instead.

Organized, clear and hefpful this professor really makes an effort to that your presentation and writing skills improve. Excellent course to take regardless of your major. Do not be discouraged by seemingly intense course load, its really is not that bad and help is always there.

Very good instructor.  Sharon's very easy going, willing to go out of her way to help students and truly cares about the course.  The workload is fairly light throughout the semester and the assignments actually do serve a purpose.  Overall, a very good class

What are you guys talking about, Miss Ahlers is the easiest grader I've ever encountered at Cornell, she runs a wonderful class, speaks clearly, and the work load is light compared to any engineering class.  Highly recommended.
Very timely and organized just as a lecturer should be. The material is useful and applicable to the real world.

analysis.name: Beth Ahner
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This class is also listed as ENGRD 251. She lectures clearly and strives to incorporate relevant current events into assignments and broaden knowledge. For most students, this is the first class where students will be assigned case studies and will need to write up an engineering report. She assigns no textbook, you buy the notes from her for $10.
She is wonderful! Very helpful, and the class is super interesting...one of my favorites! She keeps it interesting and fun, and is a really nice and approachable person.
she tries to be helpful, and she is nice, but Class lectures are boring.  Must go to class because nothing useful in the textbook

analysis.name: Ehsan Afshari
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This guy is smart, but teaches stuff wayyyy too fast, and gives homework way harder than anything we've done in class. If you have the time to read the book for hours on end outside of class, then this guy is great, but with the assumption we all have other things to do, this class was way too difficult.

Ehsan seems really smart, but he is the worst kind of professor to have. He isn't helpful outside of class, and assigns labs and homeworks that take upwards of 10 hours to complete, every week. He is great in lecture, but makes the class unbearable.
He spends enough time on all details and goes deep to the heart of the subject. Overall the course was useful, but not easy at all.
Great course and organized lectures.
He taught the course really well

analysis.name: Adhy Kim
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I think he's okay and tries his best, but his teaching style isn't really my favorite. Usually we read before class and have a pop quiz on it. It's ✔️-, ✔️, or ✔️+. Take note cuz they help for exams. Overall, I probably would not choose a professor with this style again. Too much reading and some are hard to understand...

Professor Kim is very kind! His feedback on essays is thoughtful and encouraging. The class readings were also intellectually stimulating, and it is clear he is knowledgeable in his area of study. Highly recommend this class for anyone looking for a survey course in the Asian Studies department (or if you're an English major, for LoA or GS).
The class was informative and covered a lot of different topics. He gives extra credit opportunities and the exams are reasonable. Pretty straightforward for 4 credits.

Weirdest class ever, 1.5 hour long lectures where he basically j quizzes u on the reading for that day, makes you finish specific lines from 30+ page text. Would also say class is very extreme w social politics and pretty outrageous at times e.g. asked a student how they felt abt being a person of color in finance as it aligns w imperialism

Really amazing, kind professor! Had him for my English seminar class this semester. Upholds attendance policies and syllabus guidelines, but cares about students and the texts so much. Highly recommend :)

analysis.name: Adriana Reyes
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is definitely a tough and harsh grader, I was not aware of the circumstances of her teaching and grading style before enrolling in her class. I just wanted to come on here and share that you will really have to work and give your everything to obtain the grade you want.
Class was mainly boring and non enthusiastic, grades weren't easy to maintain in the class, overall wouldn't recommend
Respectable professor, but harsh grader and is not flexible with any grade related content.
Prof Reyes means well (I think), but can be dreadfully boring….so much so that I can't even focus on what she's saying. Like would it kill to not just read off the slide?
Tough grader, must put in full work capacity.

analysis.name: Adam Hoffman
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: An absolute G. Exams were more than reasonable, lectures were very interesting. 3 exams (all doable) and 2 assignments. Extra credit as well. Very nice dude and chill lecturer. Would recommend to anyone.

Loved Prof Hoffman and this class even tho it was 8:40 am 🫣 but he made it interesting and I stayed awake! Plus if you go to the gym before class you'll prob see him there getting hyped lol 😆 Honestly, an icon and super dedicated to student learning. He brings guests to show different stages of human development & that's super cool and engaging.

Professor was great. Lectures were optional but I highly recommend attending them as he gives test hints during them. He provides extra credit which can be beneficial if you do poorly on an assignment once so its not like your grade will fall from one bad exam/assignment. Exams were super fair and based on the content and he doesn't trick questions

Professor Hoffman was a great instructor, and the course material was clear and easy to understand. The class mainly uses lecture slides and includes two assignments. Attendance is optional, but lectures often include exam hints (attend class). The course is memorization-based, so reviewing notes and using Quizlet makes the prelims much easier.

Professor Hoffman taught a very easy yet interesting class. The grading is 3 prelims, an infographic, and a 2-page paper. Attendance is not mandatory but in class he will give "hints" about what is on the prelim.  He also offered extra credit for completing the class survey. Overall, if you are looking for an easy HD class this is a great option.

analysis.name: Jawad Addoum
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Absolute aura farmer of a professor. You must attend class AND take notes. If note-taking is neglected, you will struggle on the exams bc a lot of the material is gatekept & left off the slides. Tests focus on depth rather than breadth, so ensure you understand the reasoning behind each concept. Just knowing how to use the formulas will not cut it.

Amazing professor, so engaging and genuinely all around great guy. GO TO CLASS and TAKE NOTES it will make a huge difference because you will not be able to just study based off of notes posted. Most of the important formulas and solutions he will ONLY talk about in class.

Professor Addoum is a pretty great teacher. I think hes pretty funny and his lectures are all pretty informative. Just get ready for the 3 exams since thats really the only thing your grade is dependent on.

My favorite professor on campus hands down. The finance class he teaches is critical for any business student to take as the concepts you learn are invaluable. He is a fantastic lecturer, and keeps things clear for everyone to understand. The material can be tough but curve is huge, he is awesome, and I couldn't recommend a class with him more.
chill professor but tries too hard to be funny. does not provide any practice for exams other than the 4 easy homework we had that did not help on the exams. take the hotel version instead.

analysis.name: Lee Adler
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I learned soooo much about Brown v. Board through this class. The reliance on group discussions was a nice difference from other courses. He's also VERY accessible outside of class. You really only have to lock in for the last assignment; everything else is super chill. Make sure to participate consistently, or else it may impact your grade. 10/10.
This is the greatest class I've ever had, taught by the greatest prof I've ever had. I am so serious, take any class taught by Lee Adler if you can

Professor Adler really cares about his students and caters his lectures to the class. Most of the work is reading court cases and then discussing them in class. His papers are tough, but you will learn a lot in his class.

Great professor and interesting topic, but class is unorganized. Readings and assignments are sent in long/confusing email chains. Otherwise, Prof is very knowledgeable and shares many personal stories about the topic of the day. Lots of reading, but no need to do it all. Participation/attendance and a final paper are all that grades are based on.

Prof. Adler is one of the best professors in all of ILR. He has amazing experiences to tell and truly cares about his students and the material he teaches. He may go off-topic a fair amount but only because of his enormous breadth of knowledge in literally every topic imaginable.

analysis.name: Adeolu Ademoyo
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: ABSOLUTELY do not take this class. While homework and overall work os very manageable. Class is not a good environment. Can feel very scared to make mistakes and picks on certain students for no teason. He actually picks on students that are struggling in the language. Never had anxiety until started this class because of him.

Very nice professor. Likes to encourage students. Informs us on ongoing political and cultural issues. He's very animated and enthusiastic about teaching. Sometimes he's a little too nice to undeserving students. It's not even a hard class! Simply attend and learn Yoruba (the best language and people in the world! :-) )

I really enjoyed this class! ADEMOYO IS A GREAT PROFESSOR! He aims to have his students learn the material but does not overwhelm them. The class is totally manageable and the professor is extremely helpful. Best language class I've EVER taken :)

Compared to other language courses, this is definitely one of the best options. He is EXTREMELY disorganized which makes the course much more stressful, but he ultimately gives everyone a good grade (A- or above), unless you are really bad then maybe he'll give you a B+. Much less work than other language classes. He acts a lot and is fun also!
Insane teacher!! Really animated and funny. Subtly disguises heavy philosophy in jokes. A good class to have in Freshman year.

analysis.name: Marcelo  Aguiar
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor Aguiar is definitely one of the best professors in the math department. If you pay attention in class, you should not have any problems following him and do well in the course. He would always stay after class to answer questions. If you do ask him questions, he would give an answer appropriate for your level of mathematical maturity.

By far, this was the most amazing math professor I have ever had. Sit in one lecture and you'll see why, every definition and example he works through is explained thoroughly and instead of just giving formulas, he derives them so you really understand. I felt prepared for exams because of him. My only complaint is review sheets don't have answers.

Marcelo is the best teacher I've had at Cornell. His explanations are impeccable and he makes every concept in the class easy to understand. The guy loves math. If we finish class early, he'll take a few minutes to explain how what we're learning relates to cool random things in nature, like astronomy, network connectivity, biology, etc. Great guy.

He is amazing. He explains math so carefully/simply that anyone who pays attention will understand everything eventually. This was the only class where I didn't miss a single lecture because if you do you'll miss his great explanations. Go to class, do your homework, and do the exam handouts before every exam. And you'll do great

Taking a course with Marcelo Aguiar is truly a privilege. His lectures are well-structured and inspiring. In office hours and even after lecture he is super approachable and answers all questions clearly. His exams are on par with the review materials he provides.

analysis.name: Anurag Agrawal
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Anurag's very appearance and his wonderful mien inspire me to galvanize myself into action, grow a magnificent neckbeard, and go out into the world to explore the uncharted wilderness. He also wears entrancing t-shirts.

This class was easy to do well in, but hard to get an A if that's what you're shooting for. The prelims are a bit knit-picky and the online textbook can be a bit boring but I would otherwise totally recommend this Intro. to Bio course for non-majors. The lectures were the most dynamic ones I've seen considering the size of the class.

Great professor. Clear and concise, and absolutely love to teach. Clearly wants his students to learn instead of just stuffing random bio information down your throat and expecting you to regurgitate. He is very approachable and engaging in lecture. I really wished 3690 was taught only by him instead of a group of professors.

Excellent lecturer in an intro bio course. Found lectures on monarch butterfly ecology (his area of expertise) fascinating. I thoroughly enjoyed the course, although I wish practice prelims or any sort of exam questions were given. Wish he lectured more than the other professors.

Not the most useful course but easy and extremely interesting (read: senior course). Teaches by telling awesome stories about chemical ecology phenomena in nature. Good humored, fun, really smart, approachable. Great prof.

analysis.name: Jennifer Agans
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Worst professor I've ever had at Cornell. She doesn't post the lecture slides online, she's extremely aggressive, she goes so fast and then gets when you ask her to go back. If you don't have to take this course, don't! (from her at least, I've heard good reviews about the prof that taught it before her).

She is nice. However, the fact that version A and B of the prelim did not have the same questions is unfair to both the students who had A and the students who had B. She needs to compensate for this mistake. The first prelim was manageable but the 2nd one seemed to ask very specific and hard questions that she nor the textbook covered.

It is unbelievable how unprepared one woman can be. She made me hate the adolescent development, even though it could be good if taught right. In addition to making ridiculous comments like, "The US has the highest rate of pregnancy in the world," she uses childish methods to make her class settle down such as, "If you can hear me clap twice."

Worst Professor I have had at Cornell by far! Makes information that would otherwise be interesting drag on. Doesn't use any of her own material and it shows with her subpar knowledge on all subjects covered. She even gave an exam that wasn't even on the right chapters. She is careless, unprofessional, and truly terrible teacher.

She is a nice person but not fit at all to teach this course. She gave an exam that she just took from last year's class and realized halfway through the exam that almost half of the questions had not yet been covered in class. Material is not difficult at all but she is a boring lecturer who conducts the class as if it is a middle school class.

analysis.name: Muna Aghawani
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is so nice and helpful, made me interested in the Middle East, class was fun
Muna Aghawani is an awesome teacher with a beautiful soul.

analysis.name: David Agruss
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof Agrus is hands down the worst teacher I've ever had. I was a senior taking a lower level class and he tried to make the class as difficult as possible. He believes he is above everybody he doesn't even teach his own class he leaves that up to is students.He is so vague on what he wants, but then grades students as if they should read his mind
I am not an undergrad and I was actually taking this class because I wanted to.  And this guy managed to make me hate it. Avoid him. He is awful.
I leave class frustrated every day

analysis.name: Rachit Agarwal
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 33 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Computer networks class, wow! One of the best professors at Cornell CS. His lectures are amazingly well crafted. He starts with the most fundamental questions and builds the entire protocol around those questions. It is clear how much Prof. Agarwal cares about students and about teaching. One of the best classes in my four years at Cornell.

Prof. Agarwal is perhaps the best CS lecturer I've had here. He starts from the basics and then builds up to more complex topics, but always goes back to foundational ideas to bring everything full circle. He's also willing to help students anytime after class hours. Tests can be tricky, but go to class and a good chunk of the questions are gimmes

It was his first time teaching, and he did a great job in the lectures (some mistakes, but the lectures were very engaging). The class was unorganized, but he was very approachable, always ready to help and really cared about us students. There are very few other professors who stay in office until 11PM to help students and come back at 8AM.

I took his graduate class on big data systems in Spring 2017. I was one of the few undergrads in the class, so I had to catch up on the terminology. However, Prof. Agarwal was always available to help me catch up. He has truly interesting insights about the area and an in-depth knowledge about all the papers we discussed. I liked the class.
New Professor. I find his enthusiasm and knowledge to be awesome. Was very unorganized, and this undermined the learning of this class by a lot. WIll hopefully get better soon!

analysis.name: Ailong Ke
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Class was about 1/2 grad students and 1/2 undergrads (with easier grading for undergrads).  All there is to know about RNA!

Prof. Ke seems like a competent researcher and overall nice guy, but he's a terrible teacher. Lectures are dense and boring. The course was directionless and disorganized. Exams were essentially test of memorization, which seems like a waste of time in a course level this high. I regret taking his class.

This class has the potential to be interesting. But Prof. Ke is often late to class and presents the material in a difficult and convoluted manner. The homeworks involve using PYMOL to analyze various RNA structures. His exams are very long and difficult, testing obscure details he talked about in vague terms. He does curve final grade though.

Gives way too much information in each lecture. Lectures are monotone and dry. He is inaccessible outside of class and exams are very difficult. No additional resources are given to understand the material and the objectives are not made clear.

analysis.name: Aisha Gawad
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the most insightful, cool, and inspiring teachers I've ever had. Aisha went over the material in many different, interesting ways and made me very passionate about the subject. Probably the best class I've taken with the best prof.
She is a very nice and approachable teacher, always willing to reach out to students. She gave me a lot of good advice for my essays. She is not hard on grading. I did pretty well for this class.
Aisha is a brilliant writer and professor. She was inspiring and pushed her students to becoming so much better. Her class was enjoyable, interesting, and helpful.
She was helpful during office hours, and very nice, just a picky grader/wants things to be perfect.
Essays are impossible.  Had to rewrite papers three times and still got average grades.

analysis.name: AJ Edwards
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Tests are unlike homework or any random stuff he will talk about during his lectures. I think he means well he just has no clue in the world how to prepare his students for a test, be ready for the hardest topics in each chapter, get a grip.

Professor Edwards has a hard exterior but he really is a sweet man at his core. Go to his office hours; he will help you. He will make you "think," not just tell you the answer—he really does want you to grow as a learner. Protect this man at all costs.

For AEM5241- Edwards is one of the most impressive instructors you will encounter at Cornell. Incredibly knowledgeable, unparalleled experience, cares deeply about his students - it doesn't get much better than that. Not many top universities have former heads of their endowments taking the time out of their schedules to teach.

Easy to access outside of class during office hours. Class format is extremely test + hw based and lectures are a bit dry. Professor is super helpful, and if you do well on hw's you should be able to get a reasonable grade in the class. Exams are very similar to hw as far as question format, and the class is straightforward.
One of the worst professors of

analysis.name: Ifeoma Ajunwa
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She does a great job in leading really thought-provoking discussions and challenging our assumptions. I especially liked her efforts to help us apply business and legal concepts to real-world cases and her flexibility in adapting to the class's needs. She's a fair grader and gives clear feedback. Anyone who takes a class with her will learn a lot.

This was an excellent class. I truly enjoyed the class and found the material to be current. The prof. is extremely knowledgable in the subject matter. The couses leades to and to ann understanding ofthe changing nature of work as influenced both by technological advancements and changing attitudes about the role of work and work/life balance.

She is nice and willing to help students during and after the class. In the class, she will prompt you to think and discuss the real-life decision-making scenarios in a variety of ways. The reading materials, though heavy loading every week, also help a lot before in-class discussions. I am glad to have those articles and books finished.
Standout professor at Cornell. Challenges students in a thought-provoking and respectful way, and does not take sides -- instead kindly pushes all ideas. Unique teaching message. Strongly recommend.

Went from live to online. Taken for ILR soph writing requirement. Prof is nice and pretty knowledgable about her topics. She struggles to communicate how to do assignments and when they are due but doesn't grade harshly. You may have to ask her multiple times. Not too much work and it isn't too difficult or tedious. I think participation matters.

analysis.name: Alyosha Molnar
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: hard but rewarding. my favorite professor at Cornell University

Despite the overwhelming amount of work we were assigned, most of it was not worth much. It was a very leniently graded class and the professors and TAs spent well more than the required amount of time to ensure a high level of understanding.

One of the lowest grades I received in an ECE course at Cornell yet I felt like I learned so much more in this class then any other. Professor Molnar is truly a genius. His homecooked homework problems are very difficult, and the labs are harshly grades, but if you apply yourself through and don't worry about your grade here you will learn so much.

Prof. Molnar is many things, easy is not one of them. His problem sets/labs are positively sadistic, but he holds himself to the same high standards he holds you to. He is incredibly dedicated (teaches his own discussions, writes own labs) and is super-helpful when you talk to him in person. Overall, a great guy/teacher who will teach you  a ton.

Took 4450, 5540, and 5790 with Molnar. Hardest classes I took at Cornell, especially 5540, but learned more from them than pretty much everything else combined. Amazing lectures, you walk away from his classes with an understanding and not just a knowledge of the material. Best Prof. in ECE in my opinion. There is no learning without pain!!!

analysis.name: Alice McAdams
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor McAdams is very disrespectful and discouraging to students. She answers all questions in a snarky manner, states that any question that she does not want to answer is "stupid", and she insults students if they don't immediately understand a concept. She will destroy your interest in studying Spanish.

Really a great class. The professor is great and she really cares. She will introduce you to amazing new music shows movies and podcasts in Spanish. The class really improves ur Spanish with lots of speaking exercises as well as a lot of immersion with Spanish media.

Lecturer McAdams graded harshly regarding participation and often gave unclear instructions for exams and expected attendance during office hours to understand oftentimes necessary elements. She was very rude when interacting with various students which soured the learning experience. In the future, I hope she practices more empathy.
Really amazing teacher. One of the best I've had. You have to put in some work but really not that bad. Def recommend taking this class with Professor McAdams.

I absolutely loved having Professor McAdams as a teacher! Class didn't feel like a chore, and I definitely improved my Spanish skills though the course. Her lectures were very easy to understand and in class activities were fun, engaging, and helpful. She is very kind and clearly cares about all of her students and their success in the class.

analysis.name: Alex Esposito
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Esposito, or Alex as he prefers to be called is genuinely one of the best Professors I've taken in my college career. Doing research in his class was great and he is always down to talk about your work or just chat about what's going on in your life. If you get the opportunity take his class.
Professor Big Purr is very good at illegal screens
Applied Ethics FWS Fall 22. Amazing course with great in class discussions, a great, flexible, and chill guy.
Super chill, had applied ethics FWS!

analysis.name: Jomarie Alano
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Stay away from this professor and this class, offered as an intensive 4-credit history seminar over summer/winter sessions. Workload is impossible for this short time. Class is listed as virtual, but you will have exceptionally strict deadlines every single day, including weekends, with no forgiveness. Grading is fierce; supremely nitpicky. Avoid.

Dr. Alano is an excellent instructor who really teaches you how to write historical analysis. I had never taken a history class before, so this was definitely a bit tough for me, but with her feedback I was able to improve drastically throughout the course of the class. The class requires a lot of reading and writing, but the work is worth it.
Excellent prof! She is really responsive and helpful. You get plenty of time to do the assignments and essays with ample support

analysis.name: Allison Weiner Heinemann
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor Weiner Heinemann is easily one of the best professors at ILR. While her course is definitely not the easiest way to fulfill the sophomore writing requirement, it is definitely among the ones that will teach you the most. She will challenge you and you will leave her classroom a better writer. Needless to say, her grading is also very fair

Professor Weiner is an exceptional instructor. My writing skills improved dramatically from taking her writing seminar. She expects A LOT of work, but is very willing to offer help if you ask for it.I would recommend her class, but am willing to admit that it was not an easy way to fulfill the advanced writing requirement.

Professor Heinemann's course was excellent. What a warm person.

The workload's daunting, but she'd been forthcoming about expectations and afforded students endless opportunities to access help or supplemental materials. And notwithstanding this course occupying more time than all my others combined, Heinemann clears all other ILR professors.

This professor is one of the best I have had while in the ILR school at Cornell University! Though her paper assignments are difficult, she is extremely helpful and will go out of her way to assist you. My writing has improved exponentially and I am so glad I took this class.

Hands down one of the best professors that I have had at Cornell. She’s brilliant, funny, approachable, and (genuinely) interested in her students. She is always willing to go above and beyond to make sure that you understand the material.

analysis.name: Jeremy Allen
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 57 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: It is impossible to have an adult conversation with this professor. If you are looking for any guidance from Dr. Allen, I wouldn't. If you want to get on his good side, just laugh at his jokes (regardless if they're rude or inappropriate)  BUT don't laugh too hard or he might talk about you behind your back. Good luck.

Dr. Allen is an amazing professor. He takes the time to learn everyone's name and really cares about his students. The class had an annoying amount of work (especially in lab) but I learned a lot. I would say the class is mostly busywork and memorization more than anything. Also lots of opportunities to earn points outside of prelims

Dr. Allen is one of the best professors I have ever had. Not only is he passionate about the subjects he teaches, he is always trying to make sure his students understand the material and is more than happy to answer questions. His lectures are hilarious and he really cares about his students.

Great teacher and very funny, the perfect person to teach this class. Everything you need for the tests are on the slides, and there are so many TA office hours as well as his own that you can go to if you need help. If you do poorly on tests there are plenty of other assignments that can boost your grade. Study the slides and you'll be fine.

Amazing professor, made a pretty difficult class bearable. My grade was not because of the professor but my own fault, and if you put more time into learning the material than I did, you can get an A.

analysis.name: Amy Frith
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Frith is an amazing professor who genuinely cares about nutrition and her students. NS2060 is a great and low-stakes class. I really feel like I bonded with my GPHS cohort. I'm not sure that it was super helpful in finding my ELO, but I enjoyed this class and it's an easy A+. Your grade is just attendance, reflections, and a group project.

Dr. Green was one of my chillest professors this semester. The class within itself it's a chill class aimed for an easy A, so as long as you show up, participate, and work hard on the group projects and critical reflections your fine. She wants you to succeed, and its low stress in my opinion.

She is very nice and understanding and clearly cares about her work. The class was slightly unorganized but the workload was low and it was easy (mostly focused on career exploration and exploring global health to prepare for ELO). Not super useful but I enjoyed it.

This is the worst class I have taken at Cornell. The professor doesn't care about her students, the feedback on assignments is unhelpful, the class is completely unorganized, and the content is pointless. When talking to her one-on-one, her tone is very condescending. This class is a requirement for the Global Health major/minor, so beware...

analysis.name: Ti Alkire
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 24 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Great professor, really cares about his students and improving their writing. He's very passionate about semiotics but the topic can be difficult to understand. this class is less about understanding the topic and more about effectively writing. His critics are always helpful and he's truly made me a better writer.

Ti is an awesome professor who is flexible, caring, and fun. He meets students where they are, and is more than willing to help if you fall behind. Took ROMS 3210, History of Romance Language. He focused on us learning the material over simply getting the grade. You will do well in his course if you are present in lecture and doing your best work.
Such a sweet man- one of the most interesting classes I've taken at Cornell. The recorded speaking homework definitely piles up and was hard to find time to do in a quiet area, but great class!

The only reason students love Ti is because he tries so hard to be liked by them. Ti is your teacher if you are just trying to pass the language requirements cause he'll let you pass even if you do nothing.  He likes hanging out with the students who are brownosers.  He's a nice guy but not a very good teacher.  Plus, he has major mood swings.
Excellent professor! Great personality and makes the class comfortable. He is truly concerned with our academic success and interests.

analysis.name: Amy Newman
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really nice lady. All the mancom teachers are hard graders so it's understandable that she is also. Assignments are reasonable and applicable for the future. However, I felt her feedback was weak. Overall, it's a good class and I'd take her over Snow anyday.

Prof. Newman is calm and talks slowly. Sometimes she was not able to control the class. As each class of Cornell University worth a ticket of a Broadway show, Prof. Newman's classes obviously do not worth that price. For instance, she asked students to do mock interview one by one for 3 consecutive classes while she did not give much useful feedba

TERRIBLE! SOCIAL ENGINEERING CLASS! Newman feels Any challenges, failures, or misery non-white people face in life is not their fault. And, even worse, there is nothing they can do to change their circumstances. So, just stew in your bitterness and hatred for White males along with her and her like, so goes the leftist campus orthodoxy of the day!!
better than snow but still man com

Man Comm is Man Comm and she is better than snow. I thought she was pretty good for Man comm, she is a good mix between Snow and Arlisse/Dahl. You actually will learn something and she won't harass you like Snow.

analysis.name: Silvia Amigo-Silvestre
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 28 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: There are rarely instances where she is actually teaching spanish because the entire class is done in small groups. She cares more about if you do exactly what she says than if you are learning the language. It feels like there is an exam every day. Curriculum of SPAN 2090 is merciless, and she just makes it worse. Try to take it with someone else!

Literally the worst part of Cornell by far. Spanish has actually ruined my whole semester and, in running the Intermediate Spanish program, I can see that she clearly doesn't actually care about teaching. Tbh she seems more like the type of teacher that prides herself in making her class hard even if the material doesn't help us learn anything.

Her class was easily the most frustrating and terrible class I've ever taken.  She's nice, she's helpful, she's overly demanding and often shocked when students don't understand "easy" concepts.  I don't know how much of that is her fault and how much is how Cornell structured the course, but either way - you're in for a LOT of work.  Watch out.
Professor is a solid OK. Definitely not entertaining but bearable. A ton of busy work. Attendance is mandatory. Easy A, especially if you took AP Spanish.

Silvia was my favorite professor this semester. She's caring, reasonable, and highly encouraging. The class was definitely a lot of work at times, but it was always manageable and within reason-- your Spanish WILL improve. I initially took the class just to fulfill the language req, but now I've been inspired to continue Spanish throughout college.

analysis.name: Amy Williams
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Williams is very caring about every individual's success in her class. Assignments are challenging but doable if you put in the time and ask questions and they are very relevant to course material. Her lectures were often times not the most clear, but she does try her best to be engaging. Graded on five problem sets and one final project.

Prof Williams is a fantastic teacher who cares deeply about her students. She is passionate about the material and manages to take complicated machine learning algorithms and makes them manageable. Be warned that projects are always longer than you expect, though they tend to be graded nicely. She is extremely responsive and a great professor.
Passionate about computational biology, and tries her best to make lectures engaging. Cares about her students, and responds to both questions on Piazza or via email.

analysis.name: Mike Van Amburgh
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Great class and professor. He really helped me in my ability to see both sides of an issue. Teaching style is different than most other professors, but in a great way --makes you pay attention. He also expects input from his lecture students.

Lectures are really biased towards the meat industry.  It's an easy A, but the lectures all seem random and unfocused and skewed opinions are presented as fact.  It's not a requirement for the an sci major, so if you can possibly skip it I would-- I can't imagine much of a practical application for the information presented.
world renowned professor. Negative comments below are because people never bothered taking classes this man actually offered.
Mike is a tremendous leader in the dairy industry. Actually being able to know and work with him in classes is truly amazing.

This class has a lot of potential, but falls short. When Mike speaks, you can tell that he's both extremely intelligent and extremely biased towards dairy and Italy. You can't blame the guy since dairy is his life, but you can't help but wish he would try to show all sides of the arguments he presents.

analysis.name: Ana Howie
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ana Howie is one of the best lecturers I've had during my time at Cornell. She's extremely articulated and her classes combine insightful attention to details but also connecting it to breather topics and themes. She welcomes a wide range of discussion and interpretations as long as its materially and historically grounded.

Best professor at Cornell. She guides you through each lecture using the most relevant artworks. Each class is a masterpiece -- a very entertaining history lesson that is incredibly well-organized and carefully planned. I feel so much more cultured after taking her class. On top of that, she's the most eloquently spoken person I've ever met.

Prof. Howie is a good lecturer and very approachable. There are only a few graded elements in the class and she's a tough grader on papers, but she does use a UK grading scale so it's a little lenient in that respect. There's a lot of reading every night as well. Make sure to participate in class because it contributes to your grade.

The best Art History classes I have taken! Ana provides valuable context and great insights along with super detailed visual analysis of each artwork discussed in lecture. I've learned so much and deepened my understanding of eight early modern European countries/states. We did two essays, no graded homework, but the tests were challenging.

Her class on Early Modern Art called Cosmopolitanism and Empire was a pleasure. She delivers an old school "survey" art history class while keeping us in touch with the material culture and global impact at every turn. Seriously, we learned so much and the course work was lighter than expected for ARTH with this knowledgeable, graceful lecturer!

analysis.name: Andrea Simitch
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Her studio is extremely demanding. I often feel that my previous understanding of design is utterly shattered by her, and yes, those moments are frustrating. But I couldn't be more grateful for her pushing me out of my comfort zone and challenging me to be a better thinker and designer. If you're ever lucky enough to take her class, take it.
Genuinely doesn't know how to teach architecture at all

Best Cornell Arch professor I had. Definitely tough, but architecture school HAS to toughen you up. She had a clear agenda of what to teach even if some people don't realize it. She makes a difference in the Cornell education.
Professor Simitch is tough, but very good. in reviews, she provides very good constructive criticism. her advice is direct, which can be hard and frustrating at times but often very helpful.
AVOID AT ALL COSTS!! Incredibly disrespectful to students.  Talks down to everyone.  Thinks she is the queen of the world.  If you have any self respect do NOT take classes with her.

analysis.name: Andrew Campana
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Campana is maybe the best professor I've taken a class with here. He's a wonderful lecturer who's extremely passionate and knowledgable about the subject, and always makes really interesting points which challenge your thinking. His grading policy is very fair and not overwhelming, and he's kind to students and always willing to listen.

easily the kindest and most understanding professor i had this semester. professor campana doesn't cold call on students, so it's sometimes upsetting because it seems like no one cares. my only regret is not putting in more effort into the class. would definitely take another one of his courses just to have him as a professor again.

Easily one of the kindest and most genuine professors I've known at Cornell. When I needed an extension, he never said no. His courses were well organized, and his lectures were always a joy to attend. I think he legitimately loves what he does and wants to share it with his students. Often went to his office hours just to chat, which was fun.

You get what you put in with Professor Campana's classes, but the content he goes over in class is so interesting it's hard not to be invested. Great lectures, great organization of course content, would highly recommend if you are interested in hearing about games and animation as a reflection of japanese popular culture!

analysis.name: Catherine Appert
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Good professor, always enthusiastic, and very approachable.  Clearly enjoys the subject matter, and always responds quickly to questions and concerns.

Fun and interesting class; you'll learn a lot about African history and culture while doing some cool music workshops. Professor clearly enjoys teaching the course  and is always available to help you improve your papers and assignments.
