Manages to turn extremely interesting subjects into extremely boring academic droll. Class forces liberal media and ideas down your throat. Took it to learn about Tupac and Biggie, not trans rappers from Puerto Rico that no one knows about.
She was incredibly disorganized and difficult to understand.

Everything about her is unprofessional. She refuses to speak audibly, she gives no trust or respect to her students, and she scans material too quickly for comprehension. Her lectures are painfully tedious, and her essays are contorted and impossible. She is an atrocious grader, and doesn't mind failing everyone. Avoid this class at all costs!

analysis.name: Tatiyana Apanasovich
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Great class, the professor is very sweet and helpful. Learned a lot, would recommend  anyone who is interested in Data Mining to take it.

The moment the course moved into more conceptually difficult territory Apanasovich pretty much became incoherent. But she was still really helpful and often dedicated entire classes to explaining concepts about which students expressed confusion. It also doesn't hurt that she's extremely attractive...
The prof is very approachable and clearly understand the material. It really helped to be comfortable with asking you questions and talking to her.
fun ...not boring at all...the material is very interesting and cutting edge...and it helped me to get a job offer, very glad I took it
Professor Tanya is a very sweet, wholesome young woman. ... She is a great person and a good teacher...

analysis.name: Andrew Myers
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 42 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: I love the guy so much. He's a genius.  Don't mix up the courses with the teacher. Let's separate the two:  Professor Myers is: Well spoken, focuses on big picture, focuses on good programming, understands everything. Nice.  The classes he teaches are: honors/heavy duty CS courses at Cornell University. HW is 10+ a week. Lube is advised.

Lectures are amazing, as are the course notes. There are pop quizzes that count for credit, and they're often placed at kinda evil times (such as right after thanksgiving break this year). The homework is very hard, and takes more time than you'd expect. They teach a lot of content and they teach it very well.

Pretty awesome professor, gives great and comprehensive lectures. Homework can be long if you haven't done this before, but honestly pretty chill + easy if you have experience. Lots of room for your own direction, lots of fun.

Prof Myers is the GOAT! Clearly, he knows a lot about Java and programming logic as a whole, however, some of it may be outdated (one time he mentioned a "recent" java release and it was from 2014 😭). Lectures go by fast but not so much so that the content is overwhelming. If you're stuck on anything, office hours should be your #1 resource.
Very demanding and time-consuming assignments, but lectures were great, and the final project was extremely rewarding.

analysis.name: Alyssa Apsel
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: She's smart, no doubt about that...but she lacks coherence, organization and enough of an intuitive approach to teach the class...she expected students to know way too much; homeworks and test are long and difficult; labs are worthless and she's very inconsiderate of students' time; don't take it

Nice professor. She is really knowledgeable. She is helpful during office hours, but she asks you to think hard instead of giving you the answers straight out. Exams were tough, material is extremely tough. But a great class to take if you are interested in analog IC design.

Very difficult class.  Lecture consists of reciting the book... better off just reading it instead.  She has a very heartless and uncompassionate personality.  Final project expectations were ridiculous for time given to us.  I hate analog design now because of this class.

She's a smart woman, but she REALLY doesn't know how to teach. Her "lectures" are just straight out of the text. Homework takes too long, and class isn't exactly a walk in the park. She isn't that helpful either unless you hound her with questions.
Lectures weren't very stimulating. Basically followed the book word-for-word. Homework assignments and lab reports were a pain. As long as you study the material, the prelims aren't difficult.

analysis.name: Judith Appleton
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Basic Immunology is a demanding class:  meets for an hour and a half twice a week, and covers a lot of material.  Very interesting, though, and Dr. Appleton is a really excellent professor: fair, understanding, timely with posting material, while maintaining high standards.  Powerpoints are comprehensive study tools and expanded on in class.

This professor teaches Immunology.  I thought this class would be fun but unfortunately this professor is extremely boring.  It doesn't help that the class is at 8:30 in morning.  Also, the tests are ridiculously tricky for no reason and her lectures are sooooo long (about 38 slides each).  I suggest not taking this course.

very boring professor, very monotone. i thought this class would be fun and relatively easy, but it is not interesting at all. shes not a very good lecturer. basically reads right off slides, so going to class is not needed. tests are pretty fair though

There are no "tricks" to this class. The exams can be challenging on occasion, but if you can achieve a baseline understanding of the slides in her presentations you should be able to master the tests no problem, which doesn't seem like too much to ask from someone who goes to Cornell... Prof. Appleton is extremely nice and very helpful
this woman is not a very good teacher, all of her lectures are straight out of the book. will sometimes also test random bits of information too.

analysis.name: Chip Aquadro
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Chip is hands down one of the best profs I've ever had. Cares deeply about his students and the subject matter he teaches. Is there for you to help at any time, and exams are fair if you do the reading/pay attention in class. He goes out of his way to keep students engaged by doing DNA testing/bringing in guest lecturers. Really smart and kind.

great class. take-home prelims and a take-home final. exams require a decent amount of thought/time but chip and his TAs will help you in office hours! go to class and discussion and you'll be fine. wish i could take this again, was really interesting and not too too hard

The best prof I've ever had here. Some of the lectures are a bit boring but Chip does a great job connecting material in class to the real world! Is always available for help, is smart, and cares deeply about his students - always says hi, checks in, and even takes time at beginning of class to congratulate student-athletes in the course on wins.
Wish I could take this class again. Such a kind person and professor who cares about his students so much. Not too hard and grading is fair

The material is quantitative and kinda boring. HOWEVER, it has some extremely interesting applications. And Chip is a pretty good professor, always approachable with questions. He's really challanged me as a scientific thinker and I actually feel smarter thanks to his class.

analysis.name: Kathy  Arnink
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I really enjoyed Wines and Vines and would take another class with Kathy in the future, as she was friendly and willing to help her students. I learned a lot about enology and had the opportunity to sample a wide variety of wines from all over the world. Highly recommend this class, not sure what the other guy is going on about.
Professor Arnink is great. She is really well read in wines and a very interesting lecturer. Her classes are easy to pass if you care. I wish I could take more classes with her!
This class was the easiest A+ I've ever received.  There's so much extra credit available that if you show up, you will get at least an A. Definitely recommend!

A bit montone but still sweet and very knowledgeable. Lots of extra credit and quizzes were easy. The final essay was gradely very leniently. Quiz questions are a bit rough unless you take notes from the slides. Prelec recordings were a bit info heavy. Besides that really easy and straightforward class.

I mistakenly uploaded the wrong final file. Over break I sent an email, asking if the project despite missing revisions could be graded. She replied by pasting my words, said nothing could be changed, and signed off with a snarky “use this as a lesson,” which felt mocking and unhelpful. The tone left me discouraged. I could not recommend her.

analysis.name: Arthur Degaetano
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely adore Dr. D. He made the somewhat difficult thermo material understandable, was always available for extra office hours or answering questions. 2 prelims were not too hard, final was harder than anticipated but still very doable. Super kind and awesome. GOAT

The lectures for this class were not the most straight forward, but the in class assignments and homework helps to reinforce the material. The tests are fair and generously graded. He is very accessible outside of class and corrects mistakes very quickly.

Dr. D is a good professor all around. He is really kind and wants his students to succeed. If you need help, he will help, and his behavior will never dissuade you from going to lectures. My only issue is that the lectures were sometimes pretty confusing and required extra studying.
Oh, and there are 5 tests.  Luckily you can drop one.

Professor D was great. The online part of the class was convenient and the lectures were very engaging. If you have any background in any physics this class will likely be way too intro level for you, but sometimes you're looking for that easy class. 5 tests felt like a lot but you end up dropping one. Material is a bit random at times but is easy.

My favorite professor so far! Lectures were super clear and he was very encouraging and helpful to everyone in the class. He didn't have specific office hours but was really easy to schedule a meeting with outside class. Definitely make sure to pay attention in class because he may call on you to answer a question & you'll do better on the homework

analysis.name: Arnab Basu
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 42 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Basu is an absolute baller. My guy understands students' pain and actively tries to condense material to reduce frustration. He'll flat out cancel lectures if he or the class isn't feeling it. I loved his dry, deadpan lecture style. Literally just show up to class and you'll do well on the prelims. Quizzes were free and helped a ton on prelims.

Cracks a good joke once in a while, but otherwise monotonous. He's so relaxed about everything and does not gaf, which is exciting at first. However, the fact that he does not gaf will be reflected on your first prelim score. He overcomplicates the entire instruction of econ. Got a good grade but learned very little, which sums up his class.

Basu doesn't teach the best, even he acknowledges that, however: open book exams (canvas, lecture notes, notes),  ALL MCQ, weekly quizzes, 3 grading schemes to give you the best grade. Discussions were super helpful, lectures not so much. Compared to the other prof teaching econ, I'd take easy exams over teaching quality for this intro couse

By far the funniest professor I've had, half the time I was wondering if I signed up for a comedy show instead of office hours. Which he would just cancel if he didnt feel like it lol. Somehow managed to roast us, teach us, and make us think, all in one breath. Actually cared, helped, and made every lecture worth showing up for. I loved his class!!

teaches things that aren't on the internet, hard to understand lecture and lecture slides are super difficult to understand, I took macro in high school so I thought I'd be fine in this class regardless, but he teaches things you cant even Google. some prelim questions, even AI can't understand how I get them wrong and neither does the LSC center

analysis.name: Tomas Arias
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 58 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Arias is the best professor that I have had in my time at Cornell. He is kind, approachable, knowledgeable,  and an all-around great guy. When students voiced concerns, may it be with format or content, he listened and acted, at one point even starting to produce hour-long video lectures to help. He is amazing, and deserves high praise.

Arias is an amazing professor. If you ever have to take 2213, make sure he is teaching. Arias is so caring about students performance, and he is genuinely a nice guy. This is a really difficult course, especially if you struggled in multivariable like I did. The problem sets are hard, but they prepare you well for the exams which are pretty fair
Clearest professor I've ever had. Empathizes with what the student knows and doesn't on a intimate level. However, you'll have to do a lot of work for his class (if you want to do well, that is).

Prof. Arias is a good teacher.  I learned a lot through his 'flipped classroom' method of teaching.  I would highly recommend watching the video lectures at  a faster pace, as Arias speaks kind of slowly and pauses a lot.  You can take a 45 minute video lecture and turn it into a 12 minute lecture.  Major time saver. Definitely attend lecture.

He kind of reminds me of a dad. He was very approachable and encouraging during office hours. I started off struggling, but he was always willing to accommodate my learning style. Some people liked the flipped classroom method, and things really started to click once we switched to videos.

analysis.name: Art De Gaetano
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: So this is one is coming from a meteorology major. The man is in his element with the smaller classes, like EAS 341. He's a very nice guy, more than happy to help you out in class and in his office (he never has regular office hours). Likes to call on people at random though, and gets flustered when he loses his place in his notes.

He's a really great prof!! He wants students to do well - if you are struggling/need help/want something to be different, tell him. Super accessible outside of class. Make sure you find a friend to do the end-of-class quizzes with (it's allowed). He is so nice and enthusiastic. Applies concepts to specific examples really well.

Physical Meteorology is really tough, and your notes will just be derivations, and then derivations won't be on your test. His problem sets are tough, but there are only three.  He is very approachable and a very lenient grader, although his exams will make you (want to) cry.
The nicest man ever.  The tests are exactly from lecture notes, don't read the book.  Only about 6 homework assignments.  Lectures are interesting.  Would take this class several times over.

Okay class. DeGeatano clearly cares, but he's just a very poor communicator, and the class is not organized in a way that makes it engaging. Would be a lot better if it were split up into sections so that we can discuss rather than listen to lectures. Also, dense with material, yet geared toward non-science majors. Reinforced my dislike of physics.

analysis.name: Arnika Fuhrmann
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof Fuhrmann is a wonderful lecturer and is very knowledgeable and passionate in her field. The way this course was structured was quite interesting as a bulk of it were guest speakers within the Asian Studies department and I enjoyed learning the course materials through those different perspectives. The workload was normal and pretty manageable.

Prof Fuhrmann is extremely nice, and very accommodating with extensions. Always willing to talk with students after class. Extremely easy grader. Class median was an A+. I think I had a perfect 100 average in the class because she and her TA Anna, who was also great, gave me full marks on every assignment even when I thought they weren't so great.
There is a lot of reading. Prof. Fuhrmann is very accomodating if you have problems, though.

analysis.name: Adam Arcadi
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Great professor. You do actually have to study for the tests but they aren't too bad. He is a fantastic lecturer and I've never enjoyed a class that doesn't allow technology so much. When he talks about chimps there's an art of storytelling there and its quite enjoyable. I look forward to class every time I have it.

If you need a bio distribution requirement, especially as a humanities major, take this class! The readings are short and relatively interesting (not too much hard science) and they are not tested on the exam. While you do have to study for the exams, they are not particularly intense. The lectures are also engaging and easy to understand.

Super well organized. Took the summer course, asynchronous. Everything was meticulously organized and easily understandable. Grading was fair and content was actually super interesting. He touched on real world issues, beyond just facts of biology. Highly recommend.

I had professor Arcadi for a FWS that turned out to be all about canoes. He is a chill and nice guy, you will write 10 essays over the course of the semester but with a guaranteed A if you do the work and come to class! You will have a solid amount of work but I am glad I was in this class

I wouldn't recommend this class if you're merely trying to fulfill the A&S bio requirement. This is an amazing class if you have strong interest in the subject matter. His office hours are very helpful. However, do not expect an "easy A" out of this class -- the medians for the exams were quite low, hitting the C+ and B- range (without a curve).

analysis.name: N'Drie Assie-Lumumba
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Pretty easy grader for a good writer, BUT Make sure to ask her about her grading criteria at the beginning of the semester ESPECIALLY structure. Not very engaging as a lecturerer. Overall, she's not a bad professor and nice but I would only take her if you are interested in the subject matter.She gives 2 projects and 5 6-page papers.

Taken online. The lectures are honestly difficult to understand if you are not somewhat versed in the topic area already. However, there isn't any work except for the papers which aren't easy but graded very leniently and the average grade in the class was an A. Lots of discussion in class but you don't have to speak if you don't want to.

Lectures were often difficult to understand and follow. Regarding papers, she would often deduct points for errors and not explain how to correct them, even if you already tried to correct it. Grading criteria was not always easy to understand.

analysis.name: Ashim Datta
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: When I took his class, he failed to foster cohesion in our group. We eventually dropped the course because we couldn't build a rational connection with him. Although meetings were accessible, his feedback and understanding of our situation were consistently discouraging — not something I expect from a senior professor.

The GOAT. Best professor you will have in BEE. Really cares about his students. BEE 3500 is hard just by the nature of the material, but if you show up to class and his OH, everything will work out for you.

He cares about his students' learning but struggles to teach them effectively. The theory behind the course material is very relatable to real life, but the testing includes material that you may not cover in class. Attendance is graded, but you'd likely be better off (for your knowledge and achieved grade) learning the material outside of class.

Datta is a great professor who truly cares about his students and wants them to succeed. The class itself was really difficult but it was well supported by office hours and the TAs. He really emphasized problem solving and understanding the problem itself before beginning the actual solution.

He's welling to help and he wants to teach well for sure, you can tell from the enthusiasm.  However, some of the homeworks and prelim  problems are poorly worded/made.  It's just so hard to figure out what the question is asking or what the diagram is showing. You have to talk to him and after awhile you get it but there's no time on the test...

analysis.name: Ashley Edlund-Chescheir
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Took this class for a distribution requirement for Arts & Sciences. Would never recommend this professor to anyone. My participation grade was completely inaccurate and we were told final grades were "non-negotiable." Instructor made me feel extremely uncomfortable when discussing grades or anything regarding this class.

Super nice and caring prof, put in the work and show that you care and the prof will be super nice and helpful. Accessible outside of class & loves meeting with students to talk out ideas. A LOT of work and reading, the content is interesting but the workload is extreme for a 3cred course (many indicated this in eval's so workload may be decreased)
Did not enjoy this course at all. The professor assigns wayyy more work than any other 3 credit courses I have taken. Was a pretty inconsiderate professor, I don't recommend taking her course.

analysis.name: Astrid Van Oyen
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I took Roman Archaeology with prof Van Oyen fall 2021. It was my first archaeology class and was overall very interesting, very low stress, and she presented the material in a very organized manner. Honestly, this very chill and not hard at all, just make sure to write a good final paper and participate occasionally and you will do well.

Each lecture was based on a short reading from the night before of relatively few pages and a few short readings in class + discussion. The class essentially boils down to a final paper and some reading assignments.
Loved the class, Professor Van Oyen loves her work and makes learning about ancient economies very interesting

Astrid is amazing. This class is a seminar, lots of writing and reading, but overall a great class. It is not a show up and pass, but she does give good feedback on your essays. Complete the essays, be prepared for presentations, participate, you'll be good. Take this class, you may learn something about yourself too.

I had an awesome time in her Archeology of the Roman World class. The class was taught online, and it was clear that she had spent so much time organizing it to fit an online format. Awesome lectures and interesting content too
I took CLASS 2712 and thoroughly enjoyed it. Prof Van-Oyen is very knowledgeable, and the way she organizes the lessons and lectures is engaging and is undoubtedly conducive to learning.

analysis.name: Ashley Hand
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Ashley is one of the most caring profs at Cornell. Her prompts are broad, so you're really able to run with the assignments and do what you want with them to make it your best work. You'll get out of this class as much as you put into it. Ashley made me view literature in an entirely new and exciting way, and I'll forever have this class to thank.

She is so unbelievably kind, you can tell she actually cares about you as a person. I do find myself spending a lot of time on the assignments. The instructions can be sort of complex but she's a forgiving grader as long as you put in effort. It does take a while to get your papers back, but she gives great comments and is really encouraging.

Ashley is the best teacher Ive had at Cornell. Ill never look at reading the same way after taking her class. She starts from ground zero on how to analyze stories and then shows you how to use the various narrative elements as a springboard to write your own story. The only class this semester I actually enjoyed attending.

I absolutely loved coming to this class. Ashley is so caring and so funny. She makes you look at reading in a whole new way. She also teaches you actual strategies to get better at writing. The course isnt demanding per se but she makes you want to work hard and give her your best. I wish I could take it again!

Ive learned so much from this class. Ive liked reading and writing at a young age but at high school, English classes have been taught in a way that discourages that. Hand is caring and passionate about writing. Her class is a breath of fresh air. You will learn to become a much better writer in this class.

analysis.name: Asis Martinez-Jerez
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 26 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Asiss class is a struggle. Hes a very kind and fun-loving person but he is not an effective professor. Most of the material covered in exams are never gone over in class or homework. The exams always have low averages but he curves them so everyone can do decently. The lectures are hard to follow and youll have to teach yourself the material.

Asis is one of the sweetest professors at the hotel school and you can tell by how personal he makes lectures. He puts in the effort to know EVERY student and don't be surprised if he knows your name first day of class without introduction. The class is hard but put in the effort to do the problems before class and you will find it a lot easier.

You have to come to lecture prepared with the problems and cannot arrive late. He is strict, but he has his reasons and is very reasonable if you communicate with him. The tests are hard, but he curves them and if you redo the problems / go to OH you'll be fine. The median grade is an A- and the tests are super curved.

Asis is one of the best professors in the hotel school and he is incredibly fair. If you follow his instructions (i.e. participate in class and try the problems beforehand) you will easily do well. These bad reviews clearly come from people who wanted to get an A without putting in any effort. Also, he's super easy to reach if you have questions.

Verbally harasses students and pushed all exams together so we would receive your last exam grade right before the next exam. Impossible to learn and prepare yourself. He refuses to teach, and instead walks through problems while answering them completely different from the questions asks. Broad questions mean missed points and he hates critiques.

analysis.name: Ashutosh Saxena
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Most horrible teacher I've ever had. If you like a course, reconsider taking it if he's teaching.  He maybe nice to you, but when push comes to shove, he's a terrible human being from the soul.
The professor is really unhelpful and the assignments are way off the topics that are discussed during the class. Really unorganized class material.

Jesus tap dancing Christ! I can't agree more with the previous comment. This guy is the worst professor I have had so far by a long shot. Lectures are useless, he does not know how to explain the material. He uses another prof.s notes, no textbook. HW is a huge time drain. Not helpful at all.

The rant I just wrote for my course eval was double the size allowed here. Suffice to say Saxena is horrible. I had him for CS 6758 last semester and should have known better. Say away. 0/10 stars. Would not take again.
I was so interested in this course prior to attending Cornell.Sad that I would not be taking this course again. The classes were vague and the professor has problems in communicating.

analysis.name: Kevin Attell
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: KEVIN ATTELL MY GOAT. Take ENGL 1102. This class is so much fun. It's basically just reading books written by Cornellians and talking about them with the professor. KA has so much background knowledge on the books and authors and his interpretations of the books are always so enjoyable to learn about. Exams are light. Paper is light. 11/10.
I love him (I didn't read at all and never went to class, but I read SparkNotes before every test and that was enough!).
A passionate guy. Relaxed structure. Just do the reading (in 1102's case, read some fiction every night) and you'll be fine.

The lectures were minimally helpful, and Attell is at best a merely average reader of poetry. The main problem was his lack of enthusiasm. I was very excited about the course, but Attell did not make the experience worthwhile. The readings are hard (DON'T BUY the packet; ALL of it is free through the library or online) but the grading was very easy

Kevin was a great professor in Short Stories. He openly facilitated an environment where you could really explore your ideas and share them in discussion. I really enjoyed this class and the ability I had to expand my writing skills in a way that I had not had a chance to before. Would totally recommend. Also light homework.

analysis.name: Tom Avedisian
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Well organized blackboard lectures, and course content is paced really well (I can easily keep up with writting my notes, and he takes time to review concepts from the prereq classes when needed). Not too much homework outside of class, and homework¬es are very representative of content on exams. Definitely knows his stuff. Great class
Prof. Avedisian is really nice. Do your part; you get good grade.

Lectures were example based and not too difficult though presenting a more generic solution technique could have helped the clarity a bit. The textbook was only really useful for the summary formulas. Homeworks were fine, and the best part was that tests were open book/notes.

Avedisian is a really nice guy. He kind of has a grandfatherly air about him. But he expects a lot of heat transfer students- the material is definitely really hard, but he tries to make it make sense. The prelims were atrocious, though- study your butt off beforehand!

This was a great class mainly because of the professor.  Prof. Avedisian is a wonderful teacher. Clearly, he is smart and communicates difficult concepts easily.  Plus, he always showed a caring attitude.

analysis.name: Ariel Avgar
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Avgar cares more deeply for his students than virtually any professor I've had. He takes seriously the responsibility of teaching and is fully committed to teaching undergrads and grad students alike.
Ariel Avgar is an incredibly knowledgeable and engaging Professor. He is very helpful in office hours. Labor Relations can be dry at times but he made the class worth while. Overall great guy.
Cares about his students, but the class was often very boring.
One of the best professors at Cornell

analysis.name: Salman Avestimeh
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Absolutely no learning in class, lectures are same to same content from text book. Exams r hard. Tough grading policy , professor does not make clear his grading policy he will talk in terms of mean and median , normalization if yo ask for grading criteria and will make you confuse. Better not to take this class ever. Professor is in USC now.

Probability is a very hard course and he made it harder. Does not give grades in exams and sets them too hard. His grading scheme is also too hard to get past - he expects you to get 85+ to get a B, which is very challenging given the nature of the subject. Not friendly at all.  Do not study under him. It is a disaster.

You won't like probability at all after taking this class. You would not learn anything in lectures, lectures are dull and boring, lecture notes are from book only (same content). Exams are very hard . Professor shows no pity even though nothing of that sort is taught in class as he ask in exams. Recommend, to avoid this professor.

I love his lecture because he try to explain why you should learn concepts, properties, and theorems in the graduate students' point of view. Especially, he tried to make connection between probability and your research area, how to adopt what you learned from this course for your research. I got A, but I heard that he had tough grading policy.

analysis.name: Austin Bunn
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the best teachers I have ever had in my life, both at and outside of college. If you are interested in screenwriting or even just reading and analyzing screenplays, take this course. Amazing lectures that he puts a lot of effort into preparing, and he brings in great guests from the film world. Accessible outside of class as well.

Prof. Austin Bunn is one of the best professors at Cornell, Screen & Story is one of the best classes at Cornell, BUT he sometimes isn't that nice of a guy. He pokes fun at students in class and makes the class laugh at them. He will also mock your opinion if he disagrees with it. He's incredible at his job, but when the joke is you, it's not fun.

Intimidating but brilliant. workload is alright, BUT he holds the honor of being the first cornell prof who's ever denied me an extension (I'm a rising senior)... so don't expect a lot of flexibility.  still, I learned a LOT in Screenwriting and if you're lucky enough to get into the class, you're gonna get a lot out of it.
Great Professor! He always was bringing in cool people to help give us a sense of the field. Truly awesome class, would love to take another one like it in the future. 10/10

I took Intro to Dramatic Writing and can't wait to take Screenwriting with him next semester! Bunn is an amazing teacher, very accomplished, extremely talented, and incredibly funny! Our class became super close and I was actually extremely excited to start my hw when I returned to my dorm after class.

analysis.name: Natasha  Ayaz
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is an amazing professor who genuinely cares and respects each of her students! I enjoyed taking Creative Writing with her and our class discussions were always insightful and fun. She always encouraged us to share our own thoughts, ideas, and stories, and to know that our voice matters. I've learned so much more about writing, thanks to her!

This class was great! The structure of this class made it easy to balance with other courses, reading, writing short discussions posts, and writing essays. The short stories were enjoyable. You will not regret this class.

amazing teacher. made me feel really heard and included in class; i am personally not very strong in english, but she made me want to try and want to raise my hand in class. she makes sure that everyone is engaged in class (it is not difficult to be, at all!) and is super accessible & flexible in office hours (they were over zoom, by appointment).

Ms. Ayaz was a joy to have. Classes were very orderly and meaningful. She cared about us and even extended due dates when necessarily to accommodate the class. You will pass as long as we follow her reasonable grading criteria. Helped me develop as a writer. Always gave impactful and constructive feedback. Kind and always made me feel included.

Ms. Ayaz was an amazing professor. She was very kind and professional and understood that students have other commitments outside of class. The feedback that she provided for each essay was extremely thorough and she truly shows how much she cares for each student.

analysis.name: Yimon Aye
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: When I searched in google for one of the concepts, I realized that it was an unproven mechanisms published on NCBI that she wanted us to know. I feel like if I master all the mechanisms she wants me to know I will be able to synthesize a new cancer curing drug or something by myself.

Professor Aye is definitely a bright individual who should be teaching Organic Chemistry, but teaches this Intro to Organic Chemistry class as though we have already taken her section of the class twice. The class overall is test heavy and those are what determine your grade. Professor Aye makes Dr. Ruttledge's tests look like middle school quiz

Dr. Aye is the worst avoid at all costs, try to take Dr. Ruttledge his class is not easy by any means. But, Dr. Aye is not engaging, her test involve unnecessary reading that further complicates orgo. Her test are horrible, at least in Ruttledge's class you are clear on instructions even though the problem may be really hard.

Prof Aye is definitely one of the most intelligent professors that I have ever had, but she has a hard time slowing down and breaking the material down into helpful and informational pieces. She either teaches a concept in a way that is too simple or too complicated and then she tests on multiple concepts combined.

Professor Aye is really challenging, but her lectures weren't entirely bad. There's a bit of a language barrier and she tends to focus on drugs/biological applications. SHE WILL TEST YOU ON THEM. She only gives answers to in-class practice problems during lecture, so make sure you show up ready to write really fast. Tests are rough but interesting

analysis.name: Aziz Rana
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Rana is an amazing lecturer - he has no notes but still presents information in such an engaging way. Also makes an effort to be accommodating and makes it a point to learn everyone's name and asks how you are doing. Assignments are not too bad especially if you go to office hours. One of my favorite government classes at Cornell!

I've taken a few political theory courses before and none were as engaging and thought-provoking as Professor Rana's. He is an incredibly smart guy and makes the material so interesting! Essay prompts really make you think but they're fun. Attendance is mandatory, but I would've showed up to class even if it wasn't.

He is amazing! An exceptionally caring man and he brings that attitude with him to class every day. Papers and the exam are graded fairly. About 30 pages of reading each night which was not bad at all, and if you didn't actually read on time you were still in good shape. Definitely would recommend.

analysis.name: Iwan Azis
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Following his lecture is like watching thriller movie. You think you know something, but it turns out that you don't. He teaches us a theory (or model) and its strengths very clearly, and in another session, he will open up the theory's weaknesses, and ask us to be wise in using it. A highly recommended course.

Horrible lecturer. Inconsistent. His lecture is ok, but gudelines of the term paper are very unpredictable, which never showed up in the syllabus. Never plan ahead of writing a term paper, since he changes his plan very easily. Bad personality. Not helpful in office hour.

Got an A in his class and I still wouldn't take any other course with him. He is not a good professor and his concern for students is highly questioned. I wouldn't be surprised if the two previous comments were written by him since they seem identical.
Very kind and helpful, course was very useful for real world application
Bitter, unhelpful prof, a shame to the CRP department.

analysis.name: Sumanta Basu
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Great teacher, paced the class extremely well in a combination of in-class lectures and out of class Canvas modules. There is a participation grade with occasional in class/in lab polls, but is explained clearly in the syllabus. As someone who took Stats during high school, Professor Basu brought back familiar material in an easy to understand way.

King. He is so funny, caring, and just a great professor. His lectures can be a little boring if you're not interested in data science, but he finds ways to keep you engaged. The quizzes and homework were easy and the group project would have been if I had better partners (choose wisely). I looked forward to going to lecture every day.

Professor Basu is funny and caring and makes an intro STATS class easy! He teaches in R, and assumes you do not have experience. The class contains 3 prelims, a final group project, weekly homeworks, and preparation videos. The work is very doable, and there are a lot of resources if you need help! A great professor, and even better person!

Great guy, decent class. Basu tells good, interesting, content-relevant stories during lecture. Homework and tests tended to differ a fair amount, but he is explicit about what you need to know for exams. He's fairly receptive to student concerns, this is a good class to take.
Really charismatic and extremely competent in complex mathematics.

analysis.name: Christopher Batten
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Batten is simply one of the best professors at Cornell. He is well prepared before every lecture and explains concepts very well. Video-notes are still very helpful. Homework is  long and difficult but you can learn a lot. Project can be challenging, but Batten will help you find your bug. He is as dedicated a professor as they come.

Presents course material in a very organized way so that it builds on itself. Exams were reasonable, labs were interesting, and provided a good introduction to digital logic. Recorded lectures helped with exam reviews. Used Ed discussion actively to answer questions. Prof. Batten was a great lecturer, and I would take another class with him again.

GOAT of the ECE department. Still developing the course and it changes a lot year to year, but best lectures in the ECE department and really fair exams. Labs are a decent amount of work but interesting enough.

Hands down one of the best professors in the ECE dept. Class is challenging but the effort from the prof to help out, in class, in his office or on the online forums makes you an expert in his subject. Explanations and notes are clear and always encourages constructive discussions! Labs are hard and do require time, but you'll be proud of the work!

Batten is the GOAT. He clearly cares about his students, helps out during lab sections, is a fair grader, answers ed discussion posts 24/7, and most importantly is an engaging + comprehensive lecturer.

analysis.name: Banu Griffin
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is a really good and understanding teacher. Usually a very small class (mine only had 4 people). One of the easiest languages in the school... highly recommend.

analysis.name: Rosemary Avery
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 75 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: The GPP was the worst assignment I've ever done, especially since a member of my 3-person group did nothing. Prof. Avery should have mid-semester group evals to weed out free riders. Lectures were dry and hard to follow, prelims were absurdly difficult and she took off points for not mentioning things that were not asked as part of the question.

Useless readings. Killer prelims, b/c there's so much material & b/c the way she words questions makes it hard to know what she wants. No final, but a group project. Thinks her class is the most important & schedules mandatory events over ur other classes. Hardest class I've taken in two years. Lectures are 60+ slides long & convoluted. Great TAs.

She's nice, but her class is the worst I've ever taken. Material isn't difficult at all, but there's an obscene amount of memorization. Prelims are killerher questions are poorly worded & ambiguous and you get points off for not phrasing things exactly how she wants, and for not including things she didn't even ask for. 0/10 wouldn't recommend.

The actual material for this class isn't particularly difficult, but the way that it is presented in lecture is confusing and hard to follow. The group project took up a ridiculous amount of my time (easily 80+ hours over the course of 2 months) and the prelims were hard- average raw score was a D for both of them, but they're scaled to a B.

This class was not fun. I am definitely switching majors next semester. I don't like the way this class is run, really encourages students to kiss up to her to try to be come a ta or research assistant. Feels more connection based than merit. Most of the content from lectures was useless. she made a point to take attendance after a break or project

analysis.name: Kaushik Basu
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 37 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Writes down the wrong numbers so often that the students have to correct whatever he writes on the board. Notes are unorganized and does not a good job explaining the concepts. Takes forever to post the solutions to the problem sets.

Disregard the comments pertaining to the intro econ classes... that is a different Basu. Very nice guy and most engaging lecturer I've ever had, but in-class examples are often filled with errors and exams are wayy different from problem sets. Highly recommend taking this course S/U

Really disorganized in the beginning ... engaging lecturer. However, the grading for exams is brutal as it is either right or wrong. If the algebra is wrong, but the right steps are used ... it is wrong. Answer keys don't show steps. Final is 60% so watch out for that which is atypical for the states. I would recommend taking this pass/fail

Super interesting class. Prof Basu is very knowledgeable in this field and he is very passionate about teaching it to others. The problem sets are optional are but definitely helpful for preparing for tests. The TAs were very nice too.
Great lecturer, makes the material very interesting.  A bit unorganized in his lectures.  Difficult but fair tests.  Brilliant guy.

analysis.name: Benjamin Dozier
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Dozier is a very easy to understand lecturer with neat chalkboard notes. The lectures aren't the most exciting, but I recommend going anyway because by paying attention and taking notes, you'll save yourself a lot of time on the HW. 1920 is a hard class with hard exams, but if you do the HW, you will be pretty well prepared for the tests.

Despite his approach leaning more formal and conceptual, lectures are full of valuable information and problem-solving frameworks/workflows, and clear to follow as long as you concentrate in class. Prof does a great job giving you physical intuition for concepts and is open to questions. The course textbook is great as a supplementary resource.

Dozier is a pretty good professor. Teaches almost directly from the textbook, but nothing negative other than the fact that lectures can get dry and the lack of slides. However, he is very articulate and attempts to make the class fun. 1920 is naturally a very difficult class so make sure to be on top of things.
Lectures were boring and followed the textbook almost exactly.

Note: I took multi at an extension school and retook it.
Lectures uninteresting and exactly follows textbook examples instead of his own material. HW not bad, but office hours necessary and carried by goat TA. Prelims + tests have some tricky problems, but overall fair if you study well. Didn't provide practice for prelims, but did so for final.

analysis.name: Michela Baraldi
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: She's so nice and made an effort to get to know everyone in the class and encourage participation. Students aren't penalized for mistakes and she's a very lenient grader, so long as there's effort made.
she's literally so nice, best italian professor. i would 100% take this class again.

as someone who was unsure of whether i wanted to continue italian, michela was the reason i decided to. she made me love the language through sheer personality and warmth. one of those once in a lifetime teachers you'll think about for years to come.
Prof. Baraldi is incredibly kind as a person and as a teacher. The class is super relaxed, and she is the best language teacher I've ever had!
Extremely clear and helpful. Tests do test the material well but are not unduly hard. She is very flexible with due dates and is always a pleasure to be in class with. Truly great professor

analysis.name: Michele Belot
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: grading wasn't very clear. the class consists of lectures with in-class mini quizzes and group projects and a final solo project. it seems like they are trying to implement a curve on this class now. my progress in the class was on track for an a and then the final grade was decided by some arbitrary process (lower than it should be)

One of the worst classes offered at Cornell. The grading criteria on her stuff makes no sense and they try really hard to force a distribution in the class. The TAs are not helpful and the professor isn't great I honestly learned nothing all class. It's one of those classes you take thinking it's gonna be easy and it ends up being a ton of work

Professor Belot is not a great instructor. Unclear grading criteria. Constantly forces us to put away laptops mid class. Attendance mandatory to do daily quizzes. Intro-level economic concepts, learned nothing new. Imposed a negative curve for the final grade (70% of my average was an A-, but got a B+ in the class). DO NOT TAKE! NOT WORTH IT!

DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS (2475). She makes attendance mandatory and there are quizzes during each lecture. Grading criteria is super unclear, I didn't receive above an a- on a single group project and some even got c's. I got an A on the final and ended up with a B.

This course is very easy from an economics perspective. The professor obviously cares about the material, but since lectures and assignments are not related, students do not pay attention. Would recommend as an econ elective, but course got bumped down to a 2000-level. Easy, chill professor and chill class where most students get As. Little work.

analysis.name: Benjamin Leyden
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Don't be deceived by those ppl who give 5 - the average for this course is B+. Don't let this course ruin ur life. Spent a substantial amount of time on this course, but the grading criteria were unclear, making it hard to understand expectations. The final grade is disproportionate to the work required.

Underrated prof. Lectures are very clear and easy to understand. Attendance is optional but make sure to go because there are so many concepts that make sense once he explains them in class. Definitely the harder man econ professor but there is a generous curve applied at the end of the course. Dedicate ample time and effort into psets and studying

Leyden was a great professor. His lectures were clear and engaging, and he was very easy to reach outside of class. TAs had office hours frequently and were very helpful for problem sets. The exams were difficult but fair, and going to all lectures and reviewing problem sets helped. Leyden is also very understanding and clarifies any confusion.

Most students who do not do well typically don't show up to class. The prelims can be difficult, but the class is curved. Also, the prelims have parts that are very similar to some problem-set questions. If you show up to class, do the problem sets, and study or go to review sessions, then it is reasonable to get an A/A-

One of the worst professors in Dyson. Leyden tries as hard as possible to make his students fail; incorrect/vague lectures, dry, and (very) arbitrary grading. Lectures are useless and mostly irrelevant to exams. Going to TA OHs and learning concepts on your own is enough to get an A. Huge mistake to take his class over Terry's, who is much better!

analysis.name: Carl Bernard
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 27 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Great lecturer, but not a great professor. The homeworks are intense, but the exams are worse. He purposefully makes the median low. For my second prelim, the median was a 46%. A typical math course should have a median of B- or B, but he makes his a C+. Please, do not take this class with him. But if you do, prepare for the worst summer ever.

Ehhhh, didn't get an A+, so I guess that class was a little hard. One of the more easier classes here at Cornell ECE. Prelim avgs were like 50%, but I never studied and still averaged 80%. Carl is a good lecturer and funny at some moments, but he teaches a lot in a single week, especially towards the end, so be ready.

Labs are based on faulty equipment which literally prevent you from completing them on time. Tests are horrible . Homework takes forever : course is genuine hell makes passionate ece students want to never see anything circuit related again - but hey Cornell at least you torture your engineers so they can be good in the workforce !!!

Dr. Bernard is a good lecturer who cares about his students performance. His office hours were extremely helpful in understanding class material. He is also a genuinely nice individual who helped break down complicated topics into digestible pieces. I would highly recommend taking classes with him.

Overall, Carl's lectures are chill. However, the past exams he released before prelims are ALL extremely easier than the actual ones. He could literally throw out brand new concepts at the exams and assume the students could understand. This class is useful, but it is really exhausting.

analysis.name: Tapomayukh Bhattacharjee
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
Worked so hard for this class only for his grade breakdown to required a 97 for... not an A+, no, but a regular old A.  

I used no AI, I slaved away over this class, and then I get an A- because too many people did well.

If this grading scheme was made clear to me in the beginning, then I would have not taken this class. 

96 = A-

Amazing lectures with clear explanation. Very caring and approachable professor. High scoring class. The class has become very popular over the years. This fall has around 225 students. I highly recommend this course if you want to get started with robotics.
Do not take his foundations of robotics class...

analysis.name: Benjamin Soltoff
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 48 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment:
let me be clear
1. INSANE WORKLOAD (hw + lab + project + ae + exam + extra credit... should be 8+ credit course)
2. AMBIGUOUS grading criteria (most TAs don't know what they mean either
3. 50% OF WORK COMPLETELY OUT OF CLASS MATERIAL 
4. DISORGANIZED PROFESSOR (speak too fast with no meaningful info)
5. 0 HELP FROM EXAM ("just review the material")

Grading is extremely unfair & TA's are not helpful when you go to office hours. Rubric is completely made up at the end & they will dock huge amounts of points for things they never told you to do. The exams are also difficult & the medians are extremely low. Professor admits he's doing this for a paycheck & doesn't even like teaching the class.

I read the reviews, it was worse than they said. Professor was awful, bad teacher, demeaning to wrong answers, something due almost everyday, grades extremely hard. First time there were in person exams and the highest grade was a B, he then disabled grade calculation and blamed us. STAY AWAY, just take it in the fall with the other teacher.

The fact that this guy has a job—at Cornell or otherwise—is one of the great mysteries of the universe. He has no redeeming qualities—he is below par in every metric as a professor and as a human being. I believe the sole purpose he was hired was to demoralize enough students to debloat the InfoSci major.

Benjamin is a nice person. The PPT of the course is detailed, but has HEAVY homework that must take the time to fix all details well【many little points for deduction】All his big exams, 2 midterms & final exam were F**king DIFFICULT. He will give a curve, before curve, scores may be less than 1/2, or even 1/3. RUN AWAY PLZ.

analysis.name: Sunil Bhave
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the best professors that I have had! Full points.. HWs for half the course are constructive and help us understand the material better.. and an end term project for the remaining half helps us do something of our own interest. If you plan to take ECE 432 take it under him.
Seems like prof didn't care about the class too much.  He taught from PowerPoint, which made class extremely boring.  Also didn't cover all class material--didn't even touch antennas!
Whether you like him or not, you can't deny the fact that he is so smart! His lectures are pretty inspiring as well.
Wow, I couldn't disagree with the first two more!  This class teaches nothing!
Clarity of thoughts and good teaching skills makes him my favorite prof

analysis.name: Bill Miller
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: His class is relatively easy. Participation in class lectures is graded as well as in labs (mostly field trips to surroudning ag facilities). Super chill class the exams are not hard at all as long as you study. He might not be the best at responding on emails but he genuinely cares about his student's success.

Good professor but his tests are hella difficult and u'll probably fail if you do not have prerequisite knowledge on plant sciences. His tests are super specific don't listen to him when he says that it will cover the exam broadly...he will NOT. The only good thing is that tests do not make up the majority of the grades.

Dr. Miller is a super kind, knowledgeable, and funny professor. He is very clear in outlining the expectations for grading criteria in the course, and gives ample time for assignment submissions. Class is entertaining, informative, and interactive; a great course for anybody with interest in plants, regardless of your major. He's a great professor!

Dr. Miller is incredibly respectful and kind. Although the 1101 class is mandatory for many of the people taking it, I'd still highly recommend it, or any other taught by him. Dr. Miller works hard to accommodate the needs of his students. Overall, Dr. Miller is a very funny and relatable guy who made learning enjoyable.

Dr. Miller is incredibly kind, caring, and intelligent. All the material in his class (PLSCI1101) is presented clearly, and as long as you attend lectures, you will do great in this course. I would recommend anyone to take this class (as long as Dr. Miller is teaching it)--even if you have zero interest in plant. He is a wonderful professor!

analysis.name: Bharath Hariharan
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Engaging content in the lectures. Overall a fun course. Was pretty chill, we were allowed cheat sheets for exams. People in previous years seemed to give negative reviews, but they likely did not go to class/did not pay attention. Asking questions during lectures will help you understand a lot, and the professor gives pretty detailed responses.

He really stepped it up since last spring because most (all?) of the issues are irrelevant now. Clear grading, fair exams, strong support system in OH and after lectures, assignments are well spread out throughout the semester. The syllabus is also refreshed now to include more recent topics like NeRFs, transformers, semantic segmentation, etc.

This isn't the worst course I've ever taken, but it's definitely up there. The material is interesting, but the course is so disorganized that it makes it hard to care about the material. Almost all of our assignments were shoved into the last month of the course. Lectures are boring, slide decks are not organized, and staff support is non-existent

Seriously disorganized class. Gives us about 2 assignments in the first 90% of the semester and then drops 4 towards the last 2 weeks of class. Review material has no answers, frequently canceled office hours, and no help on ed discussion.  Terrible grading scale as well which was final despite half the class doing not as well as he expected.

Lectures are well planned, and most of the learning is visual. Lecture notes are enough for a good understanding of the material and, most of the homework can be solved by just relying on it. Since the course projects are research-oriented, he grades them by looking at the amount of effort you put in and what you have learned from it.

analysis.name: Dina Bishara
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: My favorite part of ICL she felt the most enthusiastic about her section. Also probably the easiest test but even ignoring that, she's still my fav. The way she broke down the section felt the most informative about her given area compared to other sections and lectures felt less monotonous

The topic fluctuates between being interesting and boring. Reading is required to some extent, but you don't need to spend hours on it. There's a solid amount of writing on it, and she's not the easiest grader, but she is fair, provides thorough feedback, and typically allows you to submit early to get feedback before turning it in. Compassionate.

Professor Bishara is an extremely nice person, very knowledgeable, caring, and understanding. This review is more about the class itself. It is very boring but extremely easy. You don't need to pay attention in class but she does take attendance. As long as you know how to read and write 2-3 page essays every once in a while, you'll do fine.

Professor Bishara can be a bit shy, but she really cares about her students and gives a variety of assignments to complete. She is a fair grader and has a genuine interest in her field. She also asks the class for feedback about assignments and projects so she can adjust due dates and make changes for future classes. I highly recommend her!

Prof Bishara is the nicest person and has such a genuine interest in her topic (middle eastern revolutions) you can't help but get excited as well. Can be shy but if you talk to her during an office hours she loves helping and will tell you her experiences and will give you abundant resources to further examine.

analysis.name: Birgit Speh
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: She is a nice person and helpful when approached, but her lecturing style is just terrible... it basically consists of writing some stuff on the blackboard, stating some claim that seems obvious to her at least, and waiting ages in silence for us to get it on our own. Glad she's not the DUS anymore.

One of my favorite professors I've had at Cornell. Math and Politics is a great class, and she taught it well. Prelims were fair and had an easy final project instead of an exam. She will sometimes wait to get to the same number of votes every time for every iClicker question, which can get annoying. Knowledgeable about game theory altogether.

Absolutely awful. 10 minute quizzes that are worth 5% of your grade each that are hyper specific to the textbook. No drops. Lectures are very slow. Sadistic grading on exams, youll lose 10% for a mistake on a question, does not matter how big or small, minimal partial credit. Horrible communication, gives no information about means/medians.

She's a nice person but just not good at teaching... Her lecture is not well organized and her notes are very unclear. She seems to be a happy, helpful person and she tries...  I found the lectures not helpful at all and just learned most of material from the textbook. The book is very good and cover all the material fairly clearly.

Worst class every. Course not curved. Not given a single point to extra points problems, even if you did all of them and received full credits. No response in email and failed to answer students questions during classes. Told you quiz tested on a certain chapters, but actually tested on different chapters.

analysis.name: Ken Birman
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 48 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: The professor pretends to be approachable but he is actually not. If you ask questions that are not appropriate in his mind, he will rudely interrupt you and ask you to go back to review the material. Once, I was actually teased for being "poor" in English because I didn't understand his explanations... Well done!

Lectures are dry, hard to follow, and riddled with random tangents unrelated to the course. It seemed like Ken really didn't care much about the class and didn't enjoy teaching it. It felt like he wasn't invested in our learning and was just waiting for the semester to be over from the first day of class. He was a very poor professor overall.

The worst professor I have ever met. Not friendly helping students,  but only expect students to find solution all by themselves even suggesting you to read tough paper or try yourself for over 10 hours instead of giving you a hint or answer in 10 seconds.   Poor professor.

Without hyperbole, the worst class in the CS department. Dry lectures, devoid of content, full of meandering anecdotes that didn't go anywhere, content at least 15 years out of date, half the semester wasted on unrelated material, and a staggering number of just factually false statements made by the professor in lecture.

Thank god he is leaving Cornell. When he made his post on LinkedIn annoncing his leave, he ranted at the fact that only 60 out of 375 students showed up to lecture. Is it more likely that 315 students are the problem, or the common denominator, 1 professor?

analysis.name: Eilyan Bitar
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Took in Spring 2016 - his first year teaching ECE 3100. I thought he was awesome, can be really blunt/quick at times, but will help you understand it esp 1-on-1. Good lectures. I had no idea what probability was before class, but left with a solid foundational knowledge that has been really helpful in later classes (ML, robotics, etc.).

Overall nice professor, very accessible outside of class and helpful. Cares a lot about his students and offers opportunities to improve grade. Material is difficult. Organization could be better - the timeline for the class in terms of homeworks and grades were a little confusing but other than that, I thought he was a great professor.
Had no idea about optimization before this class. But I learned a ton and it has been really useful for upper div ML and data science.

Bitar's classes are super engaging because he uses the chalkboard for all his lectures. No PowerPoint slides at all! It's a nice change, but you definitely need to be on top of your note-taking game. The class is tough, with a lot of proofs, but he's really patient and explains things in a way that actually makes sense. Just be ready to work hard!
He's gives great lectures and is helpful in office hours too. Just make sure you start homework early and don't wait till the last minute. Prelim and final were fair.

analysis.name: Adrienne Bitar
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She assigns a lot of readings and writing assignments, but they're mostly short and also very interesting. She tries to make the class fun, and you can tell she is extremely passionate about the material and making a difference. She is very invested in her students and cares more about what you learn than about how well you write.

I really enjoyed this course with professor Bitar and it made me interested in pursuing the American Studies minor. The class had a decent amount of readings that were all very accessible and posted on Canvas in a reasonable time frame. Additionally, the TA Patrick was also great and he was very educated and knowledgeable about the course. Easy A!

Dr. Bitar is really a great professor. She cares about her students and isn't a stickler over little things. In addition, you can get a lot out her classes. I'm not the most "school-person' by any means, but she can you to learn something. Overall, great experience

This class is literally "food studies." There is absolutely no reason why this class (essentially an elective course) should have mandatory attendance 3 days/week and a 13-17 page final paper. This class is not important enough to be taking up that much time and commitment out of any student's week and semester. Not sure what Bitar was thinking.

Bitar is the best professor I've had at Cornell. Extremely passionate about her topic, gives interesting readings (although long), and engages with her students. You're given a lot of freedom to study what you want for the final paper. Too many group tasks for my liking, but overall, the lectures were really incredible.

analysis.name: Kendra Bischoff
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: The kind of professor that will learn your name if you speak up often, even in a huge class. She is an engaging lecturer and she explains all of the reading in a very comprehensible way. She gives lots of extra credit opportunities and doesn't give homework or have class the week of a prelim. Overall, a very pleasant person to take a class from.

Bischoff is a professor. She's energetic, but that doesn't mean anything, since everything you learn is based on the readings. Lecture is useless. Section is useless. Her grading is terrible. In her own words, "Getting the question right only means half credit. You need to really go beyond the question." Not even 2 pages on one question is an A.

She's young and has fresh energy which isn't too common in the soc. department. She has good research credentials and assigns informative readings although some might be a bit advanced if you've never had soc before. You will NEED to study for the exams thoroughly but you can really take a lot from her courses. Speak up in class, too!

I don't know how she is in other courses, but she shined in Schooling & Society. Readings were perfectly chosen and effective. Class discussion was engaging, never boring. Essays were honestly easy imo; you just had to engage with the material and organize your thoughts. Definitely a doable amount of work. Made me love education sociology!!

She is nice but not a great professor. Nearly half of your grade 45% will be quizzes based on long readings. It was hard to remember the details of the readings for the quizzes especially because she does not go into depth with them in lecture. She IS going to test you on very specific information that she does not include in lectures. unfair imo

analysis.name: Kristina Blake-Hodek
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Material was clearly explained during lectures, then effectively reinforced during labs. Treat the Lab manual like the holy grail when it comes to understanding the crosses. Listen to the TA's and learn how to accurately draw the crosses because that skill will earn you 20% on every exam.

Make sure to attend lectures for the exams. Dr. Blake is one of the most caring people on this campus, and even though the material can be tough, I really appreciate that the grade for this lab course is independent of how successful your experiment was.

Normally I would not grade this professor as low as I did. I feel like it is not only this professors fault but the class is organized in a way that is not conducive to learning. She is passive aggressive both in class and out of class, and the lectures are often incredibly simple which then is frustrating when it makes a sudden leap in prelims.

***This is for BIOMG 2800, NOT the lab class 2801. Dr. Blake is an amazing professor. Genetics is tough but her lectures are so clear and make difficult concepts easy to understand. Go to study sessions to help understand things better and go to office hours when you are struggling. It is very possible to do well in this class.

I've literally never had a better professor. So clear and concise during lecture and explains genetics in a way that's so easy to understand. Extremely approachable after class and gives very fair exams in my opinion. If you go to class, you 100% should be fine. The TAs are also super helpful with quizzes and any questions on lab stuff.

analysis.name: Bill Schulze
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data
