Would highly recommend taking any class with Professor Verhoeven, it is clear she knows what she is talking about, and always came to class well-prepared and ready to give a great lecture.
Loved the class, loved the professor. The material is interesting and her essay feedback was helpful. Also great to just talk with about anything, not just class material.

Good professor and gives good feedback on the writings. the lectures were interesting but can be a little repetitive at times but i am excited to take her class on the manson case next semester. her essay feedbacks were great but a little hard to understand.

analysis.name: Thomas Cleland
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: If you're looking for an easy stats class to fill your distribution reqs look no further. Yes it's not very interesting or exciting but I don't really think that's his fault. He is an incredibly flexible professor, records all lectures. Attendance is never mandatory I probably only went to about half of them and have an A. And you will learn stats!

This class put me to sleep. Prof was very nice, but the content was boring and lectures were long, he spoke quietly, and sometimes his writing was impossible to read. He puts recordings of the lectures in canvas, which is super nice. Assignments are quite long, and there are weekly quizzes. Grading took a very long time. I did learn stats though.

He is my favorite, super understanding guy. I love him. The course itself is digestible even if you have no knowledge in statistics. He really loves the subject and you can tell. The class is not curved at all but there are a lot of assignments and easy points, so it's not hard to do well. You get cheat sheets on the exam and they're fair exams.

The professor teaches the content with enthusiasm, which makes the class more engaging. Getting cheat sheets is a huge plus for exams + finals, and with enough time and effort, all content is understandable, just a bit time-consuming.

He would definitely be better at lecturing to higher-level students, but the actual content is very understandable and it is very easy to succeed in this class. You get to have cheat sheet(s) on every exam and there are a lot of different components that go into your final grade. It can sometimes be time consuming but is pretty straightforward.

analysis.name: Todd Clary
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I had professor Clary for SANKS 1131 and 1132 and is amazing at linguistics and the Sanskrit language. He occasionally tends to say a lot of linguistic jargon but encourages students to be participative, ask lots of questions, and is genuinely willing to spend time and support you.

He's a super cool dude. Very passionate. Extremely knowledgeable. Often there are readings and you should attend class (slides don't have to much info; he speaks a lot). Lec/OH help before exams. Exams are pretty easy (short response) and workload is not horrible especially if you like to read. Not a humanities person but the class was interesting.

Prof. Clary is a very kind, positive, and interesting professor. His clear passion for the subjects he teaches translates to engaging lectures and an approachable attitude. His upbeat teaching style makes even an ancient language relevant and captivating for students. If you engage with the material, you will do great.

Clary is a super chill professor and teaches several languages in Classics. He is very knowledgeable, and his light-hearted demeanor (can't forget the jokes and ancient Greek stories) makes learning a tough language a little more palatable.

Professor Clary at Cornell was exceptionally caring, prioritizing our comprehension over grades. His engaging lectures were complemented by his thoughtful gesture of sharing homegrown vegetables, creating an enjoyable learning environment.

analysis.name: Claire Cardie
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: So disorganized, unclear lectures

This class was very interesting, and the programming assignments, while long, were excellent. Professor Cardie was frequently quite unclear with concepts, and Piazza was very seldom used by the instructors, so it would take a long time to get what was quite often a less-than-helpful response. Assignments were graded easily, but the final was tough.

Lectures can be a little dry (and she sometimes seems a bit nervous?) but Prof Cardie is super nice and helpful in person. Programming projects are interesting and not too hard. Needs to hire better or more TAs, though - I've taken both IR and NLP with her and both times the TA team was sub-par wrt to responsiveness and knowing what's going on.

Professor Cardie herself is accessible and tries to be helpful. However, the rest of the course stuff was disorganized and it felt like Professor Cardie was out-of-loop regarding her own class, especially in terms of the assignments. Very interesting class but beware the projects, very open-ended, unclear and time consuming.
The worst professor in the CS apartment. Never knew I could dislike a professor this much

analysis.name: Claudia Fischbach-Teschl
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She is knowledgeable and pretty clear. However, the class is boring and usually consists of her talking directly from her slides. Class is 1/3 lectures on tissue eng, and then 1/3 journal club, and 1/3 project presentations. Class is helpful, but can easily be learned with the textbook + journal articles.

This professor was the harshest grader I've had to date in my time at Cornell. They have no sympathy for their students and look down upon students who ask for extensions. The exams were unusually difficult for an easy material class and the lab portion was poorly run. Weekly lab reports were a long time commitment. Lab portion was fun though.

Not a bad professor for lack of ability but lack of care about anything related to the course or its students. I don't know why she's in the field, because she could make anything associated with it more boring than simply watch the clock tick on. Also, her grading system is quite honestly atrocious.

Though the prof teaches well, can't help but feel like there is no depth. It's a lot of cell biology in the lectures and useful, interesting labs! TAs kind of had no idea about anything the whole time, so they didn't help either. Hard to study for :(

analysis.name: Elisha Cohn
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof Cohn is the best! Always accessible outside of class. Class covers variety of topics and each paper has multiple options so there's something for everyone. Some readings get dull but that's moreso the state of the industry than the class itself. Go to class and office hours and ASK QUESTIONS! She'll even learn your name

I took this class freshman year as FWS, and I immediately dropped it. The time I was in it we spent in a circle just noting random facts about the book at hand and didn't get anywhere at all, plus it was ridiculously boring with no variance in activities whatsoever. Felt like I was back in 9th grade lit. Like the topic; don't like the professor.

Pros: Super approachable and knowledgeable professor who makes an effort to keep class interesting and gives specific feedback, not difficult to get a good grade if you put even a little effort into essays and talking in class Cons: A LOT of reading, class participation is important to your grade  Overall, a good class that did improve my writing!

Elisha is hands down the best prof I've had the pleasure of learning from at Cornell-- I even asked her to be my major advisor, and nominated her for a teaching award (literally wrote 3 full pages about how wonderful she is). She genuinely cares about her students and wants everyone's input. She values student readings as much as her own!
Professor Cohn was wonderful, very easy to contact and was super flexible with deadlines. I loved her class so much. Highly reccommend.

analysis.name: Michael Clarkson
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 52 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Great lecturer with a passion for functional programming and even more so for teaching.  Plays a different song related to the class topic each day, and then made a playlist available on spotify at the end of the year.  Some lectures he comes dressed in costumes and makes the lectures very fun and engaging, as well as very easy to follow.

Heavy workload with little practical value. Clarkson often directly shamed students who were struggling to their face. While the content is deceivingly easy, the class is made to be stressful and discouraging. Exams are graded harshly, rubric is always weird. At least the grade bounds are lenient though. Overall, a waste of time and energy.

Lectures were clear and well-organized. Assignment structure slightly varies from semester to semester; this semester, we only had 4 assignments so the workload was very manageable. Exams were generally fair. While he is good at explaining and definitely knows what he's talking about, Prof. Clarkson can have a tendency to be pedantic and snarky.

I still remember walking in the first day with KPDH music playing and it was amazing. I will say I did not like how he added Interpreters at the end since he has never taught that before and the TA's did not know it at all so you could barely get help on it. Otherwise, he explains what I thought would be a mid language really well.

His lectures were good but he was just a rude jerk sometimes for no reason. He would be nice to certain people and then randomly he would just choose someone to be completely rude and mean to for no reason. I remember a girl asking a normal question and he framed it like she asked a completely braindead question.

analysis.name: Edwin (todd) Cowen
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 14 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: One of the best profs ive had at Cornell. Was definitely my favorite class ive had this semester, and the content taught was super interesting. very reasonable grading, and he is also so nice and obviously cares about his students!! wish all of my professors were like him, take any class you can with him!!
It was alright. I didn't learn much. It's just that I lowkey was taking this ENGRI because it was easy.

This was by far one of my favorite classes that I have taken at Cornell. I took it my freshman year, spring 2022 and I learned so much from the class. There were no tests, two debates (which you just had to show up to and you got full credit for being there), weekly psets, and a final project. I learned a lot from this class with no stress.

Prof. Cowen is an amazing professor. I agree with the comment that PSETs could be confusing, but TAs are helpful at office hours. It's designed to be an "easy A" course as long as you do the proper work. NO exams, just a final project in which you design a net-zero carbon home. Spring 2023 median was an A.

He is a nice guy that wants you to do well. But, his tests are far from easy- open note, book and twice as much time as you need. If you work hard, you can do well. I'm still not sure how big the curve is.

analysis.name: Colleen  Carey
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: lectures are basically just reading off the slides (stutters a lot but it's okay we're all human). I assume she changed the way she grades from past years cuz no way people got a A+. Many instances where students corrected her during review, doesn't really know the content confidently herself. Great person but not the best prof. very harsh grader.

This professor is very strict on grading & has been rude to myself and several others in dialogue and email discourse. Many colleagues have noted that what was an easy class now demands perfection of all students and is seemingly forcing the average grade down overnight through the enactment of unfair or potentially targeted strictness on grading.

While I enjoyed the course content, I felt the delivery was bad. When students asked questions during lectures, the responses were her reading directly from the slides rather than providing clarification to address our confusion. She often got confused by what she was saying herself even though she has taught this class for a while.

She is so knowledgable and PUBPOL 3110 is an extremely interesting class and lectures were engaging even if not mandatory. There is a decent amount of homework for the drug project but it is graded very leniently and the tests are pretty fair. Also there are no readings so everything you need to know is on the slides.

PUBPOL 3110 is very similar to PUBPOL 2350 but a lot easier. The prelim and final make up half your grade and are super easy. The remaining half of your grade is a project (group or solo) that takes the entire semester and is also super duper easy. Overall, a great professor and a very passable class.

analysis.name: Connie Yuan
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: I took this class five years ago and it still haunts me. My friends and I still talk about it. The material is not communicated well by the professor. She does not seem to care about the students. Ironically a lack of cultural awareness in an intercultural comms class. Save yourself and choose something else.
She knows the topic extremely well and the state of the current literature in the field. I have learnt a lot about social network analysis whilst taking this class.

I took Mindful Intercultural Communication with her for a few weeks before I dropped the course. We did not mention the topic of communication once, and largely focused on tangents and themes that were not described in the syllabus. The course description did a terrible job at describing what actually went on in the course.

Does not care. Unreasonable examination, too little time. Horrible lectures, boring, dense, goes off on irrelevant tangents. Grading is tough. Doesn't heed student feedback. You get tested on her verbal lecture rather than the actual course material. Worst professor and course I've taken. Tried to give her feedback once, and she rejected it.

By far the worst professor I have had at Cornell. Tons of busywork, inconsistent grading, and absolutely no feedback. Lectured directly from the textbook with powerpoints made by the textbook, not her. Also made many microaggressions during a class that is supposed to foster open communication. AVOID AT ALL COSTS. Such a waste of time.

analysis.name: Geoff Coates
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 49 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor Coates is one of the best lecturers at Cornell, and certainly does a great job teaching organic chemistry. He is extremely clear and organized, and is genuinely one of the nicest professors you will meet. His research focuses on polymer synthesis (and he's extremely good at it), so know those polymers and transition metal catalysts!

Arguably one of the best professors I've had yet at Cornell. Coates is the man. He is very clear and organized in his lectures (and often funny), and he wants to see you do well. Difficult course in general due to the material, but much more bearable with Coates. Very down to earth guy. I highly recommend him.

One of the greatest lecturer who knows the material and knows how to explain them. Never fell asleep because his explanations were golden. And his my advisor and is a very down to earth person. I'm glad I had him for orgo.

this prof views his role as an enabler, not a gatekeeper. no doubt this is a hard class, but the lectures are clear and organized. the Friday help sessions are amazingly helpful - relaxed informal setting. first exam was more than fair - if you attend lecture and do the homework you should do well. TAs are all very helpful.

Prof Coates is my favorite teacher at Cornell so far.  He was a great guy and teacher.  He genuinely cared about teaching the students and has a great personality while lecturing. =)  Very much recommended for Orgo.

analysis.name: Robert Connelly
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 71 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Terrible lecturer.  He's obviously a brilliant mathematician, but he cannot for the life of him explain even simple concepts. Lectures are completely disorganized, and he makes tons of mistakes in examples. He probably makes sense to people to whom math comes intuitively, but for 90% of the class this isn't the case. Exams were ridiculously hard
Makes several mistakes and is known as "Goth Moses" by among the students. Lectures are essentially useless... READ THE BOOK

Connelly is passionate about his subject but he lacks ability to teach those who have never encountered multi. The textbook only helps if you're able to grasp concepts from reading but 1920 is a very visual course in order to understand. I watched prof. Leonard on YouTube who explains very well and I'm still failing. If never taken multi stay away

Brilliant guy, but absolute horrible teacher. Constantly makes mistakes during lecture and asks the students whether he did something wrong. Teaching style seems obsolete. He attempts to explain quadric surfaces using his fingers, whereas the better professor, James Belk, is reasonable and uses a projector to show the 3d plot. TEXTBOOK > CLASS

Great person, I like him a lot. However, his lectures are so scattered and he teaches nothing relative to his quizzes/tests at all. I stopped going to lectures all together and just relied on the textbook. He makes lots of mistakes in lecture; he's funny and cares, though. If you have no prior experience with material: steer clear.

analysis.name: Ray Craib
analysis.verdict: Tough course, but students consistently rate the teaching highly, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: What an amazing professor. He's funny and clearly passionate about Latin America and his passion is infectious. He has a great way of reframing events you may be familiar with but in a new light. The reading is heavy and a lot but definitely interesting. His slides were extremely helpful and he's very kind. Highly recommend!!

Craib is a great lecturer and teacher–even if you're not knowledgeable about or particularly interested in history. His lectures are thorough, incredibly informative, and organized in an appealing fashion. Try to participate–that does mean a lot (but I think with the atmosphere he creates, you'll want to). Computers are allowed.

Take this course! Craib did a great overview of US foreign policy in Latin America. I believe his slides, however, were overfilled. He's very passionate about the subject, but it was a little hard to retain information on places and people (there were so many!). Loved the information, but good thing there was no real exam. Great, but confusing.

For HIST 1951. Class isn't hard and I an easy A if you put in the work (which is a lot). However, there's a lot of reading so if you don't want to read a LOT, do not take this class. Attendance is mandatory for lecture and discussion sections. Lecture is incredibly annoying because the assigned readings tell you the same stuff in more detail.

pretty great lectures with a lot of information. you need to be quick about taking notes in it. you'll need them cause the readings aren't as helpful for the tests themselves. Attendance isnt mandatory by him but actually is. Though they help for the essays which are a lot. Nice guy and very helpful. Essays vary in length and get bigger later on.

analysis.name: Cristina Florea
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: You'll definitely learn a lot. But don't expect to do well. The grading is very strict. Reading assignments are a bit unbalanced, sometimes you'll be reading 3-5 pages for a lecture and other days you'll be reading an entire book. The material does have a unique character which sets it apart from boring textbooks and hard to read academic texts.

prof florea is so knowledgable, funny, and caring that it's impossible not to enjoy this class. that being said, this is a difficult course with lots of reading and tough/sometimes unclear grading. but imo it's worth the hard work, esp if you have any kind of interest in soviet history

Professor Florea was very passionate about what she taught, and I really enjoyed her class. The grade was made up of 3 papers (4 pages), a final paper (arond 15 pages), and short reading reflections before each class. The grading could be a bit tough, but just make sure you put effort and really think through before writing.

Florea was super caring (we got free treats!) and is one of the best history professors, imo. She may grade a little harshly, but the feedback is so good (a rarity!). Also, borders are awesome, so the class was very interesting. If you are passionate about debates on sovereignty and migration, take this course. But be prepared for one book a week.

Sweetest professor ever! Great lectures, great feedback on papers, etc. This is a class oriented towards non-history majors IMO, but if you're a humanities major getting an A isn't difficult considering the short length of the papers in this class. Professor/TA are also available and give great advice for papers.

analysis.name: Cristobal Forestier
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: This was for INFO4145: Professor Forestier is really nice and accommodating. He has some interesting insight to offer about privacy and the data economy and is humorous. Grading is not too difficult, especially if you just show up and do the work. I learned a lot in this class while also not being stressed out which was really pleasant.
Very nice professor. Taking his Privacy and Security in the Data Economy and he is very knowledgeable and passionate.
Extremely rude and condescending. Don't bother asking questions on ed discussion if you aren't ready to be criticized and patronized
I loved Networks 2 as a class. The matter was very interesting and Professor Cristobal Cheyre Forestier is sweet and accommodating. Would recommend.

His lectures are boring (probably because the class started during the COVID Zoom class era) and the grading is incredibly harsh. Be prepared to show every single step of your work or lose three-quarter of your points.

analysis.name: Crocker Liu
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Liu wants to give too much to the students. The AVERAGE score of the class of midterm is 32.4% of total score. And during the final exam, he started the exam 20min before the official time. Then during the test he announced the exam duration shortened from 3 hour to 2:30. But it doesn't change much of the result even with more time

Crocker Liu assigns a disrespectful amount of homework. It's content is not hard, but it's poorly written, unclear, and teaches you essentially nothing. Also, it takes more hours to complete than all other classes' work combined--hence, DISRESPECTFUL. Lectures are cursory and unnecessary. He makes you buy his slides instead of posting them online.
his tests are awful. everyone fails them. i do not understand the point of making a test to fail

Oh, Crocker. Think of this class as the real estate minor weed-out course. We started the semester with 65 students and ended it with 16. You'll probably get a 25 on a test but don't worry... that'll be the median (heavy curve). Tedious psets & opaque grading. Rambling, nonsensical lectures. If you really want the minor, just get thru this class.

Worst class I've ever taken. Prof tries to pass humor off as "dry" but just comes off rude and arrogant. Case studies are difficult and take days to complete; he doesn't lecture on the material needed until AFTER they are due. The midterm was ridiculously difficult and was NOT ON THE MATERIAL he covered in class. Makes you PAY for lecture notes.

analysis.name: Craig Fennie
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 12 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: His lectures do not help with the psets at ALL. In fact my TAs were much more helpful in terms of problem solving. That being said, he does stress theory in his lectures...which he cannot do well. Makes annoying mistakes during derivations, cannot lucidly and confidently explain a concept...just plain confusing. E&M is a tough topic, but come on.

His lectures are only on the very basics and does not help at all when it comes to solving homework or test problems. The solutions to problem sets he provides are either incomprehensible or intangible. I don't want to sound like a hater, but he is really an awful teacher. And you don't have another choice for honors E&M in the spring.

Really insightful when it comes to teaching about his research field, perovskites; and gives you intuitive ways of thinking about concepts without doing too much math.  The course organization was great.
He cares about students' workload and does not want to cause stress. He genuinely loves solid state physics, but he gets a bit tongue-tied explaining things sometimes. :-)

Fennie is a good guy. As a prof he is mediocre at worst and decent at best. Gets confused sometimes during derivations. Lectures sometimes lack direction, sometimes easy to get bored. His lectures were based almost entirely on the "optional" textbook, Griffiths, while the only thing he used the "required" text (Purcell) for was homework.

analysis.name: Craig Frey
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Frey was a solid professor! Organized, quick lectures followed by daily lab assignments was a great structure for the class that I really enjoyed. He gave good feedback on the two projects and was helpful in class when you're having trouble. Plus he brings his dog into lectures. Take his class if you want to learn Python quickly and efficiently!

Cares about student understanding of (in my case) C++, and other languages that pair well with it. The final project was in a group and allowed for very significant freedom. - also he brings his dog into class.
super awesome way to quickly learn python for non CS majors!!! very sweet guy, brings his dog scooby to class

analysis.name: Maria Cristina Garcia
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 29 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: - Smart, engaging, approachable professor, one of my favorites - Moderate to heavy reading; however, as long as you understand what the general points of each piece are, you will be fine - Great discussion leader - Class requires a 25 pg research paper as only assignment FYI  - I would highly recommend MCG, she is an amazing person and teacher

Dr. Garcia is a fantastic professor. She is an awesome lecturer, very passionate and knows a ton about immigration, especially from Latin America. Class was interesting and eye opening. No tests, just a couple papers which were not too bad and fairly graded. There's also journals to write every week but only checked twice. HIGHLY recommend
Knows so much about refugee and immigration history it's remarkable. Clear lecturer, but I think she really excels in a seminar. Incredibly approachable.

One of my favorite professors at Cornell; her classes were my reintroduction to American history after not doing much of it for a few years after high school (I'm a history major). Absolutely wonderful lecturer and has amazing personal historical anecdotes. I get the idea she wants to retire soon so take her classes while you can!

AMAZING lecturer! -- Her passion for history is inspirational.  She is the BEST professor at Cornell.  Interest in class material is all relative depending on what one likes.  Personally, I love everything she teaches, thus I'm biased.  I highly recommend that students take a class with her before graduating, as again she is a wonderful lecturer.

analysis.name: Curran Muhlberger
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 53 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Muhlberger is truly an awful lecturer. He uses so many analogies that do not apply well to CS concepts. Furthermore, Muhlberger does not share the answers to the weekly quizzes. How are we supposed to learn in the end if we don't know what we did wrong?

Muhlberger is the driest lecturer I've ever had. He talks extremely fast and at a strange cadence. Muhlberger's  quizzes are full of gotcha-questions that don't teach you anything. He's also remaking the course and adding in a bunch of ludicrously complex projects. I learned more from W3 Schools than I did from Muhlberger. Would not recommend.

Took CS 2110 Freshmen fall, having learned Java already. Muhlberger is knowledgeable/nerdy in a good way, can speak fast at times but is very caring/genuine. Participation recorded using i-clicker, and assignments can be quite long. Final assignment was very fun and could compete against others. Overall, very enjoyable, you will learn a lot.

A needlessly brutal course. Of course during his last semester he makes assignments excessively tedious and time consuming, locking points behind difficult challenge extensions. Prelims and final were again excessively harsh grading-wise, with medians much lower than previous semesters. Thanks to him I have decided to no longer pursue a CS major.

Not an easy class. A bunch of work on top of an already busy schedule. TAs constantly mess up grading of exams. Of course he's not teaching this course next semester either. Had to leave the tedious stuff with us. Would not recommend.

analysis.name: Francisco Valero Cuevas
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: (At USC now, not Cornell) ENGR102 is just an awful class in general. Valero Cuevas isn't the worst to take it with, as he doesn't really give any assignments, you just have to go every week to get an A. But it's SO BORING! His voice will put you to sleep, and he looks for phones like a hawk. It might not be worth it.

I enjoyed this freshman academy class. I do believe that academy coaches (basically upperclassmen TA's) were the reason for my enjoyable time. However, although Prof Valero-Cuevas was not the most exciting person at times, you can tell he is a smart and nice guy with a good heart who really wants to see you succeed. Also an easy A.
no clear concept of student understanding.  obviously has poor grasp of material he is lecturing on.

analysis.name: James Cutting
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Yes the tests are really hard, but this class is very fair overall. Best formula to get an A: GO to lecture, write down about half of what he says, learn the bold terms in the book, and study hard for tests. Cutting is a masterful lecturer, very intelligent and knowledgeable. Boring every once in a while but usually fascinating, original material.
Knowledgable professor but he cannot communicate information clearly to students. Powerpoint slides are not very helpful. Prelims draw from pure memorization of random definitions.

His lecture slides by themselves are not too helpful. The real stuff happens in the professor's delivery in class. So attending lecture is key. If you make it, the lectures are fascinating and well-delivered; if you don't, well, good luck trying to infer the content from pictures and random terms. (When I tried that, it didn't go well.)

An incredibly thorough, clear, and well-organized teacher. Exams were detailed, but very fair. He also let the entire class know outright that lecture material and text readings would be distinct from one another, but that both would be covered on prelims. Hands-down one of the most competent people I've learned something from at Cornell.

I didn't read the textbooks, just did my best to memorize the glossary terms from the assigned readings. I took extremely detailed notes of everything he SAYS. Memorize everything you can. His delivery of the lecture was most important. I learned a lot in this class and appreciate Prof Cutting's dedication and passion for these topics :D

analysis.name: Jonathan Culler
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: It felt like I was taking a class with a legend - and I was. For someone as accomplished as he is, I never felt like he was judging me for being new to the major. Poetry recitation was a part of the grade, and he listened dutifully and with a smile. Remembers my name two semesters later. All-around great guy.

I took his class a few years ago, and just loved it.  Not only was he refreshingly clear and unpretentious, but when he walked in the room in his leather bomber jacket and raked his fingers through his silky silver hair, his uncanny resemblance to Indiana Jones was enough to make anyone go weak in the knees!

You will never look at the world the same way after this class.  The lecture format is the ideal way to digest dense theoretical texts.  Culler makes graspable the obtuse.  The work load was extremely manageable, and the grading was fair.  This class turned out to be one of the most useful classes I took, and I can't commend Culler highly enough.

Professor Culler, though a genuine sweet-heart, is not a great teacher. I love poetry but his lectures made me (and many others) fall asleep. His voice drones and is way too quiet. Lecture information isn't helpful for graded material. Also, he doesn't give clear grade criteria so I never felt confident handing in any of my assignments.
Took his lit. theory class a few years back. A bit uncomfortable at lecturing, but always clear and approachable. A really decent prof.

analysis.name: Verdie Culbreath
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I had True Stories with her and it was a disjointed and disappointing haze of poorly-led discussions. She's very passive-aggressive so DO NOT get on her bad side; after I wrote something adverse to her agenda, papers were returned with vague criticisms and I couldn't do well. I ended up with a B-, while in my other FWS I got an A+. Don't take her!

Directionless, vague, poorly put-together. Verdie's True Stories is a disjointed mess of a class that bounces from book to book; books only related in the way that they serve to push her agenda. Not that I always disagreed with her agenda. For example, fighting racism and sexism are noble pursuits, but the class felt like well-trodden propaganda.

Verdie is great and loves to hear student feedback and participation. She can be a little tough at first but as long as you take her comments it will be just fine. Not the most approachable but definitely always willing to help. She is definitely passionate about her work in order to get students engaged

Sometimes the prompts for essays were confusing, but Verdie is generous with her time in office hours and her feedback is really thorough and helpful. The discussion and student-led sections were always great. She's tough at first but if you work hard and do all of the assignments it will pay off.
Verdie is passionate about the course material and excellent at engaging students. Her class taught me new ways of thinking and reading and provided a safe space to express my views.

analysis.name: Christopher Csikszentmihalyi
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment:
He assigns a lot of readings before each class, and they feel more like long research papers than enjoyable material. It's hard to focus during lectures because he says “umm” and “uh” 30 times in one sentence. The grading is very subjective, and getting over 90 seems nearly impossible. An 80 feels like a win.

I recommend you to not take his class.

Just terrible! Grading feels like roulette depending on what TA you get / how the professor is feeling. Class has 0 structure, readings / material is laughable — for those interested in design careers, this will not help you in the slightest 

Look at how many angry students there are this is pretty indicative how peers in the class feel

First time writing RMP because INFO4240 was a nightmare. Attendance is strict, class activities useless, 50 pages of dull readings every week, and workbooks are graded completely subjectively. 

Grading is biased and ridiculous. The instructor even calls graders who give too many 90 grade, completely unfair. Avoid this class—you'll learn nothing.

GRADING IS SO SUBJECTIVE. THEY GRADE SUBJECTIVELY AND LAZILY TO JUST TAKE POINTS OFF. CHRIS DOESN'T HAVE A CLEAR STRUCTURE IN LECTURE EITHER AND JUMPS BACK AND FORTH BETWEEN TOPICS. HE ALSO OFTEN UMMS/UHHS THROUGHOUT THE LECTURE WHICH IS DISTRACTING WHEN IT'S HARD TO UNHEAR.

my least fav class ever at cornell! prof is super rude + bashes students in class / assignments. beyond the prof the class itself is awful, its all over the place and lecture is him just stuttering / umm-ing through random topics with no flow

analysis.name: Csaba Csaki
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Csaba is the best! Very knowledgeable, is able to derive formulas without even consulting his notes. Highly recommend this course! Homeworks are a bit difficult at first, but once you become accustomed to them, you learn a lot.

The material was presented very clearly and lectures were well organized. He's very good at explaining the concepts behind the material. The homework can be difficult, but the TAs are extremely helpful. Tests are fair.
Very good professor. Lectures follow book but add material and are interesting. Homeworks are long, but doable. Prelims mostly cover the material taught and are fair. Overall, a very good course.
Very nice person; can tell he likes teaching.
Gave me a B+ even though I scored higher than my peers with As because I was a sophomore. Not saying he is biased but be careful when taking his courses.

analysis.name: Richard Curtis
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 135 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Would have been a better class if he wasn't so mean. Lectures always went overtime. Review sessions went on way longer than they should have. He said they shouldn't be substitution for lectures, but they essentially were lecture repeats. Stopped going to class after the first review session because I realized this and I missed nothing.
Completely closed off from student feedback and arrogant. One of the worst professors I've ever had.
Cornell's most disresptful professor.
Take this class if you feel like going back to high school. Take any other class if you feel like continuing your Ivy League education.
His lectures and his course packet were useless and his attitude was disgusting. Worst professor ever.

analysis.name: Cynthia Kinsland
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 60 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Wow I've taken numerous "Intro to" courses during college but Cynthia's "Intro Organic Chemistry" is anything but an intro class. Hardest class I've taken at Cornell, lectures are so fast you don't have time to comprehend the material, and there's practically no help available out of class (nearly 400 students and only 2 TAs?).

All of the grades were from the three prelims and the final exam. There was homework but it was not graded or turned in. Not doing the homework will make the exams very hard. You have to do the readings. She does not explain what she is doing in lecture.

Lectures don't help, she goes by everything too fast, your entire grades are based on 3 prelims and 1 final, and to top the cake the materials gone through in class are "orgo 2 level", according to my friend in a biochemistry major. If you are going to take this class, know that it will likely be the hardest intro class you will ever take.

The way the lab reports are graded is horrendous and completely dependent on whether you get a nice TA or not. I have never had a professor spend half a lecture explaining to the class how to "respectfully email professors", but cannot even provide a grading rubric other than a link to a website for our lab reports whose format she didn't explain.

Homework was optional, meaning it didn't have to be turned in. However, not doing the homework meant not being able to do anything on the exam, so you always have to do it anyway- it should be graded. Lectures were amusing, but not helpful in the learning process. I learned almost nothing from any lecture because she never explained what she did.

analysis.name: Cynthia Robinson
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor was very knowledgeable and flexible with deadlines. The class was manageable with steady effort. The class was a collection of guest lectures, so there are many lecture styles and materials within one class.

Cynthia is definitely a little goofy but in a good way. She makes the content interesting and keeps lectures fun. She does assign a lot of readings but if you just skim them you will be fine for lecture and section.

This class was really really boring (it was all online the semester I took it, so you may have a different experience in person). The teacher was nice, but you had to go to lecture or rewatch the lectures or you wouldn't pass. Super easy tests, but a lot of unnecessary papers. Not recommended unless you love love love art history.

The online format made the class 90% straight up lecture, 5% asking students to participate, and 5% about rabbits. Professor Robinson is definitely very knowledgeable about what she's teaching, especially since she is considered a leading scholar in the field. However, it can be quite abstract, and lectures are VERY important.

analysis.name: Cynthia Lin-Lawell
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: She provides clear guidelines for success, and although participation matters, there is plenty of extra credit. I ended up getting a 125% in the class, but the highest grade you can receive is an A. She has a very positive attitude and an infectious smile, and brings snacks to class, making the night class much more enjoyable. HW is long but easy.

Summarized by nonsensical psets in both length and content, arbitrary grading policies, rambling lectures and content a middle schooler could learn in ten minutes being shoved into a 3 hour long lecture. I would have have been satisfied with this course if I actually learned something, instead I lose my weekends and my sanity to useless psets.

The class was surprisingly easy for a 4000-level course. No outside materials were provided, and all the problem sets were based on lectures. Some policies were rigid. Sooooo much extra credit! Easy A, but no A+.

Looove Cynthia - she's super sweet, funny, and smart. The only thing about the way the course was set up was that the problem sets were really long. Lots of research on real-world examples, and she made the content super digestible .

very heavy workload class. Every class has a problem set and I have to spend a whole day every week on it, In the assignments, I would write almost 10 pages with one or two Excel sheets for linear programming and data analysis. TAs are nice but tough graders, and if you relax a little bit, you will get a lot of points deducted like 80 out of 100.

analysis.name: Cynthia Camp
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I WOULD NOT PREFER. I RECEIVING A 50 ON THE FIRST ESSAY, I ASKED FOR GUIDANCE. WE EMAILED BACK AND FORWARDS ABOUT 3TIMES CONCERNING THE 2ND ESSAY. I MADE ALL CORRECTION AND TWEEKS SHE ASKED ME TO MAKE THREEEE TIMESSSS, AND I STILL RECEIVED A 'C' ON THE ESSAY. don't do it unless you want to drop your gpa!

Overall a very cool teacher. She is extremely helpful and understanding and will go out of her way to help you if you express any concern. Also, she is very well informed about the subject area. She is well-organized and clear about what she expects.
She was a fun, and very clear teacher.  Sometimes the assignments can suck because the readings are so long, but overall it was a fun class.  If you improved your writing you generally did well.
Always willing to help and give you pointers. Very knowledgable.

analysis.name: Cynthia Turner
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Turner is a sweet heart, that is, if you are in Wind Ensemble. If you are put in Wind Symphony for CUWinds, you will surely understand that Prof Turner looks down on the Wind Symphony, in a condescending matter. While sure, we're not as good as the Wind Ensemble, we're still people.
Professor Turner is an expert in her field, and an amazing musician to work with. She cares deeply about her work and students. If you get the chance to work with her, take it!
Clear, effective and efficient rehearsals, great repertoire, challenging; believes in students and what we can do. Lucky to work with her. Excellent musician and teacher.
Extremely musical and knowledgeable. Great repertoire that challenged and stretched me as a musician. More fun and interesting than orchestra!
I was in Wind Symphony and loved every minute of it. Bet that dude didn't practice. Professor Turner is an amazing teacher and musician.

analysis.name: Daniel Leithinger
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: 13 years ago, he was producing work that was truly futuristic and mind-blowing. (A phd well earned)  Nowadays, he just produces mediocre cardboard junk.  How has he regressed so much?

His work you can find by Googling him is vastly different in quality & depth compared to what he teaches in classroom on the same subject matter. As such, Daniel Leithinger's class is profoundly underwhelming. He truly doesnt live up to his own reputation.
I'm glad he decided to start being a father to his kid. Lost all respect for this guy when he was out in Colorado.
I think its cute that he sounds like Arnold Schwarzenegger if he never got into steroids.  Class was ok.

analysis.name: DD Miller
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Miller is very nice, good lecturer, extremely knowledgeable. Always replied to e-mails really quickly & good at clearly explaining things.  This class was really interesting; one of the few I've taken that's actually useful in the real world!  Super easy--if willing to put in tiny bit of effort you will get an A. Take this class!

He's really nice, and his questions on tests are really straight forward. Easy to take notes during class.  The paper is easy points.  Dr. Gravani, who teaches the 2nd half of the class is very nitpicky though.  The class is worth taking for Miller's lectures though.
Miller was a wonderful professor! Very interesting lectures and material. Easy class - grade was primarily based on iClicker questions (readings required)
Tests are far too hard.  Class averages very low.  Avoid if possible.  Needs to be reminded that 2 credit courses shouldn't be more work than Orgo.

analysis.name: Debdeep Jena
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Overall a nice and knowledgeable lecturer. But he can be very unorganized at some points, which can create distractions (a typical phrase from him: "ok forget about this, let me explain it that way"). The problem is that he tries to teach the whole course from his memory. But somebody needs to tell him it is OK to use some notes while teaching.
This man is an actual psychpath
The professor's teaching style was so confusing... completely loses track of himself and hard to follow / keep up with homework which is totally out of sync with lectures
The guy lives in his own world
one of the best lecturers at ECE

analysis.name: Debbie Cherney
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: She's a nice lady and easily one of the most caring you'll meet. However, she's not the best lecturer and lecture is a snooze fest. Just review the practice tests she provides and review the slides and you should be good. Lab assignments are tedious but it's really the only thing you have to worry about. Class is pretty easy just do the work.

Easiest class I've taken. All the assignments are easy and you can usually fight for points back. Don't take the 1 prelim lightly though, there aren't many grades in the class so make sure you do decent on that. There's a final project that's basically graded on completion. Lecture is very unorganized so you can skip and not miss anything.

Dr. Cherney is a sweet woman, but she does not lecture well and her ppts were all over the place. I had zero experience with animal care, so the messy lectures paired with no textbook to supplement made it really difficult for me to really learn anything at all, so I personally struggled a lot on the prelims

Nothing against the professor, just how to course is organized. Half of the grade is just menial busywork that doesn't correlate to any real life application or to the course itself. Valuable feedback is never given with little clarity offered on questions, leaving students guessing as to how to get points. Labs and lecture are peak nap time hours.

While I think she can be a bit disorganized, she will only test on things she lectures on. I loved writing the papers for this class and enjoyed the freedom we were allowed. She's very caring and available to answer questions. I do think that you get out of this class what you put into it so if you don't care about it, you won't gain anything.

analysis.name: Denise Green
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Took 1250&4021–totally different experiences bc big lecture vs small seminar w/a field trip to India + Sri Lanka. In both, prof Green gave her all! I am inspired by her dedication & learned so much about the industry. She has high expectations and you rise to them. Not easy but so worth it to find out what you're capable of learning and achieving.

Professor Green's class was so awesome, I could really tell she is so passionate about the subjects she teaches! She can be a tough grader but if you follow along it's actually pretty easy to do well! I saw a few reviews saying she over explains- however I really felt that the more explanation the better. 10/10 would recommend

She overexplains way too much in lectures and her tests are interesting to say the least. She made a slideshow where we have one minute to answer each question and obviously that created problems. Everyone got messed up on that test but she's too stubborn to curve or do anything to fix that. Passive aggressive weird vibe.

Loved the lectures, assignments, and exams — so clear and actually fair. Super available outside class + always extra credit available to boost your grade. You can tell she's genuinely hyped about the subject, so lectures are never drag. grateful I got to take this class.

Took this w/ zero fashion background and now I see the whole designed world differently. Prof Green is so passionate, real, and actually meets students where they're at. Also tells it like it is. It's a tough crowd in the lecture hall but she still delivers. Honestly iconic.

analysis.name: Louis Derry
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Insanely lecture heavy. He would just yap and yap, in lab too - often about something loosely related but unclear whether or not it was important. Problem set and exam questions were confusing and graded confusingly. No set office hours. Course jumped all over the place, which was annoying. Would not take again. he just yaps and is confusing.

Louis does talk a lot, but he's a great guy. If you make an effort to learn you will surely enjoy this class. Very unique professor at Cornell where he genuinely enjoys and understands his field, hard not to learn here.

This guy talks a lot but it's because he's interested in his work. Great lecturer, but if you don't show up to class good luck surviving. Despite this, very accessible and understanding outside of class.
If you get Lou going, he won't stop talking. The guy clearly loves his field, but can be monotonous after 2hrs of lecture in lab. Great guy though.

analysis.name: Miloje Despic
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: One of the best professors I've ever had. You can clearly see he has a love for the topic and enjoys talking to students after class. If you can, definitely take a class with him!
Super chill professor who also has interesting lectures. Big fan. Had him for 1141 and it was great.

Definitely expects you to take the class seriously. He provides handouts that have answers to questions he asks in class and the material that he goes through, which is really helpful when you're doing homework later in the weekstudying for exams. TA graded harshly, but Despic was considerate and mindful of the difficulty level of the work.

Miloje is such an amazing professor! The concepts are difficult (especially in the syntax portion of the class) but he explains everything very clearly, and the homework and exams are straightforward. He is incredibly kind and wants everyone to do well. Plus his accent is awesome!
I greatly enjoyed this class. Prof. Despic makes the course fun, engaging, and not difficult. Exams mirrored the weekly problem sets and even had bonus points.

analysis.name: Sanjay Dharmavaram
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really one of the best TAs (if not instructors) I have had at Cornell thus far. He's funny, engaging, approachable, and teaches the material incredibly well. Highly recommended

Professor Dharmavaram is the best! I really enjoyed this class and we used the same textbook as my high school, so it wasn't too stressful. I really enjoyed the material and his presentation of it. The man really knows how to teach! It's really unfortunate that this is his last semester at Cornell. He'll be at UCLA for research soon! Godspeed. (A+)

I used to be bored and not pay very much attention in my past math lectures, but Sanjay's engaging and clear lecturing style allowed me to pay close attention in lectures for the first time. This also deepened my interest in the course (differential equations) and made me enjoy it overall. However, the prelims were not too easy. Final was alright.
Really good instructor, I had him as a TA. His sections were probably more useful than the lectures. Also, very approachable and helpful person.

Pretty good lectures. Need to read the textbook to do the homework because sometimes the homework content wouldn't be covered in lecture until the day it was due. Grading was also fair. I think for the most part, the class isn't curved by very much and the average grade is like a B, but the tests were fair

analysis.name: Binoy Dharia
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Only once have I looked forward to class with such anticipation that I felt as though I was about to have a first kiss with a new love.  Dr. Dharia was the culprit.  And he was able to achieve that with a material as drab as economics.
Dr. Dharia is easy as can be.  I learnt a ton in this class but didn't have to put much effort into it.  He works hard to try and make the material interesting is accent is hilarious.
Dr. Dharia is hilarious.  He tells great stories of his lady friends.  He should be the president.
Prof. Dharia is good.  He hardly use textbook.  I make A in him class and hardly homework.
Such a cutie. And so bright!  Not the easiest but you learn a lot.

analysis.name: Shelby Dietz
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. Dietz was a gem. She was always willing to help and made sure rubrics were clear. Practice exams were given but only one for each. She went out of her way to make sure our questions were answered and showed patience even when me and others asked the silliest questions. It was always nice going to her office hours and chatting with her.

Nice to your face but not behind your back. Doesn't help or support students, and it doesn't understand whatsoever. If you take any classes with her, don't make mistakes, not even typos, and avoid interactions. The other professors in the courses are usually nice and understanding, so try to talk to them instead.

Very organized class structure. Only thing a little strange was that assignments were due at 11:59 AM (noon), not PM. 

Do not underestimate the prelims.

Took BIONB 2210 with Prof Dietz. While she doesn't give any of the lectures, she is the main organizer for this course. She's super kind and wonderful at explaining concepts. Discovered her office hours right before the final exam, and wish I had gone sooner!

analysis.name: Tom Diciccio
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 56 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Professor DiCiccio was definitely one of the best teachers I've had so far. He is very chill and genuinely cares about his students. We often talked with him after class since I had questions about university applications and course material

this man is truly an angel on earth. a heavenly being sent to bless cornell university with his presence. never will you meet a kinder, more accommodating and merciful math professor. he makes an effort to make his class the least stressful as possible. also his deprecating jokes give me life

Diciccio clearly actually cares about his students and reducing stress. Our exams were all online and they give half of the homework answers in section. However, he seems to be a little tired of Ithaca and teaching in general, and most people I know didn't learn anything in the class.

An amazing professor who deeply cares about his students, if you went to his office hours he would walk through the problems, would also crack jokes in lecture as well. I hope his retirement treats him with nothing but kindness.

ABSOLUTELY GOAT. Always recommend his classes. You can learn a lot if you put in effort, but you will also be fine if you just follow the slides and assignments. Tests are not hard, and the pressure of ddl is low.

analysis.name: Brenda Dietrich
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Lectures were confusing and there was no textbook so if you did not understand them it was tough luck and you had to hope you could figure out the slides. Tons of homework that took a while to do. Grading was super easy though and there were tons of opportunities for extra credit and retakes for quizzes. She was nice when speaking one on one.

Brenda is sometimes condescending (fair enough, she was the VP of IBM so her huge ego is justifiable). Lots of tedious homework, but grading is fair and there is a lot of opportunity for extra credit. Tests are challenging & take a long time but not all questions need to be attempted, so it's manageable. An A-/A is the average grade of the class.

The professor is clearly very knowledgeable in OR. GO TO LECTURE, you will only learn the material in lecture. Lots of office hours and TAs are great. Quizzes almost every week, and attendance is based on you turning them in/picking up. Canvas is organized abysmally. Generous curve in the class, if you try a little you will be perfectly fine.

Really hard teacher but if you get to know her super funny and helpful. Lots of homework and strict grading for sure, but if you go to office hours you'll do great. Also generous with curving and extra credit. I'm not that strong of a math/cs student and I got a good grade in the class.

You can probably get by without going to lecture, but be prepared to read the course packet because the uploaded lecture notes are vague--and even then, she doesn't follow the course packet that well. The homework assignments have contradictory, ambiguous directions, and sometimes unrelated to lecture content; they basically set you up for failure.

analysis.name: Naminata Diabate
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: do not take coml2723. i will say that the professor definitely cares about the subject and academic inquiry, however i do not recommend taking this class. there are better courses that value participation, effort, and discourse that are not stressful and punitive like this. grading is harsh and a lot is expected for only a 2000 level course.
Can be hard to understand, but genuinely cares about the material and gives helpful feedback

I loved taking her class my final semester. I wish I could have worked with her throughout my four years. She's simply amazing. I highly recommend going to office hours (but actually go!). In the beginning of the seminar, I struggled, but after office hours, I shared my voice and grew so much as a student and person, thank you for everything!

Enthusiastic, approachable, brilliant. For every point that a student makes about the readings, she has an entire day's worth of discussion questions. She structures her course in a way that you gain a strong foundation for later, more theoretical, topics. She expects her students to think critically about the texts, and is willing to help!

By far the best professor I have had in my (albeit short) time here. She is brilliant; she provides excellent explanation, and very clear guidelines for grading. The seminar can be a bit intense as an undergrad, but definitely worth it. I want to take as many of her classes as I can!

analysis.name: Ankit Disa
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Good prof. His lectures follow the textbook very closely, and his test questions are interesting (though challenging). He is better at teaching some topics than others, but you always have the textbook. HW can be challenging, but never unfair (contrary to what others said).

One of the best and most engaging lecturers in the AEP department.  He provides insight where the book lacks and creates engaging, yet challenging problem sets.  Of course there were hiccups with course management, but students have 3 drops (for 14 assignments).  Exams ranged in difficulty (1st Avg: 86, 2nd Avg:50).

His teaching is fine, but he is a very unsympathetic professor. Many times he released a pset 3 days before it was due, then refused to give us extra time to do it. He gave hws over every single break.  For thanksgiving break, he gave us "2 weeks" to do the last problem set, except it was twice the length of a usual pset. TAs were alsoveryunhelpful

A final HW with 11 questions to do over break. Long exams and no reference sheet to complete within 2 hours. Releasing full-length problem sets only 3 days before they're due and expecting students to complete it or take a 50% hit to that grade is unreasonable and does not facilitate learning. It facilitates rushed learning.

Assigned 11-problem homework set over Thanksgiving break, gave only Three days for another problem set, required students to know material one week in advance of the actual lecture on it, homework sets on material he hadn't taught yet

analysis.name: Daisy Fan
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 157 reviews, this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: I feel bad for Her. She has to teach an intro CS class so kids who have been coding since 2 yrs don't get 100s and kids who have never coded don't get 0s. Daisy obviously fails & the poor kids who needed it most come out with a horrid grade and hatred for CS. Cornell needs to create a different class for the kids who already have a basis in coding

Nervously went into this class with 0 experience but had a great time. Prof Fan starts out at a very intro level but is very good at getting you to a solid level of proficiency very quickly. Projects are very difficult but are well-structured. Exams are heavily weighted but much simpler than projects. If you want to learn to code, take this class!!

Prof. Fan was an amazing lecturer. She cared about her students learning and really did offer a lot of opportunities to learn and better your coding skills. With that being said, this course is ridiculously difficult for an intro course and any beginner should be ready to put at least 15 hours a week into the course. Not her fault, but it's tough.

In terms of ability to teach well, I would rate her very highly. She explained things very well in class. Unfortunately, the curve was not generous. I don't know for sure, but I would guess the average for CS1112 the semester I took it was a B-. I got about a standard deviation above median on both prelims and still only ended with an A-.

Fan is a fine lecturer, if you end up trapped here, but DO NOT take this course if you can help it. 93% is to get an A-, even a .2% lower that can land you a B-, and on every project, you get 10% taken off if you even mistitle a variable or a function.  The course presents very little material, as well, so compensates by expecting hyperproficiency.

analysis.name: Jacob Dorn
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 19 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Pretty nice guy who knows his stuff, but many basic concepts were explained in really long-winded ways that made it hard to follow. The material isn't extremely hard and you can def study it in your own time, and the grade cutoffs were pretty generous. TA slides were a godsend, it was just his first sem teaching so I think the quality will improve

Lectures are overcomplicated and confusing, problem sets and the minimal practice problems given for each exam weren't reflective of tested content. He is a nice guy and the curve is projected to be generous, but I'd rather be taught well than rely on a subjective curve and feel impending doom going into every exam. I hope this course will improve.

Avoid if possible. Over-explains simple concepts with heaps of unnecessary mathematical notation. Doesn't communicate well with TAs and regularly struggles to keep a class running effectively. Problem sets are incoherent and messy. However, he's a nice guy and obviously eager to improve, so you can expect his class to be better-run in the future.

Very caring and passionate professor and is willing to answer any questions and help out when available. Sometimes lectures got off-track, and exams tend to be partially different than the content in the lectures and problem sets. But, I'm sure the class and his lectures will be far more cohesive as he teaches more.

Professor Dorn may be a nice guy but he is fresh out of graduate school and should not be teaching this level of class. His lectures are confusing and exams very difficult. I found the TA discussion section to give me better teaching than what he could give in class. I wish I had taken this class with a different professor.

analysis.name: John Doris
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: He never seems very prepared for class, saying that many of his lecture ideas came the morning of. And although ethics is never cut and dry, the lectures are often unsatisfying and lack takeaways because Doris often avoids picking sides or tries too hard be contrarian. He also says he encourages free thought, but sometimes shoots valid ideas down.

Prof Doris' class was enjoyable mainly due to the discussions he had in class with students. One of the few classes I've taken where opposing views are respected. The class is very easy and you don't need to go to lecture to do well, but I found the lectures enjoyable and learned a decent amount

Interesting lecturer (a bit fast) and the content is interesting, exams are straight memorization focused on very specific information in the slides. Cheat all you want I guess - he doesn't care. Wow, I hated this class.
just awful. so difficult. useless boring class. exams suck and so harshly graded.
Dude blows, does not respond to emails and teaches a terrible course, get a grip.

analysis.name: Doug McKee
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 49 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: McKee is honestly one of (if not the) best professors I have ever had at Cornell. He not only knows the material but can explain it in very simple terms, and actually cares about his students. I almost didn't take 3130 (not good at maths, never seen prob/stats before) but risked it since I'd heard he's an amazing prof, and best decision of my life!

It's unfortunate that this is a required class for the Economics major at Cornell. This is a god awful class with a professor that doesn't teach clearly and gives extremely long and dumb problem sets. Be prepared to learn stata and try to work with people that may not want to work with you. WORST class I've taken so far.

This is probably the most useful class I've ever taken, and McKee is a chill guy. However, I didn't find the way he taught to be intuitive or helpful and I learned much more through self-studying. Problem sets do seem to be connected to the exams, but they were pretty long and repetitive which added a lot of work to an already difficult class.

To preface, he is an great person. However, lectures are unclear and hard to follow. Problem sets are long, but the bigger issue is that they are repetitive and include ideas meant to be "implied" (not mentioned) in lecture. The workload was certainly worth more than 4 credits yet the support systems did not align with what students needed help on.

He explains concepts in an extremly clear way. Although some people mentioned that problem sets are long, there are only 6 in total and they are not very difficult. He also gives three practice exams before each prelim and the final, whch is super helpful.

analysis.name: Douglas Kriner
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 32 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Have taken a few classes with Kriner, and they have all been amazing. The lectures are so interesting, and he loves engaging with students to hear their thoughts. If you have a decent understanding of politics, then this class will be so chill.

Great guy, clear he loves what he does and is very passionate. Not reading off a script ever, very knowledgable. Just 3 essays throughout the semester with clear outlines. Final was online. Would take again!

Professor Kriner is a phenomenal lecturer. He's very engaging and obviously knowledgeable about his field of study.
Prelims are fairly easy if you go to lecture, participate in discussion, and review lecture notes. Important to actually do the assigned readings, as they are often recalled on prelims and the final.

Class is pretty easy and Kriner is a very good lecturer, often offering different perspectives on current government problems. Lectures aren't really important, but they are interesting. Make sure to do the readings and attend discussions, as a bulk of the tests are on the ideas from the readings... other topics are common sense US Govt principles

I feel so lucky to have taken his class. He is such an intelligent person and engaging lecturer, and you will learn so much about the subject just from listening to him in class even if you already know a lot about politics. He is so chill that he made our final online since it was late in the semester and gives helpful extra credit opportunities.

analysis.name: Tobias Doerr
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Toby is the goat. He makes you genuinely interested in the material and doesn't make his exams very difficult - they just test the main broad topics, nothing too specific. This class made me switch my concentration to microbio... everyone should take it!!
Microbiology is a hard course however Professor Doerr makes this class very engaging and interesting. He cares a lot about his students, and genuinely wants them to succeed.

Doerr was such a funny and sweet professor. His lectures were always engaging, and he really is passionate about microbiology which made me enjoy it too. His lecture slides can be a little overwhelming sometimes, and he does tend to go very in-depth about topics that aren't necessarily covered on the more straightforward exams. Still great prof!
A professor that genuinely wants you to succeed. Rare to find within the bio major. Absolutely take him if you can. Also gives you skibidi toilet freebies.
Amazing guy. Smart, funny, knowledgable.

analysis.name: Peter Doerschuk
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: absolute worst!Made ECE4670 a nightmare! Has no idea what's going on in labs,didn't cover the materials required for labs in lectures; HWs,labs left ungraded(has been 60 days), converted in-class prelim to take-home exam DURING the last 5 min of the exam! 9 min late to class on average, unresponsive on email/Piazza,Messed up commcloud several times

Professor Doerschuk is extraordinarily enthusiastic and a nice guy. That being said, he has made an art form of disorganization. If you like things being on time and proceeding in a logical fashion, it will drive you insane.
