Tests: go to his review sessions if at all possible or talk to someone who was there; makes studying easier and more focused

This is definitely my worst teacher ever at Cornell. Makes such an easy course unnecessarily hard, assumes a lot when he explains, doesn't have a syllabus, jumps back and forth in the textbook, gives a lot of exam matching questions not related to the lecture and I can continue ranting on and on. But take signals with this man at your ow risk.
I really enjoyed his lectures and would show up even though attendance was not required. He was sometimes funny and the content was not too difficult.

Professor Doerschuk is a great guy--kind, funny, and approachable. However, his lectures were hard to follow and unorganized. I hardly ever looked back at my notes from lecture since they just weren't helpful when studying/doing homework and relied heavily on the textbook and youtube videos. Office hours are a must.

analysis.name: Douglas McBride
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dr. McBride is a very caring and kind professor, he makes sure to make accommodations and wants every student to succeed. I took him for an FWS and I couldn't recommend enough. The readings are historical and very interesting. If you're a freshman looking for an FWS take this one, its graded on completion; if you do the work you'll get an A.

Though I meant to write this review at the end of the class, I am writing now because the most recent one is incredibly wrong.
There have been no bad reviews since February of this year.
This professor is so kind and their grading system allows for so much flexibility, as long as you put in an ounce of daily work his grading policy GUARANTEES an A.

Dr. McBride clearly loves what he does and loves to teach what he is passionate about. He is incredibly supportive and caring for his students and if a student does the reasonable amount of work he assigns on time, he/she will receive an A.

Professor McBride is a very reasonable professor. He can be confusing at times, especially if you go to him for outside help on essays, but he tries very hard to get to know you as a person and build a healthy student teacher relationship. I would not take Love and Death in Vienna again solely because I did not like the material much.
I learned a lot from him including about how to spell P-O-N-C-E and he demonstrated it to us. Thanks!

analysis.name: Andrew Hill
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 16 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Every office hour consisted of me asking him for feedback and him saying "But what do *you* think should be changed?" while providing nothing from his side. EXTREMELY HARSH GRADER. In a required FWS class, only 2-3 people got 90+, even though the assignment topics were really hard. I communicated to him that English is my second language, no effect

Dr. Andrew Hill is easily the best professor I had at Cornell. Intelligent and well-spoken, even among his Ivy-League peer, yet lenient, caring, and clear. While he doesn't do much to make lectures exciting, the brilliant things he has to say about the material are enough to keep anyone sincerely interested in the subject matter thoroughly engaged.

At first I did not like his class at all. There were reading responses due before every class, and the texts he gives are quite challenging. The readings were a stepping stone to the final curatorial project Overall, Dr. Hill is very approachable and VERY lenient with deadlines. His grading criteria could be more clear. Helped me improve my writing

Most professors tell you how to improve essays. Not prof. Hill. Will instead ask what you think of your essay. Seeing a fortune teller about your essay may have the same results. Take a different class.

Genuinely one of the most inspiring and fascinating minds you can find. I had Dr. Hill for my FWS, and was so motivated by his incredibly designed class format and assignments that I took his ENGL 2690 course. Any of his courses are essential if you are looking to evolve as a writer, artist, historian, or scholar. I can't recommend enough!

analysis.name: Andrew Whitmore
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 18 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor Whitmore's class structure is odd, but pretty easy. 1740 is a super useful course and I genuinely learned a lot from it this semester. That being said, he doesn't really know how to answer questions. He doesn't really teach in class, only from video lectures, so I spent a lot of time working on materials outside of class. TAs are helpful.

Honeslty one of my favourite professors. He doesnt waste time and is a great teacher. He teaches exactly what you need to know for exams. Before the prelims and final, we have one full class for review where he will work through a similiar practice paper to the exam and makes the assignment as similar to exam as possible. very good guy. Lots of HW

Whitmore is pretty chill and straight to the point. Some content might be difficult but the TA's and the professor himself are very helpful. There's a fair amount of work but it never gets too difficult.

I have taken two of his classes now and can say he is a really good professor. He is not as approachable for small talk as other professors but he does a really good job at teaching. He goes through content and explains everything in the best way possible. Everything is very easy to understand and his slides are also very concise.
Really easy class, but significant amounts of work, and the TAs grade strictly but fairly. The professor is easily accessible through emails, office hours, or in person.

analysis.name: Andrew Moisey
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: My favorite course I have taken. Highly interdisciplinary-- covers many potentially weighty theoretical and philosophical topics. The majority of the grade is a 15 page research paper, which he insists on being an intervention -- asking and answering a question that begs both. He is hilarious, and he wants you to care about the class. Approachable.

There are a few pop quizzes and a lot of reading, but the workload is manageable if you focus on key ideas. Professor Moisey is extremely funny and makes every lecture engaging with great jokes. I would recommend this course to anyone, even just for fun. He is super patient, answers questions clearly, and really encourages interest in learning.

Moisey is an incredible professor. I'm taking his class as a senior and wish I knew of him sooner. His lectures not only make me laugh out loud, but the way he roots the content in the broader scope of life makes every lecture incredible interesting and at times deeply moving. If you haven't had a class with him, you're missing out!

Professor Moisey is an amazing lecturer, this is my favorite class at Cornell. He's genuinely interested in the material and explores it alongside you, plus is insanely entertaining which makes it easy to pay attention. The reading is a lot, but get the big ideas (skim+) and you'll be fine. Start the final paper early, and get your TA to proofread!

Great lecturer that keeps you engaged and makes fun jokes. Took this class just for fun and out of interest, and I was able to learn so much more about photography. Exams are not hard and the TAs are super helpful.

analysis.name: Drew Margolin
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 13 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Highly recommend this professor! Really enjoyed learning content in this class. Not a lot of assignments and lectures were interesting. Had many breakouts in class, so it was very engaging. Weekly discussions provided a helpful overview of what was learned in class that week. Extra credit offered through sona and final was very easy.

very very good lecturer, lots of interaction with students. class is feirly easy, but he keeps it interesting by relating everything to real world examples or concepts. the lectures are the key, and he does a very good job of explaining everything

Pretty good professor! He is a nice guy and each time i went to office hour he really inspired me a lot. He truly wants all students to learn sth from his class. Comm1101 is chill and graded totally depends on ta.

Engaging professor, does a good job explaining things. Tests aren't hard if you put in the effort, mostly just understanding vocabulary and coming up with your own examples. Writing assignments are easy A's if you clearly follow the guidelines. 1 video project, again easy A if you put in the effort. EC offered thru SONA.

After coming to the first day of class, it was instantly clear that Prof Margolin was a bad professor. He's outdated and out of touch (he said that technology was not allowed in the classroom at all and that we had take handwritten notes). You could also sense was egotism... he talks as if what he's teaching is very important even though it's not.

analysis.name: T. Michael Duncan
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 64 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: In a perfect situation Duncan would be a great teacher. He interacts with the class, seems friendly and is very intelligent.  But the reality is, he takes things personally, a very bad characteristic for someone in a position to educate.  Once he aligns himself against you, consider avoiding him at all costs and not depending on him for help.

This is a very rigorous introduction to chemical engineering. It is not an easy class, but you will learn a lot if you put your earnest effort to it. A lot of skills are honed in this class. For instance, interpreting graphs, guessimating, etc. It is a critical thinking class, and this is how the professor wanted it to be.

Fantastic course teacher.  Poor advisor.  Condescending if you are not confident or if you do not agree with him.  This is college; kids are supposed to find themselves, not be discouraged. His favorites get more confident while the shy ones get more shy.  This is an unfair and unstable method to students.

He knows his stuff really well and cares that you learn but the class is insanely tedious. The workload is very heavy, feels like a 7 credit class with the group homework, random assignments, 2 hour calculation sessions, 3 prelims, and projects sprinkled in between. Plan wisely and make an effort to ask him questions and engage with the class.

This class was a nightmare. Insanely long homeworks with barely any course staff that could help. Exams were graded SO HARSHLY with almost close to 0 partial credit. Always rude whenever you spoke to him and lectures were always useless. Not sure what's gotten into him this semester.

analysis.name: Mathieu Dumouchel
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 8 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: While lectures were interesting when he talked about the new tariffs, he basically just read off the slides. Easy problem sets, but hard prelim multiple choice Qs. His grading was unclear (besides the median and the “top 45% get an A” you don't know anything). Definitely not “bad”, just gave less effort than other Econ profs and uninspiring.

As long as you study and understand lectures you can pass. he's generous with grading problem sets and has a good amount of office hours to help with any questions. The only bad thing is he doesn't post lecture slides after or a recording, so you have to go to class.

I don't like macro but this class was much better than expected. He spent some time explaining the new tariffs, which was interesting. Tests can be hard so make sure that you understand the slides well.
His accent made it difficult for me to take away much from his lectures, which consisted of reading off of slides. Not a very hard class conceptually, but be sure to study a lot outside of class!

Took his Intermediate Macro class this semester and I'm glad I did. Even over Zoom, he gives good lectures and his passion for the material comes through quite well. Grading is based entirely on problem sets and exams. The problem sets are simple enough but be sure to prepare well for the exams as they will probably be harder than you expect.

analysis.name: Alexandra Dufresne
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Dufresne is such a genuine professor who truly cares. There were no exams, 4 homeworks, and a final project. The class can get a little repetitive and boring because there are no lecture slides, and it is just her talking. She does tend to call on the same group of people over and over again (who are usually students in her advocacy clinic.

Professor Dufresne is amazing!! In my opinion, she gives the best lectures. If you're considering law school as a career choice, I'd highly recommend taking one of her classes. Her classes are very participation heavy, so you get out of it what you put into it.
Very interesting class and Professor. Good class to take if interested in law school. Pick 4 homeworks out of 16, final project, and participation. Light grader.

Professor Dufresne has the amazing ability to take complex and difficult subjects like immigration and make them not only enjoyable to learn about but easy to digest. She goes out of her way to make sure both students who have a law background and those who do not are able to get the information they need.

analysis.name: Duanyi Yang
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Loved taking Professor Yang's Labor Relations class. As long as you go to class and pay attention you will do well. She and the TA want you to succeed, and the grading is pretty fair. She's an inspiring prof and had some guest lecturers in class.

She's very nice and wholesome, and the course content is digestible and easily understood. Attendance counts, but exams are straightforward and generally graded leniently. Duanyi is super genuine and nice and helpful.

I would definitely recommend taking labor relations with Professor Yang!  She cares about students and gives good lectures.  The class was very straightforward with reasonable expectations.  Participation is important but there are many ways to earn credit.  She also provides study guides for all exams.  The group projects are not too difficult.

Prof Yang is wonderful and I would recommend her to any student looking to take Labor Relations. The Collective Bargaining Simulation is one of the most fun group activities I have ever participated in. The class is super manageable and you learn a whole lot. Great class, even better professor!

Yang is great! I am currently in her class for Labor Relations and thoroughly enjoy it. You can tell that she wants her students to succeed and is passionate about the course. Will happily answer any questions you have about the material too. Recommend taking Labor Relations with her!

analysis.name: Durba Ghosh
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Professor Ghosh is a wonder of a human being who is so considerate in the way she leads class discussions. Even if you don't do the readings, you will be able to contribute in class. The grading system is extremely generous if you put any effort into your essays. It is hard to get less than a B- if you are present and do the assignments.

Gives complete control of grades to TA probably with instructions to grade tough because you usually do not have the right to question. Material is interesting, but you are encouraged to follow her view on the subject matters even though her class is mentioned as open to debate. Would not recommend taking it unless absolutely necessary.

Probably my best professor at cornell.  Her lectures were very organized and helped to explain the readings.  She is very helpful and open to questions (no matter how basic).  She grades very fairly and gives good comments.  I highly recommend Hist275 (it also fulfills a LOT of the A&S requirements).

Really great history class that pushes you to think of the big picture rather than just regurgitating events. Readings were all extremely interesting. A bit of a tough grader on the essays but she values improvements and gives a grade based off performance not numbers. Great class overall

Class consisted of 2 5-page essays, a midterm examination, and a final examination. Both examinations were in-class and a choice of essay topics. Reading assignments were moderate and interesting. The class is excellently organized and Prof. Ghosh is very articulate and interesting.

analysis.name: Dustin Paul
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 6 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Hard to contact him should you find yourself needing to, but his hockey class is so much fun. He's such a genuinely nice guy, and I actually looked forward to this even though it was early. He's reasonable and takes everyone where they're at (experience-wise). 100% should take this class, Dusty is awesome.

Great guy, actually so funny and understanding (really put up with a lot from some students). All you really need to do is show up to class and skate. I did get better at ice hockey even if I'm still bad. Highly recommend!
Great guy, way more understanding than he had to be, considering certain people in the class could not ice skate whatsoever. I got much better at ice skating, and had a very fun time.

Awesome professor. Really taught me how to become a better hockey player. Taught the class how to make Hockey Stops, and just simple maneuvers to better facilitate your flow of the game. But, you have to know how to skate on ice. Although the class is an intro, but most of the students are already good at hockey.

He's really chill and nice. If you're late, he won't be mad as long as you jump in to participate. As long as you don't go over your max limit of skips, you're cool. He will answer any questions you have about working out and building muscle.

analysis.name: Yue Du
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment:
Note: the course I took is actually HIST 2931- Making an Empire in China. 
Prof. Du is very knowledgeable, caring, and hard-working. Her lectures are information-dense but well-structured. No need to do all readings, but read papers. Go to her office hours and you'll get great book recommendations, advice on writing, and even life lessons.

If you attend all lectures you get half a grade up. I like her passion (although her accent might be a bit difficult to understand at first). Read the syllabus carefully. It's ok to not do the weekly readings. She is very accessible outside class and it's willing to help.
Super caring professor, grade based on weekly reflections and research paper at the end. Every class is a seminar, so come to class prepared and youll be fine.

analysis.name: Brooke Duffy
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Turned really interesting topics into dry discussions over common sense. Both lectures and overall class schedule are very disorganized. Would avoid class like this if you don't want to waste your time and tuition.

Professor Duffy is a true gem. It looks like a lot of work, but she's flexible and caring. She recognizes when her students are struggling with school and life in general. She's also a great lecturer and gives many opportunities to earn points through different things (essays, tests, discussions).

BEST communication professor. She is so genuine and caring. Really values students' opinions of the materials and course. Her classes are very lecture-heavy and likes to throw in a few readings that she likes to discuss in class. She's very approachable! Easy course with basic syllabus structure - highly recommend all of her courses

Prof. Duffy is one of the most incredible professors at Cornell. She genuinely cares about her student, makes class expectations clear and rather reasonable. The pace of this class was a bit slow, also lectures were heavily summaries of assigned readings making them kind of redundant. But great prof and easy course.

analysis.name: Dwayne Bershaw
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Beers with Dwayne is such a fun class! He clearly loves teaching about beer and you can learn a lot if you pay attention during lecture.

He's really chill and makes the class fun. Don't buy the textbook, you'll literally never need it. There's just a couple of assignments besides the midterm and final, and he grades really easy, so it's a grade GPA booster.
One of my favorite professors ever
Exams (multiple choice and online) were a bit challenging but there is a lot of extra credit

analysis.name: Dwight Bowman
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 22 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Too much information on slides. Slides are poorly organized with no structure to them. Talks too quickly, glosses over topics such that lectures becomes confusing. Exams are difficult and require LOTS of memorization of detail. Final is comprehensive, ridiculous given how many parasites are covered.

Not an easy class. He is unclear and extremely vague on his tests. Very specific and a too much info needed. Almost impossible to learn it all unless you take other easy classes (which I did not). Easy based on no work but the tests are horrible
Overall, really easy class but requires time to rote memorize. Go to class. Memorize slides cold. Get the extra credit points. Get an A+ and pat yourself on the back.

Took BIOMI 3310. He's a great lecturer and really cares about his students, but the course takes a ton of studying for only 2 credits. Exams are 100% of your grade and require you to memorize an insane amount of detail from the lecture slides. He does give bonus points though. Only take this course if you are really passionate about the material.

Engaging and entertaining lecturer, interesting subject material, fair tests. The material would probably be useful for anyone planning to be involved with tropical medicine in any way, though since the best way to do well on the tests is to memorize everything, the amount of information you'll actually retain beyond the course is questionable.

analysis.name: Dylan Bugden
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Better communication skills needed and less words on the lecture slides, so information won't feel overwhelming when trying to take notes in a 50 min class. This class is fairly confusing when it comes to completing assignments. Overall, this class needs better structure and order to provide a more effective learning environment for students.

You have to be a fast note taker b/c tons of information on slides that are fine print. No electronics allowed in class! Be on time to every lecture! Try to ask specific or detailed questions as much as you can b/c vague ones don't get accurate answers. Go to office hours frequently to understand structure of assignments as much as possible.

One of the worst classes and professors Ive ever had in my life. I honestly dont think he is In teaching to actually teach. The entire class was just pushing out as many assignment as possible. And then when I got something wrong, he would not teach and let me know why, he just said this is not correct, like gee thanks TEACHER

analysis.name: Corey Ryan Earle
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Story time with Corey was one of my favorite experiences at Cornell -- definitely one to take in your senior year then go to Luna's Monday night trivia afterward. Really helped me develop an appreciation and gratitude for my alma mater.
amazing class! corey gives great lectures and multiple fun opportunities for extra credit, definitely recommend

Literally the easiest A+ you could possibly get at Cornell. Honestly, I felt a little bad throughout the course because since it was so beyond easy, nobody took the course seriously. A few extra mandatory assignments could make the course better in my opinion to keep students engaged. Lectures and professor were great otherwise.

Was an amazing professor. Took this class to lighten up my science heavy semester and boy am I glad that I did. His lectures are interesting and engaging and he gives so much opportunity for extra credit. I highly recommend this class to anyone and everyone.
Fantastic instructor. Never knew there was so much behind the history of the University!

analysis.name: Earl Kirkland
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Class is difficult and six hours of lab a week is brutal, but far and away best Professor I've had at Cornell. Knows the entire curriculum by heart, great person with a good sense of humor, and extremely helpful. Lectures can be a bit boring, but office hours and lab are incredibly useful. The most useful class you can take for research.

Prof. Kirkland is quiet which can make staying awake hard but he's a great teacher with a good sense of humor. He's always around during labs and is friendly/non-judgemental if you need extra help. The labs for this class are tedious and grindy, though, so make sure you don't fall behind. Weekly homeworks are short.

analysis.name: Eanna Flanagan
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Prof. Flanagan taught the advanced undergrad quantum class. 4443 is a mathematically sophisticated and theoretical course. He's a caring professor and 4443 is awesome. He covers topics in graduate quantum including density matrix, tensor products, path integrals and more . Lectures are important because the content isn't encapsulated by one book.

Incredibly smart and kind professor. Always has a keen eye for making even the most difficult or confusing concepts understandable; he never treats any question as trivial and always takes time to give a complete answer

Flanagan turned PHYS4443 into basically GradQuantum1, covering about 80% of Sakurai through his very fast paced and extremely mathematically sophisticated lectures combined with challenging problem sets. Extremely caring professor with a gargantuan brain. Amazingly insightful and interesting class, but be mentally prepared for a baptism by fire.

Flanagan did two classes worth of material, and I am glad that he did: I learned a lot. His arguments are mathematical, offering a different perspective at problems compared to the "physical intuition method". Homeworks were the focus of class (50% of grade); they were tough, but you take a lot out of them.

analysis.name: David Easley
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 43 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: Networks is an easy class. Maybe it was made easy by Easley and Kleinberg's ability to teach, but truthfully I don't think teaching this material would be challenging for anyone. I would love to see what Easley could do in an intense economics course. He seems very smart and very personable.

i cant believe people thought this course was easy. midterms/finals are so advanced compared to psets and practice exams it was like whiplash. every time i felt like i understood something it got flipped upside down the next chapter or on the exam. i was an office hours warrior and barely passed. genuinely saw someone cry during the final today too

I didn't really attend a lot of the lectures (cause they were optional) but I thought I'd nevertheless leave a review for the class. The class content was extremely interesting and insightful. Just download the textbook "Networks, Crowds and Markets" and read through some parts. You'll know whether you wanna take this class.

Difficulty of this class made a 180 compared to last year. Practice problems before the two exams were useless as not even 1/3 of the problems made it onto the exam. If you're looking for easy liberal studies and have a rough workload definitely think again. You have to read the textbook and camp office hours, not what I expected for 3 credits.

Class: Networks. Very organized class. Most people wouldn't show up. It was pretty easy overall. Professor Easley did a great job at getting the content across. The average final grade was an A I think.

analysis.name: Christopher Earls
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: I LOVED THIS CLASS!!! Made me really excited about Civil Engineering. The class was easy and interesting. Earls is really passionate about the material. Very helpful in office hours. No textbook, a few short homeworks, 1 paper, and a group project in the place of a final. The project was fun and a great way to apply the material. My favorite class.

The professor is AMAZING!!There is no textbook so pay attention to the lecture. However, the lecture is so long(over an hour) and there is no light because of the ppt, so you can fall asleep easily even though the lecture is interesting. There're no tests, only easy homework with only 2 or 3 short questions, include one group project and one paper.

Prof Earls did a good job with steel design. Lectures were very clear and well organized. Earls was always willing to help students. Homeworks really taught me about steel design. Tests were similar to homeworks, not too difficult. I recommend this course for a CEE design requirement. PS he's hot!

Made the class fun and applicable. While he didn't teach too much into the specifics of statics, mechanics, etc, the holistic approach was beneficial to the freshman class of engineers. The final project was superb and everyone had a good time with it

Lectures were pretty interesting compared to some other classes, but lasted a long time. The homeworks are easy if you go to office hours. I loved this class because the final project was so much fun, and there's no final.

analysis.name: Emily Eaton
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: FWS: Guts, Ghosts, & Glory was a pretty good writing seminar to take.  We had one or two less essays than most others.  Professor Eaton has her positives and negatives.  While she can provide good feedback and isn't a harsh grader, she tends to think that she's the only one that's right and that writing should only be in her particular style.
Very kind and helpful professor, happy to meet outside of class, but very easy to understand in class.

analysis.name: Brian Earle
analysis.verdict: Highly rated with a manageable workload, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 23 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Wow, Brian was probably the best professor I have ever had at Cornell. He is smart, funny, caring, and basically I could go on and on. The class is not that difficult, just make sure you read the book before the two tests, other than that, enjoy yourself and the lectures! Speeches are a breeze as well. Great job Brian, we love you!
Brian saved my bacon during my years at CU. A great teacher and better advisor and excellent friend. Far too few Brian Earless in higher ed. Highly recommended!
Prof. Earle cares about his students and he obviously wants them to be able to take the skills and knowledge he imparts on life-long journeys.  He's a darling!

I just thought that this would be a continuation of COMM 201, but it's so much better than that.  His class is about REAL-WORLD stuff that I will never forget (i.e.: interviewing skills)  Really useful class and you enjoy talking to him just try not to mention that you like the Yankees.
Amazing professor - very useful course due to its relevance to real world necessities.

analysis.name: Edward Swartz
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 9 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very good professor who made content very interesting. Class got somewhat difficult after the 1st exam, but it is still a very good class to take if you are interested in higher-level math. Professor really pushes you to think about the concepts more deeply as opposed to simply applying algorithms to solve questions.
Ed Swartz is the best professor I have ever had. He was a very good lecturer, the lectures were challenging and thoughtful, although a bit scattered, they were always very engaging and interesting.
Swartz is a strong lecturer. He teaches math the right way. Would take a Math class taught by him any day of the week.
THE GOAT SWARTZ, I LOVE HIS MOANS

Tests are 80% of grade (each prelim is 25%, final is 30%). Brilliant, funny guy, but writes small/messy on the board and talks quietly, so sometimes difficult to follow. Pretty boring too, hard to focus after 40 min. Exams are pretty difficult but doable with office hours and hw. TA's are really good and know what they're talking about.

analysis.name: Ed Baptist
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 20 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Mean guy. Unreasonable. Probably unhappy with his life -- I've heard stories about him only justified by extreme unhappiness with his own life. After taking his class, he changed my entire perception of academia and confirmed the suspicion "those who cannot do, teach." Fears face-to-face confrontations.

Baptist is a nice guy and knows a ton but only an OK lecturer. Kind of rambles on and gets off topic easily. Lectures have nothing to do with what the tests are on. Assigned readings are very specific and pretty interesting. Discussion sections were pretty boring. Class was not as interesting as I thought it would be.

Insane amounts of reading material. No consistency in lectures, and just when it seems he starts talking about something actually important to the core of the class he tangents off on something again losing the attention of his students. He seems like a nice guy who is passionate about what he does, but he is not meant to teach.

Worst class I've taken at Cornell. Horrible lectures. Boring, disorganized, filled with irrelevant information. Readings were lengthy but interesting. Tests are basically unrelated to much of the readings and lecture. Terrible class

Readings were incredibly dense and lengthy. Lectures were genuinely unrelated to the readings. Baptist advertised his own merchandise and book repeatedly. Grading varied wildly across TAs. Grossly incompetent lecturer.

analysis.name: Edward McLaughlin
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 37 reviews, fairly reliable

review_highlights.comment: professor was completley inaccesible and unhelpful. In response to a question regarding the final exam he replied in an email "see class notes where this was discussed four weeks in a row" He could easily have answered my question. Tests are not too difficult but they dont test your knowledge of the subject. Steer clear if its not required

This class is a MUST TAKE if u are interested in taking a business course at CU. The lectures are not only clear and easy-to-digest,they are also VERY FUN and INTERESTING!Yes,the exams are DIFFICULT as they require minute details from the textbook+lectures+guest lectures but if u take time to read the text book and attend lectures,all will be fine!

Ed McL is one of the few professors at Cornell who is active in research and talented at teaching.  His tests are tricky and require you to know the material cold, but if you focus in class, read the book, and do the extra credit, you're golden.  Lectures presented lots of information but were fun, thanks to inclusion of funny ads and movie scenes.

STUDY FOR THE EXAMS.  I completely underestimated the difficulty of the prelims, but thankfully he let us drop a prelim so I still did really well (after some serious studying). Read the textbook, take really good notes, and pay attention to guest lectures and you'll do well.  Lectures are fun (movie clips and ads), but the book is boring as hell

His lectures are decent and he does explain things in a easy-to-digest fashion. The tests, however, may be unexpected difficult and require you to recall tiny bits of useless information from text/guest lectures. If you want the bonus credits, make your response as cheesy as possible. Just imagine you are trying to entertain your grandfather.

analysis.name: Ronald Ehrenberg
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Ehrenberg is awesome. You have to go well out of your way, more so than with other profs, to get him to notice you... but if you do it's well worth it. He's opinionated, and it's awesome, especially since ILR seems to only hire bleak, boring people who have nothing worth saying anymore. Ehrenberg is the best thing to happen to ILR

i had a great time and learned a lot. a very lighthearted but in-depth approach. was nervous about taking a course based on the prof's own book (those can be bad, when the prof just basically reads verbatim -- why go to class!??), but this was well worth it. had a good time with my end-of-term project too.

Ehrenberg cares SO MUCH about his students.  He is a very personable, endearing professor who has a passion for what he teaches.  His Econ of the University class is very interesting, great class, and good if you don't want too much work.

Professor Ehrenberg was the best professor I have had at Cornell. I am so thankful that I took his class and wish I could take it again before I graduate! He is generally an amazing professor and I recommend that all students have the opportunity to take his class before graduating. He genuinely cares about his students. You're the best RGE!
Very interesting guy, good at telling stories, funny and nice but kind of full of himself (in an endearing way though...).  Good class.

analysis.name: Matti Eklund
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The course turned out to be easy for me, but I can see where it could cause trouble for some people. Best avoid this one if you aren't comfortable with math and philosophy. Course reading was relatively unimportant, lectures were everything. Tests were pretty easy, I got a 100 on a prelim. Oh, and Eklund is great--smart, nice, and approachable.

To be fair, I was really excited for this class before I took it. You just talk about paradoxes the entire course. It's great. Just show up to lecture, minimal readings that are basically optional. Very helpful lecture notes distributed in class. Class makes you think. Explains things clearly and thoroughly. Grading was kind of tough though.

Grades hard, provides lecture notes but has no talent in teaching, other than reading off the notes. Awkward silence for 75 minutes is the essence of the class. SO many students opt to not show up even if attendance matters bc of that. His thick accent and obvious rambling in English is often hard to listen to...

Prof. Eklund is a nice guy, and pretty knowledgeable, but the class wasn't that great or interesting.  However, he has a great Swiss (I believe) accent.  And he is very approachable.  I don't recommend the class though.  The grading really depends on the TA.

analysis.name: Mark Eisner
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Simply politically incorrect, annoying in his comments to the last. Very clever but he jumps to conclussions very fast. Impatient and unclear. Self-centered, has personal and prejudice bias.
GET ME OUT OF HERE! is what I'm thinking everytime I'm in this class and he asks a question that no one cares about to the guest lecturer.
loud and ignant...his constant chiming in the middle of class is very annoying
Actuallt cares about students, makes entertaining comments

analysis.name: Tom Eisner
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 15 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: This guy is a living legend.  He has over 500 published papers, and has been the pioneer for the field of chemical ecology.  His story-telling is fantastic, and all of the stuff he talks about is just so interesting.  One of my favorite professors that I have ever had, I stand in awe of his accomplishments as a researcher and a teacher.

FANTASTIC! Incredible storyteller, beautiful speaker; brought a sense of wonder, excitement, and commitment to nature to every lecture. Also, he's hilarious! I really loved this class and encourage everyone to take it; no grades, just for its own sake. you will relax, learn a lot, and enjoy.

Every undergrad's dream biology professor. I saw someone wearing a handmade "I Want to Be Tom Eisner" t-shirt around campus. That's how cool he is. There's like five different professors who teach this course and he's definitely the highlight.

I heart Tom Eisner.  Catch him while you can.  (He's really old.) I took this class the first semester it was offered and there was no work, just a guaranteed S.  It's like getting credit for watching the discovery channel or national geographic.

SO OLD AND SO GOOD. Sometimes hard to understand because he moves around a lot and speaks softly, but if you pay attention to what he says, your mind will be blown. One of the most fascinating lecturers with the best slides. Funny, too.

analysis.name: Elisabeth Meyer
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 17 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Personally One of the worst teachers I've ever had...
Really Crazy.  Makes decisions based on her mood.  She gossips about students and teachers to students.
Manipulative, disrespectful, controlling, demanding, and downright abusive. She's regularly reported but protected by her tenure. Beware

I don't think I have ever had a worse experience in my life. She constantly interrupts her co-teacher and other students while they talk, and she would talk non-stop for three hours about unrelated matter. She makes insensitive, rude and offensive comments, and she pries into your personal life. She expects you accommodate her mood and schedule.

Elisabeth is definitely a very passionate, worldly, and insightful professor. I think the conflict with students and her is her occasional mood-swings and judgmental spurts. She definitely chooses favorites. Her class is demanding, you learn a lot, she's tough, but students need that sometimes, especially students in Art School.

analysis.name: J. Ellen Gainor
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 10 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: While occasionally a lot of work (having to watch a movie along with reading the play for the next class), I found this class to be worthwhile. The grade depends on participation and two essays, and discussions were always interesting. Many Pulitzer-winning plays are on the curriculum, and thought was clearly put into structuring the curriculum.

I honestly do not understand why this professor has lower reviews. The class has alot of readings & I haven't done half of them and still am able to participate. She only really cares about participation and is so sweet. The work looks overwhelming but it is easy and only 2 papers total. I only knew one of the plays and still found it interesting
Random grading--will give a low grade on the final if she simply does not like its tone. An easy class, but don't try engaging with critical theory on the assignments as levity is always preferred.
don't take ENGL3370 if ur not an intense theatre kid. teacher can be straight up rude, you have hours and hours of homework for watching plays each week, and is just not an enjoyable class whatsoever

Not good, strict grader, quite rude actually. Has an lively exterior that masks this. Also drones on and on for things that could have been compactly said in a few words. Does not facilitate interesting discussion. Made what would have been an interesting class boring.

analysis.name: Parfait Eloundou-Enyegue
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 25 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Honestly was questioning the integrity of the university the entire time sitting in this class. He used AI Slop images every lecture, sometimes funny. No one learned anything useful in lectures but the sections with the TAs are nice though!

An extremely intelligent man, but not a very good professor for introductory classes. Very disorganized but still managed to teach so that material stuck the first time hearing it. Attend discussionit helps. Go to office hours or email for help on essays and group projects.

Given the other professors who were teaching this course (who are supposedly terrible), I thought Professor Eloundou was fantastic. He definitely has flaws (e.g. dry lectures), but I don't really understand the bad ratings. At least for this introductory course, he made the concepts really easy and was especially lenient with tests + extra credit

Nice guy, like everyone else said, but USELESS professor. He can't stick to the syllabus, he doesn't give students enough time to do assignments (he sprang them on us all semester) and you don't learn ANYTHING in class. Attendance is mandatory but it doesn't help. Also, don't waste $$ on the textbook.

This class is way too much for 3 credits, from 30 minute student presentations to very vague and unclear essay assignments with changing and unclear due dates. The lectures are unsimulating and I felt like I am not learning anything at all. This class needs more organization and clearness.

analysis.name: Elise Finielz
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Very kind professor, feedback was good but most actual learning was done outside of class so prepare to learn grammatical rules and stuff in your own time and practice in class!
She really cares for her students and wants them to succeed. I would recommend taking her class!
Elise is very nice. She is an amazing professor and she does so much to help students succeed. Definitely recommend!
Amazing prof. Make French enjoyable
Had her for my FWS, I really enjoyed the content and the creative writing essays gave us a bunch of freedom. As long as you submit decent essays, youll be fine.

analysis.name: Kevin Ellis
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: The course was actually great compared to CS 4700 when taught by Bart Selman. Yes, it used a class from Berkley, but that class is not too terrible. Would still recommend taking if you are cosidering.

I could not stand lectures. Just read off Berkely CS188 slides all day. Much better to just save your time and watch youtube videos of Berkeley CS188 lectures. The day before the midterm only 14 people showed up including myself (I actually counted).

Absolutely the worst class at Cornell. The prof just read Berkeley CS188 slides with no passion, showing zero care for the subject or us. It was all over the place—lectures, homework, exams didn't match up. Exams had stuff we never covered, with a median of 50%. Felt pointless studying so much, and feeling like I wasted my time.
This review is for CS 6172. Kevin is a really good lecturer and very caring. The class was chill and interesting. Definitely recommend taking it.

analysis.name: Jack Elliott
analysis.verdict: Good teaching but expect to put in the work, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 21 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Jack himself said that 2-3 students got an A last year. You'll stay up late and need to conform to his unorthodox grading style. It's doable, but you'll have to perform cartwheels and jump through hoops for that A. There's no canvas page; grades are put on the wall. My classmates and I still don't know exactly what we learned long after taking it.

This guy is cool as long as you are liked by him.  Do your work and maintain his interest in you and your work and he will treat you well.  He likes good music and is overall a pretty fair teacher.  If you are at Cornell and on the Interior Design track in Hum Ec, you will most likely have him a few times so keeping a good rep with him will be pretty important.

You will spend HOURS (anywhere from 4-15) doing each [weekly] project, esp when you get into the second half of the semester. The content has nothing to do with interior design as much as just general ideas of color/light/form/etc. It's a tough course but you do learn a lot & Jack is really good. The reading summaries are tedious/time-consuming tho

You might be intimidated by Jack at first but honestly he's awesome. He really gets to know his students and everything he does is for a reason. You will spend many, many hours in the studio, but it's worth it. I think i got a lot more out of this class than any other at cornell so far. So long as you commit, i definitely recommend this class =]

Jack has been a great professor! I've really enjoyed taking his class and even though it's a lot of work, I feel like I learnt a lot. You'll get to know your classmates really well if you work on projects in the studio so you should do that. Also, presentations take a lot of time so be prepared for that! Jack's feedback is very helpful though.

analysis.name: Em Setzer
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 5 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Em's class was super engaging, and also allowed me to grow as a writer. There was only 3 essays to be written the whole semester and Em would give you detailed feedback on each one. Along with that, you were allowed to do essay revisions to get a higher grade. There were also lots of extra credit opportunities! Take fws with her!!

GREAT FWS!!!!!!! Class material and essays (3) we had to do were engaging. All essays had good feedback provided. Very kind extention, revision, and attendence policies. Enjoyed going to this class and learning. 100% reccomend to anyone wanting to take an interesting FWS and grow as a writer without an overbearing workload.

Em provided helpful and encouraging feedback on my essays and was always reachable outside of class. Attending office hours helped me grow a lot as a writer, and lectures were always engaging. Such an amazing and enjoyable FWS!
Best FWS class ever. Clear grading, amazing feedback and fun classes.
EM THE GOAT. Peak FWS, take it with them if you can.

analysis.name: Emma Wang
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Really great professor. Her lectures were engaging and she was very accessibly outside of class. It's clear that she cares for her students and as long as you study and complete the practice questions, you'll do well in the class.

First time teaching the class and it showed 

Not competent, many mistakes were pointed in her lecture material multiple times in lecture/office hours 

Wrote an exam that averaged 61% in a class where historical averages were ~80%, blamed her students

Dramatic discrepancies in prelim grading, >30% of class received prelim regrades

Avoid.

she does not have a clear teaching style and lecture is very boring and unorganized. the homework is nothing like the exams. if you can avoid. she is very nice however the class could be improved significantly.

analysis.name: Emma Teitelman
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Don't really have a strong opinion on her. She is a nice enough lady who cares about history. I think it was her first time teaching it because she gave take home essay, in class essay, quiz, and test. Grading was not very clear and half the class never showed up. She also does have favoritism towards girls. Overall, nice lady not bad, not best.

Dr. Teitelman is a great professor! She genuinely wants to see her students do well. Not a very demanding teacher, and if you go to her office hours, she will tell you exactly what she wants to see from you. Would 10000% take again
Make sure to go to class and pay attention to the lecture. The class is manageable as long as you put in effort.

analysis.name: Emily Anderson
analysis.verdict: Mixed reviews. Student experiences vary quite a bit
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 3 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor was kind, passionate & grading criteria was clear. The course was discussion-based & work-heavy. The reading workload frequently exceeded the Knight Institute's 75-page guideline. Writing instruction was nonexistant, and earning an A required completing 8 additional essays. Take only if you are truly passionate or else it will be boring.
Professor is very helpful in office hours, is passionate about the subject she teaches, and provides good feedback. Get ready to read though.
10/10 professor, cares deeply about the content despite it being an fws, really cares about her students, makes time to give extra feedback on essays, would definitely take again

analysis.name: Emanuel Mora
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 7 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Far and away the best professor I have ever had. There is no comparison to make. Professor Mora is inspirational, knowledgeable, eloquent, and extremely caring and passionate about his field and his students. There is nothing more to say; he is the best there is.
I love Prof. Mora. The kindest and most enthusiastic professor I've ever had. The course is super engaging and not perplexing at all. He even has a YouTube account, go watch it!

Highly recommend this class!! Prof Mora is extremely engaging, passionate, caring, and knowledgeable. Probably the best professor I've ever had! The class included weekly quizzes and three assignments (a group project, a paper, and a solo assignment). I genuinely enjoyed this class and learned so much!
The best professor I've ever met. Interesting lecture and engaging class.

What an amazing prof! Prof Mora has done some amazing research and taught all around the world. BEN is not an easy subject for me, but Prof Mora ensured I could understand the material. The extra credit helps you prepare for the prelims/quizzes. Prof is a guest lecturer, but I STRONGLY recommend his class!

analysis.name: Emilia Illana Mahiques
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 11 reviews, decent sample but could shift with more data

review_highlights.comment: Learning any new language is difficult and I think Emi does as well as any other professor would. The course itself is just content heavy. Nothing too easy or too extremely difficult, grammar is learning on your own so prepare for that, but she will help with vocab in class.

I enjoyed this class as it gave me more experiences with Spanish and especially using it in global and real-world contexts. Prof. Mahiques was great and conducted the class well. There was no cold calling in the class as well and allowed people to participate when they wanted to. She also was understanding, considerate, and responsive to emails.

The class was an absolute disaster: incomprehensible notes, confusing homework, terrible teaching, struggling students, ridiculously hard tests, absurd participation, etc... Google became a better resource than her, 100%. It was an exhausting, disappointing, and frustrating experience that left everyone running out of her class. Fire her!

Overall the semester wasn't too bad but she gave 5 cumulative assessments that she marked as not counting for much but we didn't have many other grades. The final paper was ridiculous. The instructions were extremely unclear she didn't tell us the due date until a week before it was due.

I had a great experience with Emi. Everyone in the class loved her as well. She genuinely wants us to succeed and become better language speakers, even if that means cold calling and pushing us to our limits to improve our language understanding, it pays off! Take her class if you want to learn Spanish.

analysis.name: Emily Epperson
analysis.verdict: Well liked with reasonable difficulty, and ratings have been declining
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: Professor Epperson is a fantastic teacher who really understands how to teach a foreign language to students so that they really understand the concepts. Took both 1210 and 1220 with her and the classes are not particularly hard but they are a lot of fun. Oral exams, short writing prompts and quizzes but that can be expected for any language class.

FREN 1210*. Prof. Epperson is very effective at teaching a foreign language and I feel like I learned a lot in one semester. She is caring, accessible, and gives good feedback. Assignments are very manageable. Quizzes are easy as long as you actively participate/complete homework and do a little review.
Great teacher, kind and helpful

analysis.name: Emaad Manzoor
analysis.verdict: Below average reviews. Worth checking if this fits your learning style, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Only 4 reviews so far, take these numbers with a grain of salt

review_highlights.comment: One of the best professors at Cornell! He has deep expertise and is a great presenter. It's clear that he has designed this class very thoughtfully, with content that is highly practical and relevant. I enjoy his lectures and his slides are well created too. His industry experience also effectively bridges AI theory with real-world application

Emaad doesn't encourage much interaction or participation from students. Overall just tiresome to attend class and feel motivated to do well.

You will most likely sleep as it's just one hour and fifteen minutes of straight lecturing. Its unfortunate that this professor is even at Cornell. All focus is on the engineering aspect, none on marketing.
far better courses to take at cornell, don't waste your time with this course

analysis.name: Andrew Musser
analysis.verdict: Most students had a tough time. Look into alternatives if you can, but ratings have been improving recently
analysis.confidence_detail: Based on 930 reviews, this is a reliable picture

review_highlights.comment: Unfortunately, the lecture and course organization is non-existent. The material is complicated and muddied by the heinously bad teaching. Professor Musser is a potato when it comes to teaching general chemistry.

If you want to teach yourself chemistry, take CHEM 2090 with Professor Musser. Professor Musser is a very nice, smart individual... who should never teach an Intro to Chemistry course. His lectures leave students with less understanding of the topics & don't align with the quiz & 50 weekly homework problems. Don't take his class. It's not worth it.

Andrew Musser is too smart for his own good. Countless times throughout the semester he would spend entire lecture teaching to the board. Not only was the caliber of the lecturing atrocious but the majority of time spent teaching was spent on information irrelevant to the exam. I would not recommend taking this class if you have any other option.

Seems like a nice person, but is an absolutely awful professor. Way to smart to be teaching Gen Chem, and has no idea that his lectures are beyond the scope of the class. Goes to fast through super important topics and spends to much time on irrelevant info. If you take his class, read the textbook and listen in lecture, hints for tests

Honestly, he seemed like a decent guy, but… Exams were way harder than anything he covered (just irrational). He did not prepare us enough for the midterms (not enough resources & did not go in enough depth). Labs were inconsistently graded & expectations were not made clear at any point in the course. Only did OK cause I knew most of it beforehand
